"The targeted outcomes of the Tamil Nadu Irrigated Agriculture Modernization Project are to enhance productivity and climate resilience of irrigated agriculture, improve water management, and increase market opportunities for farmers and agro-entrepreneurs in selected sub-basin areas of Tamil Nadu. Additionally, the project aims to actively promote gender inclusion and women's participation in all key project interventions, ensuring women are fully engaged in Water User Associations (WUAs) and represented in Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and other alternative livelihood sources. The project is also expected to have significant positive nutritional impacts by promoting high-value pulses, millets, vegetables, fruits, and enhancing dairy and fisheries sectors, contributing to reductions in diabetes and increased consumption of essential nutrients. It is projected to achieve 5.7 million tons of CO2 equivalent mitigation over its life. To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve the following specific targets: 1) Provide new or improved irrigation and drainage services to an area of 160,000 Hectares. 2) Increase yields of agriculture crops to 4250.00 kg/ha for Rice, 6200.00 kg/ha for Maize, and 600.00 kg/ha for Pulses. 3) Increase yields of horticulture crops to 12.76 MT/Ha for Vegetables (Brinjal, Bhendi & Tomato) and 63.00 MT/Ha for Tissue Culture Banana. 4) Bring 75,000.00 Hectares under climate-resilient technologies (like System of Rice Intensification and resource-conserving technology). 5) Increase the area cultivated for non-paddy crops to 75,000.00 Hectares. 6) Achieve a 20.00% share of selected commodities sold through new marketing channels. 7) Reach a total of 500,000 project beneficiaries, including 225,000 female beneficiaries."

"The Program to Strengthen the Electricity Sector aims to enhance the sustainability of Bolivia's electricity sector through policy reforms and a reinforced regulatory framework. Its specific objectives include: ensuring a consistent macroeconomic context, strengthening the institutional framework for optimal planning and management across generation, transmission, and distribution subsectors, facilitating sustainable diversification of the electricity matrix using renewable and non-conventional renewable energy (RE/NCRE) sources including distributed generation, increasing reliable and sustainable electricity access in rural areas, and supporting energy efficiency (EE) improvements. The program targets the reduction of Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions by up to 3.3 million tCO2 from energy matrix diversification with RE/NCRE and up to 1.4 million tCO2 from EE measures in public lighting and the residential sector. It also expects to generate annual savings of up to US$100 million by 2023 due to lower electricity generation costs from RE/NCRE compared to natural gas, along with a total annual energy saving of 1,358 GWh/Year by 2023 through EE measures. Furthermore, it aims for a lower electricity expenditure for local public lighting entities, freeing up resources for investment in other sectors, and a greater surplus for residential consumers due to reduced electricity costs and subsidies from the government. The program also seeks to improve the institutional structure by separating roles in hydrocarbons and electricity, enhance sector development planning, and increase female participation in technical and leadership positions within ENDE. It also aims to increase the participation of RE and NCRE in the National Interconnected System's electricity generation matrix and increase national electricity coverage. Overall success would be achieved when these targets are met, specifically: the reduction of accumulated GHG emissions as indicated, the implementation of a specific institutional structure for the energy sector, the approval of a comprehensive sector development plan, an increased percentage of women in technical and leadership roles at ENDE, an increased percentage of RE and NCRE in the electricity matrix, the achievement of the projected annual energy savings from EE measures, and an increase in national electricity coverage percentage. Additionally, the approval and publication of the National EE Strategy via Supreme Decree, the establishment of minimum quality standards for efficient electricity use across residential, public, and transport sectors, and the creation of an EE support unit within the Ministry of Energies (MEN) are crucial for success."

"The activity targets include increasing average fisher monthly income to 400 Euro, boosting fish catch to 2,000 tonnes, and empowering 100 artisanal fishers to shift to off-lagoon fishing by 2024. It aims to train 550 fishers in modern FADs, facilitate 100 fishers in accessing financial support, and upgrade 5 fish landing stations with a new gutting facility. Furthermore, 200 fishers, including women and youth, are targeted to use new processing plants and be trained in EU norms for post-harvest. Success hinges on achieving these quantitative targets and fostering empowered, economically independent, and environmentally responsible artisanal fishers."

"The targeted outcomes of the EC Manta Public Services Improvement Project are to increase the quality and sustainability of public services for water, sanitation, and urban mobility in Manta, Ecuador. Specifically, this involves increasing the availability and efficiency of water supply and sewerage services, improving urban mobility and accessibility through enhancements to the street network and sidewalks, and strengthening the municipal government's capacity in planning and managing these services. The activity would be considered an overall success if the following targets are achieved by the end of the project: the Working Ratio of the Manta Water Utility (EPAM) decreases from a baseline of 88.00% to 60.00%; the continuity of water supply service increases from 14.00 hours per day to 24.00 hours per day; the percentage of roads in good and fair condition increases from 50.00% to 62.00%; the average daily cyclist traffic along Par Vial increases from 400.00 to 505.00; 2,318 new piped household water connections are established; 17,500 piped household water connections benefit from rehabilitation works; 2,500 new household sewer connections are constructed; 17,318 household sewer connections benefit from rehabilitation works; and a multi-year road maintenance strategy is adopted."

"The activity targets improved urban services and enhanced climate change resilience in Kampong Chhnang and Pursat municipalities by 2023, benefiting 100,000 residents. Key outcomes include 80% and 50% reduction in flood-affected households in Kampong Chhnang and Pursat respectively, 19,600 households served by improved SWM, 90% sanitation coverage, and strengthened institutional capacity for climate resilience. Overall success requires achieving these outcome targets by 2023 and completing all five outputs by 2022, including infrastructure, SWM, sanitation, climate change adaptation plans, USUs, and capacity building for project implementation and O&M."

"The activity targets increasing renewable energy generation capacity and enhancing financial/environmental sustainability of the Maldives' power sector. Key outcomes include deploying 36 MW of solar PV (11 MW land-based, 10 MW floating in Addu, 15 MW floating in Greater Malé), reducing annual diesel consumption (e.g., 4.3-15.6 million liters, 313 million over 20 years), and cutting net annual GHG emissions (e.g., 33,500 CO2 tons/year, 186,400 metric tons CO2e by 2040). It aims to mobilize US$45 million private capital, reduce the import bill by US$8 million annually, and cut fuel subsidies by US$2.6 million in 2021. Success requires meeting these capacity, consumption, and emission reduction targets, mobilizing private investment, demonstrating financial viability, and achieving broader energy security and pollution reduction goals."

"Targeted outcomes include reducing public exposure to contaminated land, managing/closing 284 Ha of contaminated land, reducing exposure risk for 6,237 people, benefiting 262,000 direct beneficiaries (50% female), and ensuring 80% of residents are informed about remediation. Success hinges on achieving these targets, making land clean/healthy for living/working, increasing land value, enhancing food safety for 300,000 people, training 200 individuals in site remediation, publicly disclosing environmental reports, and reducing/recycling 1,100,000 tonnes of waste."

"The Hunan Forest Restoration and Development Project targets increased species diversity (40 by 2018) and tree canopy cover (25% fully replanted, 48% partially, 50% naturally regenerated forests) to enhance resilience and environmental functions. It aims to reforest 58,900 ha, upgrade 3 nurseries producing 60.5M seedlings, establish 22 forest cooperatives, complete 5 research topics, and deliver substantial training (13,800 staff, 60,200 beneficiaries). Outcomes include improved ecosystem health, soil/water conservation, carbon sequestration, and stable, climate-tolerant forest ecosystems."

"{\"targeted_outcomes\": \"The activity targets include reducing road user costs and strengthening the capacity of the Roads Department (RD) and Ministry of Economy and Sustainable Development (MESD) for road network management and logistics services. Specific outcomes also encompass improving road quality, safety, and reducing congestion, travel times, transportation costs, urban pollution for road users, and decreasing CO2 emissions.\", \"success_criteria\": \"Overall success requires achieving key targets: travel time reduced to 6 minutes, car operating costs to $0.21/km, truck operating costs to $0.50/km, and asset value increased to GEL 4,880 million. Additionally, 8 km of roads constructed, 5 RD/ITS staff trained, 100% key ITS equipment installed, 80% road user satisfaction, a National Logistics Program adopted, a Public/Private Steering Committee operational, key logistics legislation drafted, logistics studies completed, and 8 MESD staff trained. Sustained resource availability and effective RD resource use are crucial, along with an EIRR of at least 15.3% (overall) and 12.3% (IBRD section).\"}"

"The activity targets improving road transport efficiency along the Northern Corridor, enhancing urban transport institutional capacity, and promoting private sector participation. Key outcomes include decongesting Nairobi, improving JKIA road access, facilitating trade, reducing traffic congestion, lowering transport costs/travel time, decreasing air pollution, and enhancing mobility for low-income groups. It also aims to improve urban transport governance, establish the NMTA and RTSA, foster private sector involvement in BRT/commuter lines, and strengthen oversight functions."

"The overall project development objective (PDO) is to improve Kampala Capital City Authority's (KCC) institutional efficiency through the Strategic Framework for Reforms (SFR II), with physical investments aimed at preserving assets, arresting deterioration, enabling Kampala to function as a capital city, and attracting investors. Targeted outcomes include refining/expanding the SFR, developing a long-term city vision, implementing a financial recovery plan, creating an organizational development strategy, improving transparency/accountability, and enhancing service delivery capacity."

"The targeted outcomes for the China Bengbu Integrated Environment Improvement Project include: protecting 95% of Bengbu from flooding by stormwater runoff (up from 60%); reducing pollution discharges to the Huai River to 18,000 Tonnes of COD/year (from 20,260); increasing the reliability of Bengbu's water supply to 90 days of emergency supply (from 30); and achieving break-even for wastewater and/or water services (a ratio of 1.00 for total revenue to total expenditures, up from 0.60). Additionally, intermediate outcomes include completing 5 flood control pumping stations, building/upgrading 100% of storm water networks, desilting 100% of drainage canal sediments, achieving 80% coverage of wastewater networks, collecting and treating 80% of wastewater, and increasing the water level of Tianhe Lake by 1.0m (from 0.0m). The activity would be considered an overall success if these specific targets are met by the planned end date, indicating improved effectiveness and sustainability of urban environmental services in Bengbu Municipality."

"The Private Sector Competitiveness activity targets removing business and investment constraints by simplifying business registration and construction permitting, improving financial services access, and fostering mining. Outcomes include increased private sector GDP/employment, better Doing Business ranking, and stronger financial regulation. Success entails: 69,000 new businesses registered, 432 construction permits granted in Dushanbe, $10M in collateral-backed lending, bank supervision improvement in at least 8 BCP principles, payment system improvement in at least 3 CPSIPS areas, and 25 new mining exploration licenses issued."

"The China Energy Efficiency Financing Project targets the improvement of energy efficiency in medium and large-scale Chinese industries by developing sustainable energy efficiency project lending programs in selected banks. Key outcomes include energy savings, reduced greenhouse gas and other pollutant emissions, and increased energy efficiency project lending by participating banks. Specifically, by Year 5 (YR5), the project aims to achieve a cumulative US$900 million in incremental energy conservation investments, an annual energy savings capacity of 2.07 million tons of coal equivalent, and an annual CO2 emissions reduction capacity of 5.05 million tons. Additionally, cumulative energy efficiency lending by participating financial intermediaries (PFIs) is targeted at US$400 million, leading to an annual avoided energy consumption of 1.58 million tons of coal equivalent and 3.84 million tons of CO2 emissions avoided from EE lending. The project also targets a demand for EE financing in the pipeline of US$150 million by YR5.Intermediate outcomes include the establishment and sustenance of EE lending business lines at PFIs, strengthening of government EE-financing programs, piloting of EE investment preparation procedures (1-2 pilot projects completed by YR4), and development of EE investment monitoring and evaluation procedures (final draft by YR4). Furthermore, the National Energy Conservation Center (NECC) is expected to be formed and staffed by YR3 with a finalized business plan and work program by YR2. A mid-term review will be conducted by YR3, with necessary actions taken to enhance results, and project targets and delivery schedules are to be met.To be considered an overall success, the activity must achieve or exceed these YR5 target values for the key project outcome indicators and successfully implement all intermediate outcomes, demonstrating that sustainable energy efficiency project lending programs are fully developed and operational within the selected banks, leading to the targeted reductions in energy consumption and CO2 emissions in Chinese industries. Success also implies that adjustments made based on earlier monitoring (YR1-YR3) effectively fed into a strategy for sustaining and scaling up EE lending by YR5."

"[\"The activity aims to improve living standards by providing equitable access to safe, sustainable water supply and sanitation (WSS) and improving hygiene practices, targeting 40,000 urban and 70,000 rural residents with improved water sources (including 24,500 female beneficiaries). Key outcomes include reduced water-related diseases, improved livelihoods, and increased service equity. Policy targets by 2030 include 70% rural/95% urban access to safe water, 70% rural/85% urban access to sanitation, and 100% access for institutions.\",\"Overall success hinges on: adopting a 5-year WaSH Sector Strategy, adopting 10 District WaSH Development Plans, establishing a functional WaSH MIS, and strengthening 25 Community-Based Organizations. Infrastructure targets include 2,000 new/rehabilitated community water points and 6,000 urban piped connections. Service improvements include increasing urban safe water supply by 1,000,000 m3/year, achieving 85% customer satisfaction, and reducing Water PNG's Non-Revenue Water to 28%. Additionally, 13,000 people must be trained in hygiene.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes: Improve living conditions, foster economic growth by expanding water supply (80% coverage by 2010, 24-hr, national standards, 130k m3/day production, 200k new connections) and wastewater management (105k m3/day treatment, 440km networks). Enhance institutional capacities. By 2010/2012: 4.2M people with improved water, 743k/811k with wastewater, 4.6% pop. growth, 57.6M/110.4M Rmb WSC net profit. Success requires: achieving these targets, utilities covering all expenses via full-cost pricing, strong management, capacity building, robust regulatory framework for private sector, and financially viable project components (water FRR>10%, wastewater FRR>5%)."

"The activity targets the total elimination of HCFC consumption and imports in Cuba by 2030, aiming for a 67.5% reduction by 2025 (5.49 ODP tonnes) and 100% elimination by 2030 (10.97 ODP tonnes of HCFC-22). Targeted outcomes include strengthening the legal framework, customs control, technical education, and the Recovery, Recycling, and Reuse (R&R) network, promoting low GWP alternatives, and raising public awareness. Success is defined by achieving these consumption reduction targets, updating the legal framework, training specified numbers of technical and customs personnel, and detecting zero illegal HCFC traffic, ultimately eliminating 361,012.7 tonnes of CO2 equivalent emissions."

"Targeted outcomes include: rehabilitating 41,000 ha Irtysh pine forest (reversing degradation on 642,000 ha; 180,000 ha replanted, 650,000 ha good condition by 2025); vegetating >150,000 ha Dry Aral Seabed (>800,000 ha by 2025); sustainable grazing on >156,000 ha saxaul rangelands; reducing replanting costs by \">=20%\" (to <US$190/ha) with 85% survival rates; preventing ~71,500 tons CO2/year through improved fire management for 642,000 ha; sequestering ~3.9 million tons carbon; and increasing employment (6,000 Irtysh, 4,500 Aral). Success depends on achieving these specific targets, demonstrating improved agency performance for scaled-up investment, meeting efficiency goals, and showing improved livelihoods."

"The Chad Rural Mobility and Connectivity Project targets the improvement and sustainability of access by rural populations to markets and basic social services in the Mandoul and Moyen-Chari provinces. The activity aims to benefit approximately 365,000 people, enhancing their living conditions through better access to markets for food security and essential services like health and education. Specific targeted outcomes include boosting agricultural profitability for farmers and producers, stimulating employment, raising incomes, and reducing poverty through reduced transport costs and times. The project also specifically targets women by increasing their access to employment and other economic opportunities, supported by the provision of women's facilities, and implements a gender-based violence (GBV) risk management strategy. To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve the following: 1) Increase the percentage of rural population provided with all-season roads (Rural Accessibility Index) from a baseline of 5% to 30%. 2) Decrease travel time along improved roads by 30%. 3) Ensure 115 schools and health centers are reached by an improved road. 4) Ensure 100% of improved roads are under routine maintenance. 5) Rehabilitate 400 km of rural roads. 6) Allocate 5% of construction costs to climate change adaptation measures. 7) Achieve 30% employment of women in routine road maintenance. 8) Provide 200 Intermediate Means of Transport (IMT) vehicles. 9) Ensure 75% of beneficiaries are satisfied with road improvement and transport services. 10) Address 100% of grievances through the established mechanisms within two months of registration. 11) Ensure 100% of workers sign a code of conduct. 12) Inventory 100% of roads. 13) Train 25 first responders in road safety. 14) Ensure 100% of villages implement physical road safety measures. 15) Provide and build 100 small common facilities for women. 16) Reach 5,000 people through GBV awareness campaigns. 17) Ensure fair compensation for populations displaced by road rehabilitation, and successfully implement the project to support national strategies for economic stability."

"Targeted outcomes: Improved incomes/food security, sustainable agri/fisheries/marine biodiversity, better LGU systems, livelihood access, decentralized service. Overall success: 20% avg household income increase (vs baseline/control); 30% fish biomass/density increase; 10% siltation decrease; 10% live coral/seagrass increase. Intermediate: 70% LGUs rate planning satisfactory; 15-35% beneficiaries report improved LGU service; 75% LGUs mainstream NRM & pass resolutions/allocate funds; 30% travel time reduction; 10% transport cost savings; 150-180% cropping intensity; 65% water fetching time reduction; 50% fewer water-borne diseases; 30% CFAD allocations by IPs/women. IP partnership DA/NCIP."

"Targeted outcomes include: By Year 10, a 30% agricultural income increase for small producers (30% women/youth), 500,000 ha avoided deforestation, 2,894,400 ha under climate-resilient practices, and 60% of beneficiaries (40% women/30% youth) adopting diversified livelihoods. By Year 5 (Phase I), 50% productivity increase for 3 basic crops, 80% small producers in value chains (40% women/30% youth), 30% AgriMSME sales increase, support for 2,500 individual (1,000 women/750 youth) & 10 commercial applicants, and METT score of 32 for NRM effectiveness. Success requires marketable surpluses, adoption of environmentally friendly technologies, improved institutional capacity, and gender-responsive extension/support."

"The targeted outcomes are improved water quality in the middle Bogotá river basin to Class IV standards (BOD: 50 mg/l, SST: 40 mg/l), reduced flood risk by establishing 100-year return period flood control protection (less than 1% annual overflow probability), and environmental recuperation via the recovery of approximately 175 hectares across eight multifunctional areas along the river (featuring flood zones, ecological habitats, and public spaces). The activity would be considered an overall success upon achieving these specific parameters for water quality, flood control, and land recovery."

"The targeted outcomes are to increase the reliability and capacity of the transmission network. Specifically, to increase the targeted section's capacity from 250 MW to 310 MW and reduce technical failure-related outages from 14 to 2 per year, eliminating bottlenecks and increasing national grid stability.Overall success requires achieving:1. Targeted section capacity of 310 MW.2. Targeted section outages reduced to 2 per year.3. Elimination of transmission bottlenecks and increased national grid stability, thus ensuring overall network reliability and capacity for all power consumers."

"The targeted outcomes of the activity are to improve water quality and operational efficiency for 5.69 million people (49% women) in Shenyang, Anshan, Fuxin, Fushun, and Gaizhou. To be considered an overall success, the project must achieve 100% water quality health standard compliance; significantly reduce Non-Revenue Water (e.g., Fushun to 22.1%, Gaizhou to 35.1%) and energy consumption (e.g., Gaizhou to 0.36 kWh/m³); improve working ratios; achieve 75% customer satisfaction; complete 300 km of rehabilitated pipelines and install 115,000 water meters; establish hydraulic network models in 3 cities and computerized Asset Management systems in 5 cities; train 754 staff; and resolve 100% of grievances on time."

"The targeted outcomes are improved mobility, a safe and efficient transport system, lower fatality rates on key corridors, increased bus ridership, and reduced bus travel times in Anqing, Huaibei, Lu'an, and Wuhu. Overall success is achieved by: reducing distances between safe crossings (e.g., Anqing: 500m to 300m); increasing safe pedestrian crossings (e.g., Anqing: 0 to 10); providing more bus off-street parking (e.g., Anqing: 0 to 229); completing kilometers of new and reconstructed roads (e.g., Anqing new 1.5km, old 5km); increasing population access to bus stops (to 50%/90%); increasing the number of buses in service (e.g., Anqing: 292 to 500); training 40 people; and adopting 3 capacity building plans/strategies."

"The activity targets reducing transport connectivity costs and improving road safety along selected paved sections, enhancing network management efficiency, and strengthening MOPC's institutional capacity. Success requires: reducing travel time on Routes 1 & 3 to 134 minutes; increasing roads in good/fair condition to 90%; expanding the classified network to 5,930 km; rehabilitating 245 km of roads; implementing 8 road safety countermeasures; a fully operational road asset management system; 385,000 direct beneficiaries; 75% road user satisfaction; and an EIRR of 20.9%."

"The Himachal Pradesh State Roads Transformation Project targets several key outcomes: it aims to strengthen state-level transportation institutions, enhance the safety and resilience of state roads, and develop the logistics system for horticulture and overall economic development in Himachal Pradesh. Specifically, the project seeks to improve the efficiency of transport institutions by 15% from a baseline of 50%, reduce maintenance unit costs from US$22,000/km/year to US$10,000/km/year, and decrease overall transport costs by 25% (from 100% to 75%). A critical safety outcome is the reduction of road accident fatalities from 18.17 to 11.53 people per 100,000 population. The project also targets the construction of 80 kilometers of roads, including 80 kilometers of non-rural roads, to stimulate horticulture and economic growth. Socially, it aims for 30% of maintenance contracts to be awarded to women and a 50% increase in women assigned as operational staff by the HP Motor Vehicle Administration. Road user satisfaction with improved road conditions is targeted to reach a score of 5 on a 1-5 scale. Additional outcomes include the development and adoption of a logistics system and strategy for horticultural and overall economic growth. To consider the activity an overall success, all these quantitative and qualitative targets must be met. This means the complete reorganization and operationalization of HPRIDC, the Direct Labor Wing of HPPWD, HPMVA, and the Directorate of Transportation of HPDOT. A pilot service level/performance agreement for direct labor operation must be implemented, and 1,000 kilometers of the State Core Road Network must be maintained under private contractor performance-based maintenance contracts. A dedicated maintenance financing scheme needs to be established, and a policy framework for mainstreaming resilience in Himalayan mountain roads developed, with bio-engineering solutions applied. Environmental and social management capacity must be strengthened, and patrol and emergency response equipment commissioned in pilot districts and a safe corridor, alongside the training of police and community volunteers for road safety. Furthermore, women should gain improved access to self-help groups for bio-engineering solutions and post-construction road maintenance, as well as employment within corporate entities. Success also implies overcoming the challenges of inefficient institutional performance, inefficient SME and agro-logistics systems, and poor driver behavior."

"Targeted outcomes include improved productivity and commercialization of livestock products for smallholders, enhanced livelihoods and resilience for pastoralists, reduced disease losses, increased sustainably managed rangelands, and a net GHG sink of 242.7M tCO2eq. Success requires achieving specific end targets by 2023: ruminant mortality at 12%, broiler live weight 90kg/m2/yr, 10 weanlings/sow/yr, 720L milk/cow/lactation, 20kg honey/beehive/yr, 25% incremental sales, 140,000 farmers reached, 72,000 adopting improved tech, and 80% beneficiary satisfaction."

"The activity targets: (i) improving access to 'safely-managed' water supply for 589,680 people and sanitation for 3,618,900 people in rural Bangladesh, and (ii) strengthening sector institutional capacity by having 78 Upazilas establish and use a monitoring system. Overall success requires achieving 80% user satisfaction with quality WASH services, mobilizing BDT 12.19 billion in MFI financing, building 763,000 handwashing stations, ensuring 1,010 piped water schemes are continuously operational (80% water quality, 80% tariff collection), 30% women chairing water committees, 150 public toilets operational, 600,000 sanitary napkins sold by female LEs, training 2,340 LEs and 14,718 officials, endorsing 4 policy documents, and 70% beneficiaries receiving monthly BCC messages."

"The targeted outcomes of the Regional Electricity Access and BEST Project are to increase access to grid electricity, enhance power system stability, and integrate renewable energy within the West Africa Power Pool. Specifically, the project aims to provide new or improved electricity service to 1,150,000 people (with 510,000 being female), deliver an additional firm capacity for frequency control of 205 Megawatts, mobilize US$100 million in private capital, and enable 200 Megawatts of additional renewable energy capacity. It also seeks to enhance the market regulation capacity of the ECOWAS Regional Electricity Regulatory Agency (ERERA). For the activity to be considered an overall success, it requires the availability of energy generation, households' financial means to connect, availability of renewable resources, favorable regional and national integration policies, and effective management of the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic."

"The Malawi Resilience and Disaster Risk Management Project targets supporting the Government of Malawi in meeting immediate food security and livelihoods restoration needs of drought-affected communities, promoting recovery and resilience in key affected sectors, and potentially providing emergency support. It aims to lower vulnerability, improve community resilience, and enhance disaster response capacity. The project will directly benefit 200,000 households with livelihood support via an Inputs-for-Assets (IFA) program, over 1.6 million people with direct food support, 160,000 households through increased agricultural productivity, and 2.1 million people from resilient water supply infrastructure and irrigation. A key outcome is addressing the financing gap for immediate and long-term food security needs and strengthening GoM's capacity in disaster preparedness."

"The targeted outcomes of the Lilongwe Water and Sanitation Project are primarily to increase access to improved water services and safely managed sanitation services in Lilongwe City. Specifically, the project aims to:1. Directly benefit 250,000 people with improved water services, with a target of 125,000 (50%) being women and 100,000 (40%) from the poorest two quintiles of the population.2. Directly benefit another 250,000 people with safely managed sanitation services, with a target of 125,000 (50%) being women and 100,000 (40%) from the poorest two quintiles of the population.To achieve these overarching goals, the project also has several intermediate targets:Water Supply Improvements:1. Lay 169 km of water supply pipeline.2. Establish 27,594 new household water connections receiving improved services.3. Reduce Non-Revenue Water (NRW) from a baseline of 36% to an end target of 26%.Sanitation Improvements:1. Lay 107 km of sewer pipeline.2. Provide 15,000 household sewerage connections benefiting from safely managed sanitation services.3. Establish 5,000 new sewerage connections.4. Implement 32,000 improved household sanitation facilities.5. "[\"The project aims to interconnect Malawi and Mozambique's transmission systems for bilateral/regional power trade within the Southern African Power Pool (SAPP). Specific outcomes include enabling 1,200 MW of wheeling capacity and increasing annual electricity traded between ESCOM and EDM to 360,000 MWh. Intermediate goals include the construction of 218 km of transmission line. Broader objectives are regional integration, promoting competitiveness through infrastructure, reducing vulnerability to shocks, and improved governance.\", \"Overall success requires achieving the targeted wheeling capacity and electricity trade. It is contingent on fair, transparent, and firm Power Purchase Agreements, maximizing mutually beneficial trade, designing technical solutions with excess capacity for long-term load development, advancing key technical studies/procurement, and ensuring clear co-financing arrangements. ESCOM's financial viability is crucial, requiring approved tariff increases for positive Free Cash Flow, debt service coverage, financing new connections, and addressing challenges in attracting private sector transmission partnerships.\"]"

"The RY-Power Sector aims to improve electricity supply efficiency/quality, enhance PEC's operational/financial position, strengthen corporate governance, and establish a new industry structure/regulatory framework, relieving power constraints. Success requires: 80% consumer satisfaction with supply quality, terminal voltage <200V at <=5% points, avg. supply failures/week reduced to 2. Key reforms include separating policy/regulatory/operating functions, corporatizing PEC for financial viability, and establishing cost-recovery tariffs. Financially, PEC must achieve break-even on operating costs (from FY06), maintain a 1.5x debt service coverage (FY06-10), self-financing ratio of 10-15%, and accounts receivable <=3 months of sales."

"Targeted outcomes: Improved Java-Bali power sector performance, PLN financial/corporate restructuring, strengthened electricity supply (transmission/subtransmission) for enhanced reliability, relieved bottlenecks, and met demand growth. PGN gas sector restructuring/utilization, reduced energy sector fiscal burden/risks, promoted commercial viability, rationalized pricing, and cleaner fuel. Success: PLN's independent generation, distribution, transmission companies established; improved PLN rate of return; completed natural gas pricing/PGN restructuring studies; increased East Java generation dispatch; reduced substation loadings; improved voltages. Overall NPV US$34.3M and EIRR 14.6%."

"The activity aims to strengthen Amman's Municipal Solid Waste Management (MSWM) operationally, financially, and environmentally through landfill upgrades, electricity generation, GHG mitigation, and improved collection efficiency/cost recovery. Success requires: increasing sanitary landfill disposal from 0% to 80% of collected MSW; generating 160,000 MWh from LFG recovery; improving SWM cost-recovery ratio from 63% (2007) to 75% by 2013; generating 950,000 tons of CERs by 2013; constructing 8M tons capacity landfill infrastructure with leachate treatment meeting Jordanian standards; reducing collection vehicle km/ton waste ratio from 5.5 to 2.5 in new transfer station areas; and achieving positive economic (NPV US$16.9M) and financial (NPV US$18.4M) viability."

"[\"The activity targets increasing agricultural productivity on approximately 0.80 million feddan, preventing yield losses from waterlogging and soil salinity, and building EPADP capacity through technical assistance. It aims to install/renew 0.72 million feddan of subsurface drainage (0.39M old, 0.11M reclaimed, 0.3M rehabilitation), implement a 10,000 feddan pilot trenchless system, and improve open drains serving 0.35 million feddan. Expected outcomes include 7-10% yield increases for cereals, 15-20% for vegetables/fruits, and financial returns of 25-28% for farmers. It also aims to strengthen M&E and foster farmer participation in O&M via CUAs.\",\"Overall success requires achieving an Economic Rate of Return of 23% and a Net Present Value of US$178 million. Key success factors include demonstrating planned incremental crop yield increases (7-10% for cereals, 15-20% for other key crops) on 0.72 million feddan by reducing water table depth and soil salinity. All physical drainage infrastructure targets must be met, encompassing 0.72 million feddan subsurface and 0.35 million feddan open drain improvements. Additionally, financial returns for farmers (25-28%), effective EPADP capacity building, continuous M&E, and active farmer participation in O&M through CUAs are crucial.\"]"

"The CV Road Sector Support Project targets several key outcomes. Through Component 1, Institutional Support, the project aims to enhance the Borrower's road sector management by strengthening the Ministry of Infrastructure and Transport (MIT) as a sector planner and quality guarantor, fostering policy and strategy formulation, and maintaining continuous sector dialogue. This includes supporting the Road Sector Program Coordination Office (PCO) for coordination, providing technical assistance and capacity building to the Road Agency (IE) and Road Maintenance Fund (CV-FMR) for network management and financing, assisting the Civil Engineering Laboratory of Cape Verde (LEC) with testing facilities and training, and reinforcing the General Inspectorate of Public and Private Works (IGOPP) for civil works regulation. Within the MIT, it aids the General Directorate of Infrastructure & Basic Sanitation (DGISB) for procurement and management, and the Studies and Planning Unit (GEP) for an information management system. The strategic objectives for the road sector are to facilitate commerce and ensure access to social and administrative services via a continuous and sustainably maintained road network, ensuring each island has a core network and local roads with reliable access, and establishing an efficient, transparent, and sustainable management system, rationalizing management with commercial approaches and user-fee-based financing. Component 2, Road Infrastructure, targets achieving basic connectivity and improving mobility on specific island networks through prioritized investments, rehabilitating segments such as Alcatraz-Figueira (Maio), Ribeira Brava-Tarrafal (São Nicolau), and Salamansa Intersection-Baia do Norte (São Vicente), filling gaps not covered by other donors. Success for the activity depends on achieving outcome indicators, including: increasing and sustaining the asset value of the national road network; implementing performance-based road maintenance contracts; improving institutional capacities (e.g., DGISB contract implementation within budget, LEC staffing/self-sufficiency, IGOPP staffing/work plan implementation, GEP report production, PCO reporting); and realizing socio-economic benefits such as reduced vehicle operating costs, increased adult mobility, decreased travel time to markets, and reduced comparative travel costs. Specifically, the project will be considered an overall success if the Road Maintenance Fund is created and functioning, establishes and manages annual budgets, and the Road Agency completes and implements annual road network maintenance plans within budget. Additionally, successful implementation of pilot performance-based maintenance contracts, DGISB implementing all contracts within 15% of the planned period and budget, LEC increasing staffing by at least two to meet responsibilities and achieving financial self-sufficiency for operational costs, and IGOPP increasing staffing by at least 15% and implementing its annual work plan by 90% are crucial."

"The targeted outcomes include improving the efficiency of the state road network by reducing vehicle operating costs, freight transport losses, and travel times, and by enhancing access to markets and services, especially in areas with economic potential. Other outcomes are increasing competitiveness, reducing regional disparities, establishing the state as a logistics hub, and improving public sector efficiency in transport management, leading to improved accessibility, safety, and reduced logistics costs. Success would be achieved with a 5% reduction in operating costs to vehicle users, a 15% increase in the use of surfaced road transport infrastructure, and 66% of the state paved road network being in good condition by project end."

"The activity targets enhancing long-term environmental sustainability and resilience of food production systems in Karamoja, primarily by: 1) Strengthening policy and institutional frameworks for improved crop/livestock production, food value-chains, and INRM, supported by multi-stakeholder platforms and legal instruments. 2) Increasing land area and agro-ecosystems under INRM/SLM through enhanced technical capacity, trained community members, income-generating activities, and small grants. 3) Establishing a multi-scale assessment/monitoring framework for resilience and Global Environmental Benefits (GEBS), including capacity building and regional linkages. Additional outcomes include community adoption of gender-sensitive integrated landscape management practices and innovative multi-stakeholder planning mechanisms for SLM."

"The activity aims to improve sustainable urban environmental infrastructure in selected Zhejiang Qiantang River Basin areas. Targeted outcomes include constructing water supply treatment plants/distribution networks (Zhuji, Wucheng) for full cost recovery; developing wastewater collection/treatment facilities for O&M cost recovery (Jiande, Qujiang, Lanxi, Pan'an, Tonglu, Longyou); enhancing solid waste management (Jiande) for limited O&M cost recovery with subsidies; and strengthening institutions for project management and financial sustainability. Broader benefits encompass improved public health, reduced pollution, increased land value, and protected water bodies."

"The targeted outcomes are to improve electricity supply efficiency and reliability, and increase access across Côte d'Ivoire. By 2022, key targets include reducing cumulative power outages in rehabilitated substations from 3,773 to 3,207 hours, decreasing annual electricity losses from 15% to 12.5%, and providing new or improved electricity service to 623,316 people. Additionally, 201 villages will be electrified, with 85,153 rural and 18,733 urban households gaining connections. The project aims to reinforce 18 and construct 1 high voltage substations, increase bulk supply capacity to 1,908 MVA, and reduce average outage times in Abidjan (to 20 hours) and 10 capital cities (to 5 hours)."

"The targeted outcomes are: increased electricity supply (200 GWh/year) to Eastern Guinea, enabled electricity trade (800 GWh/year) between Guinea and Mali, and enhanced Guinea's export capability (150 MW added) to WAPP countries. This aims to provide Mali with cheaper power (US$0.11-0.13/kWh imports vs. US$0.23/kWh thermal), allow Guinea to earn hard currency, enhance regional trade, and facilitate renewable energy integration. Success requires achieving these quantitative targets, maintaining economic viability (positive NPV, EIRR > 10%), government commitment to power sector reforms for credit-worthy utilities, and sustainable operation/maintenance, alongside establishing a strong regional power market with energy quality, quantity, and payment commitments."

"Targeted outcomes aim to increase food production (200,000 MT of crops, 36,000 MT cassava, 33,500 MT other staples annually) and improve resilience for 420,000 farmers (210,000 female). This includes distributing 2,800 MT of inputs, providing 20,000 food baskets, vaccinating 72,000 animals, establishing flood early-warning systems, and creating 580,000 person-days of employment. Overall success requires achieving these targets, an NPV of US$8.3M and EIRR of 15.4%, timely input availability, reasonable food prices, and government commitment to market resilience."

"Targeted outcomes include: improved access to irrigation/drainage, strengthened institutional arrangements, improved/expanded irrigation infrastructure, increased rural production/incomes/employment, transfer of facilities to WUAs, 37 MW power generation, 400k tons rice/700k tons tomato per year, flood risk mitigation for 10M people, and enhanced dam safety. Success requires: 1M direct beneficiaries (50% female); 50,000 ha irrigated land (27k improved, 23k new); cropping intensity 180%; 550 operational WUAs (joint O&M fund management by Apex WUAFs/RBDAs); 8 dams meeting ALARP standards; 80% irrigation fee recovery by WUAs."

"The targeted outcomes of the Power Sector Efficiency Enhancement Project (PROMEF) are to improve ENEE's operational and financial performance, thereby contributing to the sustainability of Honduras's power sector. The activity would be considered an overall success if the following performance indicators are achieved: an increased Cash Recovery Index (CRI) in each of the three distribution regional sub-networks, specifically through reduced losses; a nationwide decrease in the System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) and System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI); an improvement in Earning Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA); enhanced independence and accountability of ENEE's distribution unit; improved quality of power supply nationwide; and better customer services."

"[\"The targeted outcomes of the Lusaka Transmission and Distribution Rehabilitation Project are to increase the capacity and improve the reliability of the electricity transmission and distribution system in the Lusaka area. This includes reinforcing networks, improving ZESCO's power delivery capacity, and enhancing services for agricultural, commercial, industrial, and household customers, thereby supporting economic growth and welfare. The project aims to achieve better voltage levels, improved reliability, and reduced load shedding during peak hours.\", \"Overall success is defined by achieving: transmission capacity along the Lusaka transmission ring reaching 900 MVA, energy sales increasing to 3,548 GWh by 2020, average interruption frequency (SAIFI) reduced from 12 to 6, and 1.2 million direct beneficiaries (51% female). Intermediate targets include rehabilitating 75km and constructing 5km of transmission lines, installing 6 new switching stations, upgrading substation capacity in Waterworks to 270 MVA and in the Lusaka area to 180 MVA, training 50 ZESCO staff, and completing new project studies. Financially, success requires a Net Present Value of US$585 million (at 10% discount rate) and a Financial Internal Rate of Return of 18%.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes include fostering a profitable mining industry contributing to Romania's economic growth and improved living standards. Specific outcomes by pillar are: Employment: Re-deploy unemployed workers (target 25,000 jobs, 10,000 beneficiaries, $11M micro-credit at 90% repayment, 250 sub-projects). Mine Closure: Reduce environmental liabilities; local communities own reclaimed land (target 20 mines closed, 923 ha rehabilitated). Institutional Development: Improve mining sector environmental performance; companies comply with regulations (subsidies reduced to $60M). Success hinges on reducing operating subsidies, acceptable employment restructuring, closing uneconomic mines, and restructuring/privatizing viable mines."

"The Power Sector Sustainability and Efficiency Program III aims to improve the financial sustainability and operating efficiency of the power sector in the Dominican Republic.Key targeted outcomes include:1.  Reduce fiscal transfers as a % of GDP from 0.67% (2019 baseline) to 0.2% by 2023.2.  Decrease the index of electricity losses in transmission and distribution from 28.4% (2019) to 22.5% by 2023.3.  Improve the ratio of operating expenses to operating revenues from 18.5% (2019) to 13.6% by 2023.4.  Increase transparency in managing electricity distribution companies, maintaining 2 published management indexes by 2023.5.  Enhance cost efficiency of electricity service, reducing the annual cost of frequency regulation service from US$3.6/MWh (2019) to US$2.75/MWh by 2023.6.  Optimize electricity system expansion by increasing installed renewable generation capacity from 1211 MW (2019) to 1331 MW by 2023.7.  Promote more efficient management of electrical power demand, achieving 1 energy efficiency project in the public sector by 2023.8.  Increase the effectiveness of the Bonoluz targeted subsidy coverage from 36.2% (2019) to 63% by 2023.To be considered an overall success, the activity must achieve these specific targets by the end of 2023, alongside strengthening the institutional and supervision capacity of the power sector, improving sector planning and regulations, and supporting enhancements in the management and operation of electricity distribution companies. This includes distribution companies meeting loss reduction and management improvement targets, the Executive Branch approving an accountability regime, the Ministry of Finance issuing and applying regulations for full payment to distribution companies, and annual audited financial statements from distribution companies, supported by online offices for customer service and billing."

"The targeted outcomes include strengthening the sustainability and efficiency of the Lifeline Road Network, improving its overall quality, upgrading roads in selected regions, creating local employment in road construction, and improving access to markets. Specific goals are to reduce travel time and increase the percentage of Lifeline roads in good or fair condition in selected areas. The project also aims for better road conditions, reduced public spending, improved industry standards through Output and Performance-based Road Contracting (OPRC), and enhanced road safety. Institutional strengthening of MoTC, ARD, and PIU, and supporting sector reform for long-term sustainability are also key outcomes."

"The targeted outcomes of the Geothermal Clean Energy Investment Project are to increase power generation from renewable geothermal resources and reduce local and global environmental impacts. Specifically, the project aims to install 150 MW of new geothermal power generation capacity (110 MW at Ulubelu and 40 MW at Lahendong). This substantial increase in renewable energy is expected to displace coal-based production, reduce the power sector's carbon footprint, and decrease local environmental impacts. For the activity to be considered an overall success, it would need to achieve specific annual pollution reductions: 3,000 tonnes of nitrogen oxides, 5,400 tonnes of sulfur dioxide, 2,500 tonnes of total suspended particulates, and 1,100,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide. Success also hinges on ensuring sustainability through proper assessment of geothermal field capacities, optimal sizing of steam gathering systems and power plants for efficient energy utilization, and securing long-term power purchase agreements with PLN."

"The Rural Access Programme 3 targets reducing poverty and exclusion, enhancing incomes, and fostering sustainable market access in Western Nepal. Key outcomes include: lifting 30,000 people out of poverty, generating 8.3 million person-days of employment, maintaining 1200 km of roads (over six years), constructing 153 km of new roads (e.g., Humla link), benefiting 2 million people via improved access, and increasing 50,000 farmers' incomes by £130 annually. Success is contingent on achieving these quantitative targets, enhancing gender-disaggregated results, providing jobs for women, strengthening social protection via the Karnali Employment Programme, and expanding rural infrastructure efforts for the most marginalized."

"[\"The main targeted outcome is providing all-season road access to rural communities in Wangdue, Dagana, and Pemagatshel. Specific outcome indicators include bringing 60% of beneficiaries within 2km/30 min walking distance of an all-season road, reducing travel time to economic/social centers by 50%, increasing trips to these centers by 50%, and ensuring key commodity prices in beneficiary villages are within 10% of nearest town's prices. Also, a 50% reduction in transportation costs of goods/supplies and improved beneficiary satisfaction.\", \"Overall success requires achieving all targeted outcomes and completing all physical and institutional components. This includes 100% construction of target new feeder roads (approx. 75 km), upgrading existing roads (approx. 15 km) to all-season standards, constructing/upgrading all 8 target bridges, and maintaining all feeder roads to all-season standards. Institutional success includes delivering all planned training programs and ensuring the Department of Roads (DOR) applies Environmentally Friendly Road Construction (EFRC) in all road projects regardless of funding sources.\"]"

"The Alat and Karakul Water Supply Project targets improving public water supply service coverage, quality, and efficiency. Success entails: providing improved water supply access to 232,000 people (50.70% female) by Year 5; 98% of water samples meeting potable quality standards by Year 5; maintaining a financial cash working ratio below 0.9; reducing hydraulic water losses; improving bill collection ratio to 80% by Year 5; reducing main breakages; increasing water production facility availability; and achieving water supply reliability of 20 hours/day in urban and 6 hours/day in rural areas by Year 5."

"The activity targets include avoiding 1.80 million tons of CO2e emissions and reducing deforested area to 49.70 thousand hectares by 2025. It also aims to increase agroforestry income for native communities to USD 423/family/year and for small forest users to USD 573/family/year by 2025. An additional target is to implement forest monitoring and surveillance across 200,000.00 hectares of Amazonian area by 2025. Overall success requires achieving these specific targets, along with capacitating regional governments, civil society, and native communities to manage forests, monitor land use change, and protect biodiversity, while contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions and increased household incomes."

"The targeted outcomes are: increasing productivity and sales volume of small and medium-size agricultural producers, enhancing rural economy's competitiveness, 10% increase in labor productivity, 12% in land productivity, 20% in sales volume, and improved profit margins. Success would entail achieving these increases, preparing/adopting 15 strategic investment plans, medium/high private sector participation in 15 provinces, creating 673.8 new productive hectares, 65% improved irrigation efficiency, increased yields, upgraded product quality, and 60-86% net incremental farm income for 68,000 producers."

"The targeted outcomes include improved management and transparency of the sovereign wealth fund and fisheries assets, more efficient cash management, better public procurement, a reduction in the gender gap in water collection duties, expanded household access to 24/7 reticulated water in South Tarawa, and enhanced early grade literacy results. The activity would be considered an overall success upon the achievement of these specific results, thereby strengthening public financial management and supporting inclusive growth, as tracked through data-driven monitoring by the Economic Reform Taskforce."

"The targeted outcomes of the Second Road and Safety Improvement Project are: improved condition and quality of sections of the M-03 road, increased safety on roads in Ukraine, and improved capacity of Ukravtodor for strategic road development and safety planning. The activity will be considered an overall success when: the riding quality on rehabilitated M-03 road sections improves from an International Roughness Index (IRI) of 5.0 to 2.0; improved road safety on the Lubny-Poltava road reduces fatalities and serious injuries per 100 million vehicle-km from 24.0 to 14.4; improved road safety on project corridors shows a 20% reduction in fatalities and serious injuries per 100 million vehicle-km (from 100% to 80%); and 50.0 km of road sections are upgraded from 2 to 4 lanes."

"The targeted outcomes of the activity, \"CI -DPO-Poverty Reduct. Support Credit 3,\" are to strengthen governance, improve public administration, and facilitate private-sector-led growth, aligning with Côte d'Ivoire's 2012-2015 National Development Plan. Specifically, by June 2016, the activity aims to: 1) increase the Transparency International Corruption Perceptions Index score to 35+ (from 29), increase procurement audits to 100 (from 10), reduce single-source procurement contracts to <15% of total (from 37%), and have 10 local governments produce computerized budget reports (from 0); 2) reduce days to settle commercial disputes to 450 (from 770), reduce construction permit days to 175 (from 475), and reduce property registration cost to 6% of value (from 13%); 3) increase farm-gate price as % of CIF export price to 50% (from >60% baseline), restructure 20 cashew/cotton cooperatives (from <5), increase domestic cashew processing to 8% (from 0%), provide technical assistance to 10 women's cashew cooperatives (from 0), increase paddy rice production to 2 million tons (from 950,000 tons), and reduce government arrears to SIR to CFAF 35 billion or below (from CFAF 92.4 billion). The activity also targets reducing extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity, with expected positive impacts on poor and non-poor households through accelerated economic growth, expanded employment, income opportunities, and enhanced government capacity for pro-poor spending.Success would be defined by the achievement of all these specific targets, the realization of the broader program development objectives, and the absence of adverse poverty or social impacts, leading to increased farmer incomes, reduced income volatility, and overall improved living standards and governance."

"[\"The activity targets sustaining governance and civil service reforms, and broadening reforms to include economic transformation and human capital development under the AfT. Outcomes include strengthening transparency, accountability, anti-corruption, budget execution, and oversight; addressing infrastructure constraints for economic expansion, diversification, and job creation; and improving access/quality of health/education for the poor. It also aims to build resilience to shocks, increase fiscal space, and reduce poverty/inequity.\",\"Success would be evidenced by sustained governance and civil service reforms, and broadened reforms in economic transformation and human capital development. Key indicators include greater transparency, accountability, and reduced corruption, leading to efficient public service delivery, especially to the poor. Also, economic expansion and diversification with more jobs, improved infrastructure, and better access/quality of health/education, building resilience to shocks, increased fiscal space, and reduced poverty/inequity.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Ethiopia Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene Project are primarily focused on improving access to water supply and sanitation facilities and promoting better hygiene practices. For water supply, outcomes include increasing the number of people provided with access to improved water sources (urban/rural), constructing or rehabilitating improved community water points, establishing new piped household water connections, and rehabilitating existing piped household water connections. For sanitation, outcomes involve increasing the number of people with access to improved sanitation facilities (urban/rural), constructing improved latrines, and training people (male/female) to improve hygiene behavior or sanitation practices. The activity would be considered an overall success when performance and progress, tracked against these specific sector core indicators using baseline figures from the National Water Inventory, demonstrate the achievement or surpassing of the set targets for these outcomes."

"Targeted outcomes: Provide safe, reliable, and cost-effective bridges on Nepal's Strategic Roads Network, measured by reduced likelihood of road departure crashes, enhanced DOR capabilities for resilient bridge designs, and estimated road user cost savings. Overall success requires: 8,000m road safety upgrades; 4,500m major maintenance; 4,000m new 2-lane bridges; 2,000m new 4-lane bridges; 5,000m backlog bridges completed; >NPR 2,971 million user cost savings; 10 advanced bridge designs prepared; a staffed/mobilized DATC; >33% female engineers recruited; revised design-build guidelines endorsed/submitted by MoPIT to PPMO; 5,000 BSMS-monitored worksites; and a 10-day average response time for DOR's Grievance Redress Mechanism."

"[\"APPIIC targets: easing 4 bottlenecks, developing 8 IBN quality projects (Stage 1), securing $1.8bn private investment, and IBN gaining autonomy in HR/finance/approvals. EPI targets: developing 3 provincial economic strategies, implementing 6 key provincial projects, effective municipal economic management via prioritized projects/policies, harnessing sectoral growth across 3+ sectors (manufacturing, tourism), and addressing cross-cutting constraints including investment climate and e-commerce.\",\"FSSP targets: strengthening banking stability (financial infrastructure, liquidity for BFIs, improved lending practices, robust deposit guarantee), improving NBFI regulation and supervision (CIT, EPF governance), securing a sovereign credit rating for Nepal to access international finance, and improving long-term infrastructure project funding via an Infrastructure Development Bank. Overall success implies achieving these specific outcomes and targets for APPIIC (by March 2021), EPI, and FSSP as detailed.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include: strengthening provincial government capacity for water resources monitoring & management; increasing water-efficient practice adoption by users in Nari & Porali basins; transforming water management to an integrated multi-sectoral river basin approach for equitable distribution, environmental sustainability & improved livelihoods, focusing on agricultural efficiency. Also, developing IWRM capacity, improving hydromet data, addressing infrastructure, promoting ecosystem conservation & climate resilience. Success entails: ~705,000 direct beneficiaries (50% female); developed & maintained river basin information systems; ~38,000 water users adopting new irrigation/drainage services; ~16,000 farm households adopting improved agricultural technologies; ~82,000 people better protected from floods."

"The targeted outcomes of the Urban Transport in Metropolitan Areas activity are primarily focused on improving urban transport quality, access, and governance, especially for low-income populations in Metropolitan Areas, particularly within the Buenos Aires Metropolitan Area (AMBA), Tucumán, Posadas, and Mendoza. The activity aims to:1. Support the design and creation of a multijurisdictional Metropolitan Transport Agency (MTA) for AMBA.2. Strengthen authorities' capacity in urban transport decision-making, planning, priority setting, and resource allocation.3. Improve the quality and performance of urban transport infrastructure and/or services in Tucumán, Posadas, and Mendoza.4. Enhance physical integration and access to public transport networks in AMBA.5. Improve academic training in urban transport through the design and delivery of a postgraduate Master's course.To consider the activity an overall success, the following would need to be achieved:1.  **For AMBA:**    *   The Buenos Aires Transport Metropolitan Agency is legally created, adequately staffed according to the designed organigram, and possesses adequate offices, equipment, and its own operating budget.    *   The AMBA Urban Transport Planning Observatory is fully functioning and data is accessible via a webpage.    *   The AMBA Transport Master Plan is completed.    *   Users' quality perception of access to AMBA suburban railway stations is increased.2.  **For Metropolitan Areas in general:**    *   An increased number of Metropolitan Areas have updated origin-destination household surveys and urban transport/land use master plans and transportation modeling tools.3.  **For Tucumán:**    *   A reduction in the percentage of primary school students absent on low transitability days.    *   A reduction in the number of appointments in CAP (Centros de Atención Primaria or Community Health Centers) cancelled on low transitability days.    *   A reduction in the number of days without public bus circulation.    *   An increase in the average speed of public buses in Barrio 11 de Marzo.4.  **For Posadas:**    *   An increase in users' perception of public transport system quality.    *   An increase in the average speed of public buses on corridors.5.  **For Mendoza:**    *   Improved public transport services on the Colector Papagayo.6.  **For Sectoral Training:**    *   A post-graduate course in urban transport is registered with the federal Ministry of Education.    *   The Master's course achieves self-sufficiency after the initial two promotions."

"The Provincial Road Infrastructure Project targets several key outcomes: improving the reliability of provincial road sector assets and the efficiency of their management to support the country's productive sector, competitiveness, and economic growth. Specifically, it aims to strengthen the planning process for comprehensive multi-year road programs, introduce performance-based contracts (CREMA) as a sustainable and cost-effective road management strategy, and build the necessary capacities in provincial road agencies to transform them into results-oriented organizations. The project also expects to reduce transport costs, foster regional productivity and competitiveness (especially in six participating provinces), and provide better logistics through easier access to national road networks and production centers, thereby creating business development opportunities and contributing to provincial economic growth. Furthermore, it seeks to avoid overspending on some network segments by using economically viable standards, allowing resource reallocation, and increasing the involvement of private firms. The project aligns with the Country Assistance Strategy to improve infrastructure for economic growth and poverty reduction, and contributes to poverty alleviation through new labor opportunities. It also aims to remove infrastructure bottlenecks, balance performance among provinces, rationalize road expenditures, generate incentives for cost-efficiency, facilitate CREMA implementation at the provincial level, and mainstream environmental management and road safety policies.To be considered an overall success, the activity must demonstrate: a positive evolution of average network roughness on targeted provincial roads; an increased percentage of the total paved network under CREMA contracts; the preparation and public dissemination of strategic 5-year road programs; and the preparation and public dissemination of annual performance reports indicating achieved results. Project sustainability is crucial, requiring the implementation of comprehensive, cost-effective strategies to maintain the provincial network in a steady-state condition. This involves the transformation of Provincial Road Agencies into results-oriented organizations, the effective application of CREMA systems, and complementing rehabilitation works with results-oriented maintenance in non-CREMA networks. For routine maintenance on sections not covered by CREMA contracts, success hinges on ensuring that the level of service provided to road users matches, to a reasonable extent, the standards achieved in loan-improved sections, and that annual maintenance programs prevent open potholes, edge breaks, and ensure adequate restoration of road markings and signs to meet critical road safety requirements."

"The targeted outcomes of the Second Karnataka State Highway Improvement activity are: (1) To accelerate the development of the Core Road Network by leveraging public sector outlays with private sector financing, and improving the institutional effectiveness of road sector agencies to deliver effective and safe roads to users. (2) GOK generates at least US$400 million in new private sector capital for CRN improvement and management by end-of-project. (3) A 15% reduction in Vehicle Operating Costs and a 25% reduction in Travel Time Cost on project corridors by EOP. (4) A 30% reduction in road-accident related fatalities on safe corridor pilots by EOP. The activity would be considered an overall success if all these targets are met."

"The targeted outcome of the Citywide Inclusive Sanitation Project is to improve access to inclusive, climate-resilient, adequate, and safely managed sanitation services in the project cities of Mataram, Pontianak, and Semarang. To consider the activity an overall success, the primary condition is that by 2030, at least 2.5 million people in the project cities must have access to adequate, safely managed, and resilient sanitation services. This success is contingent upon achieving several key outputs and indicators, including: by 2029, the construction and operationalization of four new climate-resilient wastewater treatment plants (WWTPs) with co-treatment of fecal sludge and a combined capacity of approximately 57,000 cubic meters per day, built to national standards and climate resilience criteria; the construction of at least 200 kilometers of climate-resilient sewer networks; the strengthening of city-level information and disaster management systems to include gender-responsive early warning systems integrated with new sanitation facilities; and the development of a road map for upgrades of fecal sludge management systems that incorporate gender-responsive, climate, and disaster resilience measures. Additionally, success requires the adoption of sanitation operation and maintenance (O&M) guidelines considering climate resilience and gender equality and social inclusion (GESI)-responsive measures, and the establishment of wastewater tariffs and other financial measures to ensure 100% O&M cost recovery for sanitation services. Institutional effectiveness must also be improved through the development of guidelines for integrating sewered and non-sewered systems, the expansion of utility operators' roles to include wastewater management, and through community engagement where at least 50% of women participating in sanitation awareness training report increased awareness of safely managed sanitation, hygiene, and health, and at least 20% of wastewater operator staff report increased knowledge from training."

"The targeted outcomes of the Energy for Rural Transformation III activity are to increase rural access to electricity, increase the use of renewable energy, and promote energy efficiency. To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve an increased number of: on-grid connections, households using Solar PV systems, health centers with access to electricity using solar systems, rural schools with access to electricity using solar systems, and water pumping stations with access to electricity using solar systems. This aligns with Uganda's FY2011-2015 CAS and GoU's RESP-2 (2013-2022) to attain \"Vision 2040\" developmental goals."

"[\"The Decentralized Rural Electrification Project targets testing institutional, financial, and delivery mechanisms to promote better access to electricity in rural and peri-urban areas, particularly developing decentralized and affordable village electrification schemes and mobilizing private sector financing. A global objective is to remove barriers to adopting and disseminating Renewable Energy Technologies (RETs) and mitigate CO2 emissions, targeting a reduction of over 30 kilotons by project end (100 kilotons over economic life). It aims to establish a DRE regulatory framework, create BERD, and operationalize FERD to address financing. The project plans to supply electricity to approximately 20,000 households in over 75 villages, including at least 5,000 Solar Home Systems (SHS) in 50 villages and 80 pico-hydro systems.\",\"For the activity to be considered an overall success, the established institutions and regulations must work effectively, and individual loan collection rates should not fall below 75%. The FERD must be operational with sustainable government and donor support, and private sector financing mobilized to electrify 20,000 target households. Critical assumptions include political, social, and economic stability; sustained government commitment to attracting private financing and supporting FERD; continued interest from villages and the private sector; acceptance of the new regulatory framework; affordability of DRE services; and the successful delivery of rural credit by local banks, without high delinquency rates.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include immediate support for critical energy infrastructure repair, restoration of basic services, provision of winterization materials, and enhancing natural gas supply to Kharkiv. Broader aims involve building capacity for energy management and accelerating a green transition. Success requires 4.6M people to benefit from enhanced energy infrastructure, 4M from improved transmission, 1.9M to gain renewable energy access, 1962 MWt renewable energy capacity installed, and $680M investment leveraged for green recovery, alongside training 2300 individuals and reaching 130,000 through awareness campaigns."

"Targeted outcomes are to reduce market distortions, improve market efficiency, and promote public sector fiscal efficiency and accountability. Success requires reduced foreign trade transaction costs, increased tax collection, easier business entry/exit, improved access to finance, a stronger financial market, expanded social assistance, and a more efficient/transparent public sector. Overall success also entails neutral/positive poverty and social impacts (e.g., better jobs, living standards) and positive environmental impacts (e.g., anti-deforestation, Escazú Agreement ratification)."

"The targeted outcomes of the \"First Fiscal Reform and Growth Credit\" activity are broadly categorized into two main pillars. The overall development objectives are to support macroeconomic stability by strengthening fiscal management, and to promote economic growth by increasing agricultural productivity and competitiveness, alongside strengthening the power sector's financial viability and production capacity. The activity would be considered an overall success if the following specific targets for 2019 are achieved:1.  **Strengthening Fiscal Management:**    *   Increase tax revenue to 14.5 percent of GDP (from a 2014-16 average of 13.9 percent).    *   Process 70 percent of non-custom taxes through banking or e-payment systems (from 0 percent in 2016).    *   Remove 70 percent of identified ghost workers from the civil service payroll (from 0 percent in 2016).    *   Process 80 percent of specific payments (pensions above CFAF 50,000, scholarships, and civil servants' benefits) through the banking system (from 0 percent in 2016).2.  **Increasing Agricultural Productivity and Competitiveness, and Strengthening the Power Sector's Financial Viability and Production Capacity:**    *   **Agriculture:**        *   Improve the average quantity of processed cashew to 58 kg/ton (from 55 kg/ton), pineapple juice to 285 kg/ton (from 270 kg/ton), and milled rice to 230 kg/ton (from 220 kg/ton) per ton produced.        *   Expand irrigated land to 20,600 ha (from 20,000 ha in 2017).        *   Ensure a higher proportion of cashew samples (96% from 90%) and pineapple samples (93% from 85%) meet international standards when tested by LCSSA.    *   **Power Sector:**        *   Reduce SBEE's (Société Béninoise d'Energie Electrique) arrears with CEB to CFAF 10 billion (from CFAF 39.2 billion in 2017).        *   Increase SBEE's revenue collection rate from public sector institutions to 70 percent (from 5 percent in 2016).        *   Establish a legal framework supporting private sector participation in the power sector (from no existing framework in 2016 to one in place)."

"The targeted outcomes of the First Programmatic Energy and Water Sector Reforms DPL are to: 1) Achieve 100% electricity tariff cost recovery by 2017; 2) Implement a Debt Management Plan for NEPCO to meet 2017 debt reduction targets; 3) Achieve 100% operation and maintenance water cost recovery by 2017. Additionally, the activity aims to: 4) Increase the share of natural gas in fossil fuel power generation from 7% (2014) to 70% by 2017; 5) Boost renewable power in the generation mix from 0% (2014) to 10% by 2017; and 6) Reduce electricity distribution network losses by 2017, with a multi-year loss reduction target achieved. The activity would be considered an overall success if these targets are met, leading to significant financial benefits (up to JD1.7 billion in revenues/cost savings in electricity, reduced fiscal burden, fiscal space for pro-poor programs), environmental benefits (23.5 million tons CO2 reduction), sustained economic growth, and alignment with Jordan's 2025 Vision."

"Targeted outcomes: Increased agricultural profitability and improved equitable access to higher-quality water for ~140,000 small-scale farmers across 80,000 ha. Success requires: 30% reduction in irrigation operating costs and farmer time/labor, 70% client satisfaction with agricultural services, 75% water availability equity at tail and head ends, improved irrigation water quality, increased agricultural output, reduced yield differences, 1000 strengthened operational water user associations, 7500 client training days, 15 demonstrated technologies, and 100 feddan for higher-value horticultural crops."

"The targeted outcomes for the Niger Second DPO Series are to foster rural growth, achieved by end-December 2019. This involves: increasing rural productivity growth (20,000 households with subsidized inputs, 25,000 farmers with e-extension, 7 financial institutions offering leasing/warrantage, 60,000 artisanal miners with permits, 10% reduction in government transfer arrears); and supporting growth-enabling sectors (25% localities with 3G+ telecom, NIGELEC EBITDA CFAF 5.000B, 14.9% tax revenue/GDP, 100% fuel levy to Road Maintenance Fund). Overall success requires meeting these specific targets."

"The overall targeted outcomes of the Nigeria - Public/Private Partnership Program are to increase private investment in Nigeria's core infrastructure sectors (transport, power, and urban development), address infrastructure constraints, enhance firm productivity, and foster employment-generating growth. Specific outcomes include establishing effective institutional and technical mechanisms and instruments for PPP project origination and development. Phase I aims to achieve five transactions reaching commercial close, supported by fully-staffed PPP Project Management Teams in lead Ministries, signed MOUs between line ministries and the ICRC, and documented deliberations of FGN PPP coordinating arrangements.
