Overall success for the program will be measured by a minimum of three PPP projects reaching financial close and a 50% increase in private investment realized in the PPP market over the project's life, targeting US$1 billion from an identified pipeline of US$5.13 billion."

"The activity aims to foster inclusive growth and support pro-poor decentralized transfers and social protection. Key outcomes include: 80% functional land commissions; average domestic call tariff reduced to CFAF 90; 16 rural localities benefiting from telecom extension; microfinance non-performing loans ratio at 4.5%; 6,000 users with smart meters; 25% of regional budgetary resources allocated to the three poorest regions; over 100,000 agricultural e-voucher beneficiaries; and 150,000 households receiving cash transfers. Success hinges on achieving all these targets by June 2019, alongside good governance and macroeconomic stability."

"Targeted outcomes: Improve Niger River's economic/environmental services for NID population/ecosystems. Demonstrate effective navigation/port services, feasible environmental restoration, improved livelihoods, and enhanced crisis response. Key results: removed navigability barriers (dredging), safer/more reliable IWT (infrastructure/regulations), sustainable IWT (maintenance), decreased sedimentation, protected flora/fauna, efficient fishing/agriculture, specific rehabilitation (e.g., 136ha rice, 2051ha bourgoutieres). Success: vessel speed Diafarabé-Macina 14->30km/hr; 50ha NID restored via lateral channels; 12 villages access improved livelihoods; port traffic (passengers) 1,000->10,000. Requires community monitoring, capacity building, and acts as catalyst for further development."

"Targeted outcomes include demonstrating viable utility-scale private solar power, reducing fossil fuel dependence, promoting renewable energy, and helping Indonesia achieve sustainable development goals. Specifically, increasing renewable energy's share to 23% by 2025, curbing greenhouse gas emissions by 29% by 2030, and enhancing quality of life are key. The project aims to annually contribute 61 GWh of solar power, avoiding 41,400 tons of CO2 emissions, and install 42 MW capacity. Success requires achieving these goals, generating local employment, stimulating economic growth, improving women's skills, and demonstrating long-term financial sustainability by amortizing high upfront costs."

"The activity targets enhancing competitiveness and disaster resilience. Key outcomes for competitiveness include improving mobile internet penetration (>57% unique subscribers, >$3.0B investment), reducing business start-up time (<20 days, <8 procedures), streamlining customs clearance (8 days), narrowing the domestic-international rice price gap (<50%), and increasing non-rice agriculture GDP share (>83%). These aim to improve food security and reduce malnutrition. For resilience, outcomes include boosting excise tax revenue (>2.9% of GDP), improving disaster financial mechanisms (>4), increasing public assets in registries (>60%), and enhancing social program delivery/digital payments via foundational ID use and electronic payment growth (>15%). Success is measured by achieving these specific 2022 targets, along with a 3.1% cut in child malnutrition and 15.4% in chronic hunger by 2025."

"The activity targets improving water and sanitation service levels, reducing open defecation, and strengthening service delivery arrangements in targeted Punjab villages, benefiting ~5 million rural residents. Key outcomes include: upgrading service standards in ~570 villages, providing 650,000 household water connections, constructing 625,000 toilets for Open Defecation Free (ODF) status, supporting sewerage schemes in 315 villages, and improving water quality in 271 affected villages. Success depends on 6,556 villages achieving higher service levels under Gram Panchayat Water & Sanitation Committee (GPWSC) management and 5,841 GPWSCs managing O&M of water schemes with full cost recovery, alongside reduced disease incidence and time saved from water collection."

"[\"The targeted outcomes of the VIETNAM WATER RESOURCES ASSISTANCE activity are to foster agricultural diversification and productivity, raise farm household incomes, and reduce rural poverty through irrigation system modernization; establish effective dam safety management systems to lower associated risks; and promote environmentally sustainable water resource development and management in the Thu Bon River Basin.\", \"To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve: a serviced area of each of the six irrigation schemes reaching 75% of its design command area, with comprehensive management plans implemented and water user groups covering 25% of each scheme area with volumetric service contracts. Additionally, a functional Dam Safety Unit must be established in MARD, remedial safety works implemented on at least 3 dams, dam safety audits completed for 9 dams (6 in Component 1 and 3 others), and dam safety instrumentation for Hoa Binh Dam improved. River flows to Da Nang City should be restored, at least two feasibility studies for water resources infrastructure prepared, and a River Basin Coordination Committee established.\"]"

"The targeted outcome is improved performance of participating PNPs in delivering sustainable water supply and sanitation services. Success by 2021 requires: 155,000 urban residents accessing reliable, quality piped water; 160,000 urban residents accessing improved sanitation; Non-Revenue Water in five provincial capital systems reduced to \"20%; all participating PNPs achieving full cost recovery with arrears <90 days; and at least two PNPs accessing external financing due to improved creditworthiness. The ultimate impact is expanded access to quality, reliable water and sanitation for urban Lao PDR."

"The ReNew Clean Energy Project targets increasing renewable energy capacity, generating 1,400 GWh of clean energy, and avoiding 1.2 million tons of CO2 emissions annually from 2018. It aims to develop and commission 709 MW of capacity (398 MW solar by 2018, 311 MW wind by 2017) and create local employment (6,000 construction jobs, 200 operational jobs) and Rs28 billion in local purchases. Success depends on achieving these targets, adhering to regulatory and financing terms, effective hedging, and coordination with cofinanciers, ensuring satisfactory operation."

"Targeted outcomes include: reducing PM2.5 by 5% at 7+ monitoring stations by 2018; providing 8.5M rural people with improved water sources by 2018; increasing treated wastewater reuse from the hydrocarbon sector by 20% by 2018; 916 municipalities using compliant sanitary landfills by 2018; achieving 20% recyclable solid waste not landfilled by 2018; completing an Emissions and Pollutants Registry model; producing 4 forest land use change early-warning reports/year; establishing 5 Forest for Peace/PES projects; updating air quality norms; increasing diesel biofuel to 9%; and accelerating climate change mitigation and adaptation, improving population health and environmental quality."

"Targeted outcomes include achieving 40% rural water supply coverage by 2004, providing safe water/sanitation to 828,000 people, improving water supply in 1640 villages/2 piped schemes, and sanitation for 50% of target populations. This involves constructing/rehabilitating 2240 water supply and 430 sanitation facilities, increasing hygiene awareness, and building local capacity. Success requires sustained sector reforms, beneficiary hygiene, effective institutional changes, community participation, timely funds, and proper facility maintenance."

"{\"outcomes\":[{\"description\":\"Developing a national standard system for biodiversity conservation in China and establishing a group standard/guideline on the raw material and production areas of alpine plant diversity conservation for cosmetic products manufacturing enterprises.\",\"success_criteria\":\"A comprehensive research report on national standard system of biodiversity conservation in China is provided; an authoritative group standard/guideline on raw material and production areas for alpine plant diversity conservation for cosmetic products manufacturing enterprises in China is officially issued.\"},{\"description\":\"Piloting community-based biodiversity management and conservation and ABS action models by demonstration at specific sites.\",\"success_criteria\":\"Restoration of a sustainable village-based pattern of medicine plant resources management, utilization, and conservation in Manyuan village, including protection of Dai traditional medicine culture and strengthening participatory conservation; an ABS management model for A. buchneroides intercropping with other cash crops is established over at least 3,335 m\b\b(0.33 ha) in Daka Hani village, leading to a significant increase in local community income; an effective management model for cash crops intercropping cultivation under forest is established at YAFG arboretum over 13,340 m\b\b(1.33 ha).\"},{\"description\":\"Supporting AI technology-based intelligent monitoring in environmental protection and biodiversity management to promote the mainstreaming of biodiversity and ecological conservation in Yunnan Province.\",\"success_criteria\":\"Technical support is provided for AI technology research and application through consultancy; big data management and establishment of species information databases are supported; intelligent monitoring of biodiversity and forest resources management in China is promoted and provides example reference.\"},{\"description\":\"Improvement of capacity building and public awareness in China.\",\"success_criteria\":\"Three trainings on community-based biodiversity conservation and ABS resource management are provided to farmers, researchers, local government officers, and enterprise representatives; concept, experiences, and new technologies of biodiversity conservation and ecological maintenance are disseminated through diverse media; a successful workshop and exhibition with over 50 participants is held at COP 15 conference to share project achievements.\"}]}"

"The activity aims for universal water/sanitation access, \"world-class\" cities, water security, climate resilience, and enhanced livability in at least 10 cities. Overall success by 2027 requires: 100% sewage collection/treatment in 8 cities (from 7.6%), 100% flood risk reduction in 3 cities (from 0), water supply 6+ hrs/day for 100% households in 4 cities (from 48%), NRW reduced to 20% in 4 cities (from 30%), 3,400 tons CO2e avoided (from 0), 52,000 m3/day treated wastewater reuse (from 32k), and 15% increase in sewerage user fee collection (from 49%)."

"Targeted outcomes include: financing two renewable power projects (350 MW capacity) and establishing a comprehensive EVCN; achieving an annual GHG reduction of 53,250 tons; the loan being Thailand's first certified climate loan; supporting Thailand's renewable energy sector and transition to an EV industry; increasing green loan awareness; supporting Energy Absolute amid COVID-19 disruptions; and improving gender equality. Success requires: EVCN completion; climate loan certification and verification; financing existing renewable projects; improved gender equality; and operational performance monitoring."

"The targeted outcomes of the National Agriculture Development Program are to improve agriculture productivity and market access for smallholder farmers in selected regions, strengthen public sector capacity to respond to eligible agriculture sector emergencies, and support the establishment of more accessible land registration mechanisms, including customary land certificates and collective titles. This includes increasing smallholder assets and production, facilitating market access and inclusion into agriculture supply chains, and investing in agriculture public goods and services at national and local levels."

"The targeted outcomes are: increased private sector renewable energy capacity, annual generation of 1,200 GWh clean energy, avoidance of 1,185,165 tons CO2 annually (both from 2018), creation of at least 64 full-time equivalent local jobs and 415 contractual jobs for operations, and an average annual corporate tax contribution of Rs890 million ($13 million equivalent) to government revenues. Overall success requires commissioning 476 MW wind and 100 MW solar capacity by April 2017, achieving these generation, emissions, job, and tax targets, diversifying India's energy mix, expanding private sector investment, and MEIL attaining sustainable competitiveness."

"[\"The targeted outcomes are to improve the integration and increase utilization of renewable energy (RE) in China through battery storage systems and innovative RE technology applications. This includes increasing RE absorption by the grid, increasing energy production from innovative RE technology applications, and reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. The activity would be considered an overall success if it achieves these PDO-level indicators. Further indicators of success include increased installed capacity of battery storage systems and DRE, a healthy co-financing leverage ratio, high beneficiary satisfaction, and low non-performing loan levels for sub-loans to SMEs.\", \"NO RESPONSE\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the TN-Tunis West Sewerage activity are to improve sanitation service quality in Greater Tunis, promote sustainable and significant reuse of treated wastewater for irrigation, and enhance ONAS's financial and operational management capacity. Success would be measured by: the volume of sewage collected and treated in Greater Tunis; the number of new connections in areas like Sidi-Hassine and El Agba; the proportion of yearly investments allocated to maintenance/rehabilitation works; the volume of treated sewage effluent available for reuse in El Attar irrigation areas; client satisfaction rates; and improved financial performance metrics for ONAS."

"Targeted outcomes include transforming Dongsun into an environment-friendly, inclusive, and livable urban area by improving urban infrastructure (river rehabilitation, flood control, roads, social facilities), enhancing the urban environment (reduced pollution, better waste management), and generating economic benefits (cost savings, higher land values, increased income, jobs). Social impacts include improved living conditions, poverty reduction, stronger community organization, and gender equity. Capacity building ensures sustainability."

"The Hebei Air Pollution Prevention and Control Program targets reducing specific air pollutant emissions in key Hebei sectors, aiming for: SO2 reduction by 150,000 tons, NOx reduction from industrial enterprises by 160,000 tons and from transport by 40,000 tons, and PM2.5 reduction by 1,300 tons via 800,000 clean stoves. Success requires achieving these emission reductions, strengthening the CEM system (12 EPBs implementing protocols, 100% enterprise integration), deploying 800,000 clean stoves, replacing 4,500 diesel buses with clean energy buses, establishing a smart environmental monitoring platform, and contributing to the HAP's 25% PM2.5 reduction target."

"The activity targets expanding rooftop solar coverage in urban areas, increasing installed capacity by 36% from the existing 191.8 MW, generating approximately 88.08 GWh/year of electricity, and reducing about 54,347 tons of CO2e emissions annually. It also aims to demonstrate rooftop solar viability and sustainability, generate gender-sensitive local employment, support gender equality in BEI operations, and attract Philippine commercial banks to provide longer-tenor loans for rooftop solar, thereby reducing perceived risk. Overall success would be achieved by meeting these capacity, generation, and emission targets, demonstrating sustained gender inclusion, and observing a catalytic effect where local banks increase participation and the private sector replicates such projects, coupled with environmental and social compliance."

"Targeted outcomes aim to enhance COVID-19 response and support post-crisis recovery in Seychelles. Success metrics include: daily COVID-19 testing capacity reaching 700 and 95% of international arrivals tested by Dec 2020; a 20% increase in households receiving social protection by June 2020; 25,000 private sector workers benefiting from salary guarantees by Dec 2020, 90% of firms benefiting from tax postponement by Aug 2020, and 80% uptake of MSME credit line by June 2021. Additionally, 80% of legal entities submitting verifiable beneficial ownership info by June 2021 and 10 government sectors guided on mainstreaming climate change by June 2021 will signify success."

"The targeted outcome of the Upper Trishuli-1 Hydropower Project is the increased delivery of clean power to the domestic grid. To be considered an overall success by 2026, the activity must: increase electricity delivered to the off-taker to 1,282 gigawatt hours per year, avoid 446,000 tons of CO2 emissions annually, create at least 65 operations jobs (10 for women), and generate over $3.3 million in annual domestic purchases. By 2025, outputs contributing to success include: installing 216 MW hydropower capacity and 1.2 km of 220 kV transmission lines, providing free electricity (20 kWh/month) to all households within a 500-meter radius (including women-headed households), and creating at least 1,090 construction jobs (200 for women)."

"[\"The project targets increased utilization of the Gan River for more reliable freight transport and cleaner energy production, promoting 'clean' economic development in the basin by optimizing water resources for environmentally-friendly transport and renewable electricity production.\",\"Targeted outcomes include reducing trade logistics costs for communities in the Gan River corridor, facilitating direct container barge services to Shanghai for manufactured goods, and meeting the heavy demand for bulk transport of coal and oil products.\",\"The activity aims to contribute to Jiangxi Province's power demand through hydropower generation, improve flood control, and enhance inland waterway transport capacity and service levels for primary and secondary industries in the Nanchang-Ganzhou corridor.\",\"Additional outcomes involve maximizing benefits of navigation, power generation, and flood protection via optimal Shihutang Complex reservoir operation, and addressing insufficient water depth in the 17km river section to enable 1000-dwt vessel navigation.\",\"The project also targets enhancing sustainable management and strengthening institutional and technical capacities for IWT management, waterway development, maintenance, construction management, hydropower plant operation, and cumulative impacts at watershed level for JANA/JPMO.\",\"Overall success requires an increase in the volume of cargo along the Gan River to 131,600 (1,000 Tons) and containers to 237,000 (TEU) by 2013. Additionally, cargo passing the Shihutang ship lock during low water season must reach 1,031 (1,000 Tons) by 2013.\",\"Success in navigation further requires increasing navigable time for 1,000-ton vessels on the 38km upgraded channel to 95% of navigable time per year by 2012. Also, measures identified for treating the 17km river channel below Shihutang Dam must be approved and accepted by JANA.\",\"For hydropower and environmental aspects, success means an annual output of approximately 526.5 GWh of electricity from a 120 MW powerhouse (reaching 472.41 GWh by 2013 according to monitoring targets), coupled with a reduction of carbon emissions by 446.17 (1,000 T CO2 reduced) by 2013.\",\"Success in flood protection entails safeguarding 4,389 hectares of cropland against a 10-year flood by 2011. Institutional capacity building success is achieved when optimal operation solutions for the Shihutang Complex are developed and adopted by JANA by 2011/2012, and 28 trainees adopt learnt international and domestic experiences by 2013.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes are to improve energy consumption performance in public sector buildings, generating energy and financial savings, and providing better energy services. The project also aims to enhance sustainable economic growth by reducing demand for high-priced electricity imports and providing a demonstrated basis for a sustainable energy efficiency improvement program in the public sector. Overall success will be achieved through quantified energy savings, increased end-user satisfaction via surveys, and the number of public sector buildings retrofitted, alongside the development of an energy efficiency policy, regulatory framework, and institutional structure to support long-term sustainable programs."

"The targeted outcomes for the Vietnam Managing Natural Hazards Project are: protecting 50,000 hectares of land through enhanced flood protection measures; protecting 550,000 people through enhanced priority infrastructure built by the project; covering 100 communes with storm and flood early warning services and increased awareness of emergency management; and having 10 provincial river basin plans updated or prepared that integrate Disaster Risk Management (DRM) and are approved. To consider the activity an overall success, all these target values must be met."

"The targeted outcomes of the Mali Energy Support Project include: increasing the number of households connected to electricity by EDM SA in Bamako from 252,000 to 382,000; increasing connections in other targeted areas of the country from 131,000 to 182,000 households; reducing technical losses from 12 percent to 10 percent; reducing non-technical losses from 7 percent to 5 percent; increasing the use of compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs) by households from zero to 1,000,000; increasing the installation of efficient lamps for public lighting from zero to 15,000; and achieving a peak load reduction from energy efficiency investments of 20 MW. The activity would be considered an overall success upon the achievement of these specific targets."

"The activity targets increasing agricultural productivity by 50%, smallholder net incomes by 30%, and cropping intensity by 30%. It aims to sustainably increase incomes for approximately 196,550 poor rural households, enhance smallholder productivity and crop diversification, and improve rural infrastructure and marketing. Success requires effectively serving about 2,600 hectares via improved irrigation (benefiting 10,600 households), constructing 400 water harvesting structures (benefiting 5,100 households), benefiting 191,000 households from employment/asset creation and 24,000 from FSLF for post-harvest/marketing, and building capacity for at least 7,000 farm households. Overall success also entails at least a tenth of assisted households moving above the poverty line by project closing, and strengthened national irrigation development capacity."

"The HN Rural Infrastructure Project primarily targets two outcomes: (i) to improve access, quality, and sustainability of infrastructure services (roads, water & sanitation, electricity) for the rural poor in Honduras, and (ii) to develop capacities and an enabling environment for locally-driven service provision and planning. These outcomes are specifically measured by key indicators such as kilometers of roads passable, percentage of population with improved access to water/sanitation (with cost recovery) and electricity (with quality/sustainability), and strengthened local capacity shown by UTIs with trained staff/budget and successful procurement."

"The Rantau Dedap Geothermal Power Project (Phase 2) targets expanding geothermal power generation in South Sumatra. The intended outcome, to be achieved by 2021, is the generation and delivery of 702.5 gigawatt-hours of electricity per year to the offtaker, alongside annual emission reductions of 403,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent, and the provision of at least 100 jobs during operation. Key outputs by 2021 include a total installed electricity generation capacity of 90.9 MW, cumulative employment of 1,000 jobs during construction, $200 million in total domestic purchases during construction and early operation, and at least $20 million in payments to the government. Economic opportunities for women are targeted, with at least 30% women participants in training, five women's groups receiving technical/financial support for livelihood and skill development in at least five project villages, and five women entrepreneurs supported to supply project requirements. The activity will be considered a success if these targets are met, ensuring no measurable adverse impact on critical habitat or reduction in endangered/critically endangered species populations through BAP implementation, full compensation and opportunities for project-affected households, adherence to national labor laws and core labor standards, and successful amortization of capital costs for long-term sustainability."

"Targeted outcomes: sustainable climate-resilient road infrastructure on Dili-Ainaro corridor, reduced rainfall impact, improved drainage/slope protection, adequate maintenance, multiyear performance-based maintenance/emergency contracts for Aileu/Ainaro, and a pilot emergency planning/response system. Success needs: 1) Dili-Ainaro drainage 50% good (from 20%). 2) Dili-Ainaro good/fair condition 50% (from 10%), reduced roughness. 3) 30% traffic increase. 4) 30% fewer emergency repairs. 5) 50% Aileu/Ainaro national roads under maintenance/emergency contracts. 6) Functional pilot emergency system with 80% response time success."

"The activity targets increased renewable energy utilization, reliable/cleaner electricity supply, and decreased generation costs by replacing diesel (>$0.50/kWh) with cheaper solar (~$0.40/kWh), reducing CO2 emissions, and improving supply for ~7,300 beneficiaries. Success requires: reducing diesel importation by 0.9 million liters/year by June 2020; increasing renewable energy to 78% in five provincial grids by Dec 2020; cutting CO2 by 840 tons/year by Dec 2020; installing 2 MW solar plants generating 3.1 GWh/year by Dec 2020; and training 10 Solomon Power staff (20% women) in plant operation by Dec 2020."

"[\"The activity aims to safeguard Albania's major hydroelectric dams, preventing catastrophic dam failures, significant loss of life, property damage, prolonged falls in hydropower production, increased electricity prices across the SEE region, and disproportionate negative impacts on vulnerable populations.\",\"It also targets improving the operational efficiency of the dams and enhancing the stability of power supply for the regional electricity market.\",\"A key outcome is to maximize Albania's hydropower benefits through improved operational practices of existing facilities and enabling more effective participation in the regional electricity market.\",\"The activity intends to promote private sector investment in hydropower by collecting, organizing, and making available better data and studies on the country's hydropower potential.\",\"Overall success would require that all major hydroelectric dams and their generation capacity are safeguarded.\",\"Increased electricity output due to reduced water leakages and equipment rehabilitation must be achieved.\",\"The value of electricity output should increase as a result of improved reservoir optimization procedures, leading to reduced power system supply costs.\",\"Maintenance of system load frequency variations within international norms is essential for success.\",\"KESH must prepare financial statements in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), ideally resulting in unqualified audit reports.\",\"Success is also contingent on hydropower development studies leading to competitions for the award of concessions for projects judged to be economic.\",\"Improved monitoring and alarm systems for dam safety must be implemented, and the institutional capacity for dam safety within KESH and AlbCOLD must be strengthened, including the preparation of an emergency preparedness plan and the establishment of a 'Safety of Dams culture'.\",\"The unbundling of KESH Gen is also a key indicator for overall success.\"]"

"The activity targets improved urban service quality, coverage, efficiency, and sustainability in Madhya Pradesh by 2028. Key outcomes include: 90% continuous piped water supply coverage for 485,000 households in 128 ULBs (25% vulnerable/BPL/women-headed), reducing women's water fetching time to 10 minutes, 70% decentralized sewage management in 10 ULBs, 90% water quality compliance, 50% reduction in waterlogging/flooding in 10 ULBs, nonrevenue water below 15%, and 80% O&M cost recovery. Infrastructure targets: 148-180 MLD water treatment, 3,100-3,800 km pipelines, 185,000-300,000 metered household connections, 7 sewage/septage treatment plants (45 MLD), and 880 km sewer/storm networks. Institutional outcomes: GIS for O&M in 128 ULBs, training 800 staff/representatives (O&M, sanitation, GESI), operational IT systems, water/sanitation safety plans in all ULBs, 128 customer service centers, 100% volumetric water billing, 100 women internships, and 240,000 households reached by awareness."

"[\"The targeted outcomes of the Golovnaya Hydropower Plant Rehabilitation Project - Additional Financing include increasing the operational efficiency of Golovnaya HPP to a weighted average generation efficiency of 91% by 2026, and rehabilitating electric power generation equipment by commissioning Generation unit 4 by 2026. It also aims to increase the plant's installed generation capacity from 225 MW to 274 MW by 2026, contributing an additional 49 MW and 106.9 GWh annually. These efforts support meeting increasing domestic and external power demand and modernizing the HPP.\",\"Further outcomes include enhancing the capacity of Barki Tojik staff and energy faculty students through training for 20 staff and on-site demonstrations for 25 students (at least 30% female) by 2026. The project also targets contributing to climate mitigation through renewable energy generation, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and fostering regional cooperation and integration via enhanced energy trade, aligning with national development strategies and ADB's operational priorities.\",\"For the activity to be considered an overall success: the weighted average generation efficiency of Golovnaya HPP must increase from 83% (2012 baseline) to 91% by 2026. Additionally, Generation unit 4 of the Golovnaya hydropower plant needs to be commissioned by 2026, leading to an increase in the plant's installed generation capacity from 240 MW (2012 baseline) to 274 MW by 2026, thereby adding 49 MW of available capacity and 106.9 GWh to annual generation.\",\"Success also requires that capacity building training on HPP operation and maintenance is conducted for 20 Barki Tojik staff (at least 30% female) by 2026, and an on-site demonstration is provided for 25 students (at least 30% female) from energy faculties by 2026. Critical assumptions for success include the effective implementation of Barki Tojik corporate restructuring, achieving efficiency in utility operations, receiving qualified bids for the turnkey contract, and ensuring adequate female participation in training and demonstrations.\"]"

"The activity targets improving water supply and sanitation services for 300,000 consumers in La Guajira's urban and peri-urban areas. Key outcomes by 2011 include 90% urban household water access with 22 hours/day service, 72% urban sewerage connection, reducing unaccounted-for-water to 40%, 63% revenue collection, 82% metered connections, 11 rural pilot systems implemented, and established institutional capacity. Success requires achieving these specific targets, maintaining economic viability (US$54M net benefits, 19% IRR), and ensuring service sustainability through effective utility management and reforms."

"Targeted outcomes by 2024 include 124,000 households with uninterrupted water, nonrevenue water reduced to 25%, direct wastewater discharge cut to 25,000 m
/day, and seasonal flooding reduced for 18,000 households. Water/sanitation budgets will be ring-fenced. Overall success by 2023 requires: water production capacity at 183,000 m
/day, 135km new/rehabilitated water networks, 20,000 connections replaced; 58km new sewerage/interceptor networks, 75,000 m
/day wastewater treatment, 10 septage vehicles, 60km dredged canals, and stormwater pumping to 65,100 m
/hour. Additionally, 50 staff must be trained (30% women) in urban management, and 30% of unskilled civil labor must be women."

"The activity targets strengthening local government authorities' capabilities for responsive, accountable service delivery and supporting economic infrastructure development in Northern, North Central, Eastern, and Uva provinces. This aims to improve living standards, promote inclusion, strengthen governance, and advance gender equality, benefiting approximately 1 million vulnerable people. Overall success requires: 5 timely Basic Transfers disbursed; 35% of LGAs improving service delivery performance; 67% of LGAs implementing participatory planning; 50% of LGA spending aligning with community needs; 4 economic infrastructure sub-projects benefiting multiple LGAs; 1,000,000 beneficiaries (500,000 female) benefiting; 50% of respondents satisfied with LGA sub-projects; regularization of Social Audit Committees with 30% women chairpersons; and the establishment of a geo-referenced M&E system and a Performance Transfer system."

"The activity targets building human and technical capacity in state agencies for environmentally sound site remediation and developing a framework for a National Program for Rehabilitation of Polluted Sites (NPRPS). Key outcomes: development of NPRPS methodological framework (site inventory, best practices, cost recovery); establishment of functional ECACs in West Bengal (by year 2) and Andhra Pradesh (by year 4); water/soil quality at pilot sites complying with national standards with long-term monitoring; piloting cleanup technologies at orphan hazardous waste/dumpsites; strengthened technical staff capacity and remediation guidelines; minimized environmental/health risks; and realizing social, environmental, and economic benefits like reduced pollution exposure, improved water, employment, and property value appreciation."

"The targeted outcomes include: reducing road transport costs and improving road safety along the upgraded section, strengthening the capacity of the Roads Department (RD) and the Ministry of Regional Development and Infrastructure (MRDI) in planning, managing the road network, and improving traffic safety. Success will be achieved when transit time from Agara to Zemo Osiauri reduces to 7 minutes; vehicle operating costs for cars/trucks decrease to $0.18/$0.72 per km respectively; road fatalities decrease to 8.7; East-West Highway asset value increases to GEL 2,300 million; and a road sector strategy with social/gender dimensions is adopted by RD/MRDI."

"[\"The project targets increased access to safe water and improved sanitation in urban areas, enhanced efficiency, climate resilience, and sustainability in greater Honiara (GHA) and five other urban towns. Specific outcomes include: continuous safe water supply through infrastructure upgrades (e.g., new water mains, reservoirs, treatment plant for Kongulai Spring); effective urban sanitation services via rehabilitated/new outfalls and pump stations, and a new septage treatment facility; enhanced hygiene and water conservation awareness; strengthened SIWA financial and technical sustainability; and improved management of Honiara's drinking water source areas to build climate resilience (e.g., source protection plans, revegetation, legislative reviews, landowner engagement).\",\"The activity will be considered an overall success upon achieving all specific performance indicators and milestones by project closure. Key success metrics include: 5 MLD increase in treated water for GHA; 75 km new water mains and 15 km upgraded; Kongulai Spring disruptions reduced to \b30 days annually; 3 ocean outfalls rehabilitated, 1 new, 6 new sewer pump stations, 2 rehabilitated, and a 60 m3/day septage treatment facility; 7 km new sewer pipelines; 10,000 persons reached by hygiene awareness (70% women); 60+ hectares of water source areas revegetated; landowner groups established for water source protection with >=50% women participation; and SIWA financial/technical sustainability via reviewed tariffs and staff skill updates.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes focus on sustainable development, environmental management, and biodiversity protection in Burundi. This includes reducing deforestation, GHG emissions, and biodiversity loss, while improving community climate resilience through watershed protection, land restoration, clean energy, and sustainable agriculture. Success will be considered achieved when: 5,000 ha of protected areas are managed; 20 women participate in resource decisions; 1,000 ha are developed; 10,000 km of protected lines are achieved; 11M forest/agroforestry, 0.5M bamboo, and 3M fruit trees are planted; 200 people trained; 100 carbonization demos; 10,000 improved stoves distributed; and 3 gender-sensitive resource management solutions are in place."

"The targeted outcomes of the Golovnaya 240-Megawatt Hydropower Plant Rehabilitation Project include an increased supply of renewable energy to national and regional power systems, aiming to increase hydro generation supply from 743 GWh in 2012 to 1,130 GWh in 2026. The project also targets increased operational efficiency of the Golovnaya hydropower plant, with the weighted average generation efficiency rising from 83% in 2012 to 89% in 2020. Additionally, the project's output involves the rehabilitation of electric power generation and transmission equipment, leading to the plant being commissioned in 2020 and its installed generation capacity increasing from 240 MW in 2012 to 252 MW in 2020.To consider the activity an overall success, the government's continued support for regional energy trade is needed, Barki Tojik's corporate restructuring must be effectively implemented, achieving utility operations efficiency, and qualified bids for the turnkey contract must be received in a timely manner. Effective mitigation of identified risks, such as equipment transportation issues, manufacturing fit, project implementation delays, staffing deficiencies, weak financial management, and operational challenges, is also crucial."

"The activity targets include ensuring key infrastructure assets perform satisfactorily with sustainable resilience to natural risks, and that oversight and management of infrastructure, natural resources, and national emergency are effective and efficient with private sector and stakeholder participation. This involves consolidated air transport infrastructure, restructured land transport and road systems, enhanced access/capacity/safety for remote communities, restructured environmental/natural resource/disaster management, and sustainable protection of coastal assets. Success criteria include: all primary road, international airport, and public coastal infrastructure under asset and information management; at least 60% of road and coastal infrastructure developments justified by appraisal; at least 80% of coastal communities participating in CIMS; 80% cost recovery for aviation (SAA) and primary roads (MWTI) preservation; MWTI fully operational and staffed; and 80% of coastline on Upolu and Savai'i protected for 20-year storm events."

"[\"Targeted outcomes: By 2020, Cihu, Qingshan, Qinggang lake water quality improves to Class IV for organic pollutants; urban household average annual per capita income increases by 10%; unemployment reduced to no more than 5.0%. By 2018, Cihu's chemical oxygen demand reduced to 3,400 tons and phosphorus discharge to 70 tons. Wastewater collection coverage increases to 85%, and collection rate to 80%. Sewage sludge treatment reaches 100%, and solid waste collection to 95%. Project also targets creating 1,823 jobs during implementation and 593 during operation, with 30% each for women and the poor.\",\"Overall success requires effective implementation of environmental protection and urban plans under the Twelfth Five-Year Plan, continued government commitment and support for infrastructure development, and effective enforcement of national and local environmental laws. Additionally, 100 tons/day of sewage sludge must be treated in the Huaxin cement factory. Actual development in Hexi needs to align with the city master plan, lake rehabilitation works implemented in proper sequence, and community participation must be active. Ensuring stable sewage sludge quantity/quality and strong authority support for capacity development are also crucial.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include enhanced financial literacy for members/managers, increased client memberships, a 4% PAR 30 for the portfolio, improved cost control via mutualization, strengthened liquidity management with a treasury plan, better client adaptation, and institutional memory. The activity also aims to develop a database of existing and potential savings/credit products, update credit procedures, and ensure timely credit repayments. Success is achieved when these specific targets are met, signifying improved financial inclusion, operational efficiency, and client satisfaction."

"The targeted outcomes of the BD Dhaka Water Supply and Sanitation Project are to improve the sustainable delivery of stormwater drainage, wastewater, and water services by the Dhaka Water Supply and Sewerage Authority (DWASA) to the population of Dhaka. This objective will be achieved by installing selected stormwater pumping stations and rehabilitating canals to minimize urban flooding, rehabilitating, repairing, and expanding priority investments in the city's sewerage network and treatment plant to improve the urban environment, and supporting DWASA's pilot expansion of water and sanitation services into selected Dhaka slums to increase services to the urban poor.  To consider the activity an overall success, the following specific targets must be met by Year 5: an increase of 180,000 people in project area ward slums having access to safe water and 218,000 people having access to improved sanitation. Additionally, 57,000 households should gain access to sewerage services, and there should be no flood for the 5-year, 2-days rainfall event in the eastern part of Dhaka. Key intermediate outcomes contributing to this success include the rehabilitation or extension of 280 km of sewage lines, collection and treatment of 120,000 m3/day of wastewater in Pagla, reduction of sewer blockages to 0.25/km, development or rehabilitation of 30 km of stormwater canals, an increase of 40 cum/sec in permanent stormwater pumping capacity, and the collection of 20,000 tons of solid waste along the canals. Furthermore, all entitled Project Affected Households (PAHs) must be fully compensated prior to relocation, and DWASA's annual statutory audits must demonstrate transparent and accountable financial management with no fiduciary issues regarding project funds."

"The targeted outcomes include maintaining/increasing electricity supply/reliability, reducing service costs, and enhancing institutional performance. Specific targets for 2007/2008: increase electricity consumption to 1,875 GWh; reduce undelivered energy to 8 GWh; achieve a 7% reduction in SENELEC's variable costs; decrease transmission/distribution losses to 15.5%. Institutional goals involve establishing a monitoring/evaluation system, a new tariff mechanism, staff training, and public/private partnerships for SENELEC, including commissioning a 67.5 MW Kounoune facility by 2006. Success requires meeting these targets, generating substantial net economic benefits, and achieving an EIRR of 23.25% and NPV of $415.97."

"Targeted outcomes: increase natural resource use efficiency and rainfed agriculture productivity. Specifics include: 25% increase in water discharge, 20% increase in biomass, 7,800 ha of rainfed land under irrigation, 50% (irrigated)/20% (rainfed) increase in crop productivity by YR7, 45,000 direct beneficiaries, rejuvenating 1,530 water sources, 30% reduction in soil loss/runoff, and afforestation of 21,734 ha (50% survival). Success implies achieving these and the projected Financial Rate of Return of 22.7% and Economic Rate of Return of 21.6%."

"Targeted outcomes include achieving a clean, green, healthy Bagmati River system by 2038, improving water security, reaching bathing water quality (DO > 3 mg/l at Pashupatinath) & reducing flood cost by 10% by 2020, and reducing wastewater by 17.3 MLD by 2026. Success requires establishing integrated river basin management, constructing 12.95 km of river corridor, restoring 23 monuments, forming community waste groups, developing 850,000 m³ storage (Dhap dam), harvesting 178,000 m³ rooftop water, stabilizing 12.5 ha erosion-prone land, and implementing a flood early warning system with 72-hour warnings."

"[\"Targeted outcomes include increasing water coverage from 86.1% (2013) to 100% by 2021 (benefiting 7M people), and sanitation coverage from 67.9% (2013) to 100% (benefiting 13M people). It aims to improve service efficiency, equity, sustainability, and environmental conditions, yielding US$4.4B (water) and US$8.4B (sanitation) in socioeconomic benefits, and a US$150M annual revenue increase for 50 EPSs with affordable services for the poor.\", \"Overall success would require achieving 100% water and sanitation coverage by 2021, providing services to 7M (water) and 13M (sanitation) people, realizing projected socioeconomic benefits and EPS revenue increases, and ensuring affordable services for the poor. Success also depends on strengthening institutional/regulatory frameworks, establishing environmentally sustainable management, and effective climate change adaptation, with 2016 targets (e.g., 80% water coverage) indicating progress.\"]"

"The Floating Solar Energy Project targets by 2023: supplying 63,138 MWh/year of clean electricity to the domestic grid, achieving 30,302 tCO2e annual emissions reductions, providing at least 10 operational jobs, and stimulating over $25M in annual domestic purchases during operation. Outputs include a 47.5 MW floating photovoltaic plant and 3.5 km of transmission lines, generating at least 40 construction jobs, and ensuring gender equality seminars for affected people. Success requires achieving these targets, along with conducting workplace training by 2019, securing at least $200k/year in government payments, and $25M in domestic purchases during construction/early operation, while complying with environmental/social safeguards and mitigating risks like regulatory changes, climate events, and cost overruns."

"[\"The activity targets include: an 11% increase in annual income for titled Sierra farmers and 15% for Selva farmers; increased annual yield per hectare for priority crops (e.g., Coffee to 1.64%, Rice to 2.24%, Quinoa to 2.84%, Potato to 1.35%, Hard yellow corn to 1.19%); increased use of agricultural inputs (e.g., Certified seed to 20%, Chemical fertilizers to 18%, Insecticides to 46%, Tractor to 29%); reduction of average titling cost to US$114/title (from US$341); reduction of titling timeframe to 4 months (from 18); 10 regional governments adopting a single titling process (from 0); enhanced legal security of rural land tenure via formalization in Selva and Sierra; strengthened institutional capacities of MINAGRI and regional governments; development of a technology platform for cadastre and registration; and fostering sustainable land use, poverty reduction, equity, and women's property rights.\",\"Overall success would require: achieving the targeted increases in farmer income (11% Sierra, 15% Selva) and crop yields (e.g., Coffee 1.64%, Rice 2.24%); meeting targets for increased agricultural input use (Certified seed 20%, Fertilizers 18%, Insecticides 46%, Tractor 29%); reducing titling costs to US$114/title and timeframe to 4 months; achieving 10 regional governments following a single titling process; successfully surveying and registering rural properties; developing and implementing the technology platform; strengthening institutional capacities and legal framework for titling; maintaining updated cadastres and registries for sustainability; effectively managing beneficiary expectations and sociocultural aspects; ensuring citizen participation; and achieving broader socio-environmental benefits like poverty reduction, equity, and women's property rights.\"]"

"[\"The activity targets strengthening CHT communities' capacity to shape decisions, addressing land issues, communal violence, and violence against women. It aims to improve governance, accountability, and legitimacy via strengthened local authorities and enhanced service delivery by Regional/Hill District Councils. A key goal is to support full Peace Accord implementation by strengthening institutions, decentralizing powers, making the Land Commission functional, resolving land disputes, and supporting army withdrawal. Expected outcomes include citizen demands for voice, development, and accountability being met by institutions providing universal basic services, alongside improved justice, human rights, inclusive economic growth, and environmental sustainability.\",\"Success means strengthened community land, resource, and livelihood management (e.g., 25-30% agri-yield increase, 90% improved farming skills, 80% improved living conditions, 5,000 active community committees, 15,000 households accessing public services, 2,000 skilled youths, 50% reduced climate-induced losses). Additionally, increased participation in decision-making, 60% of justice actors with increased knowledge, and 62 disputes resolved by mediation. Empowerment of women and girls is key, with targets including a 40% girls' ratio in schools, 400 gender-responsive schools, 60% women/girls claiming education rights, 200 upgraded gender-responsive infrastructures, and 500 girls in income-generating activities by 2023. Overall, citizen demands for voice/development/accountability are met, with an access to justice index target of 0.50, and 100 disaster-resilient habitats.\"]"

"The project targets improved HVC production, natural resources, women's status, and nutrition. Success by 2028: agriculture profitability $1,060/ha; 50% HH income rise (HVC/livestock); 3,200 women producing HVC/livestock; 223,000 ha rehabilitated; 60% repro-age women meet dietary diversity. By 2027: 18 market plans (45% women); 200 matching grants (50 for women); 4,200 ha dry season HVC; 9 PLUPs; 100% new land titles (both spouses); 18 modernized irrigation subprojects (4,200 ha dry/wet irrigated ag., 100 km roads); 120 WUGs/18 WUAs (25% female mgmt.); NNSPA in 6 districts/100 villages (25% women), WASH in 8 communities."

"[\"Achieve a primary fiscal surplus of 2% of GDP (0.5% after arrears) and an overall fiscal deficit below 1% of GDP (2.2% after arrears) by 2019. Increase total tax revenue to 19.8% of GDP and reduce tax exemptions to 2.1% of GDP by 2019. Increase property tax revenues to 0.68% of GDP by 2019. Decrease spending to 25.7% of GDP and rationalize the wage bill to 6.7% of GDP by 2019. Reduce public debt to 73.5% of GDP by end-2019. Improve public investment efficiency (PIMA rating from 30% to 60%) and lower pre-financed investment costs (interest 8% to 7%, maturity 3 to 7 years) by 2019. Improve CEET's financial viability, expand electricity access for poor households with usage-based tariffs, and help reduce electricity poverty. Enhance ICT sector competition, productivity, and job creation.\", \"The activity would be an overall success if the targeted primary fiscal surplus (2% GDP, 0.5% after arrears) and overall deficit (<1% GDP, 2.2% after arrears) are achieved by 2019. Public debt must be on a firm downward trajectory, reaching 73.5% GDP by end-2019, with a path to below 38% GDP by 2025, alongside prudent borrowing. Tax revenue must increase to 19.8% GDP, exemptions reduce to 2.1% GDP, and property tax revenue rise to 0.68% GDP by 2019. Spending must decline to 25.7% GDP, wage bill to 6.7% GDP, and public investment efficiency (PIMA) reach 60%. The electricity sector must achieve financial viability, expand access for poor households with welfare-improving tariffs, and reduce electricity poverty. The ICT sector must show improved service delivery, business productivity, and labor demand, fostering inclusive growth and long-term poverty reduction.\"]"

"Targeted Outcomes & Success Criteria: The activity aims to reduce unsustainable wildlife trade and biodiversity loss in Indonesia, leading to stable/increasing threatened species populations. Key targets include 10% population increases for Sumatran Tiger, Elephant, Rhinoceros, and poaching reductions (40% for Tiger/Elephant, 100% for Rhinoceros). Success requires strengthening IWT policy/legal frameworks (e.g., two revisions legalized), enhancing institutional capacity (>25% increase in seizures/arrests, >75% prosecutions, Gakkum capacity score 60% to 80%, 200 staff trained), improving enforcement strategy (e.g., 50% PortMATE score increase at 5 ports, 25% IWT seizure increase in demo regions), and ensuring effective knowledge management (2100 staff, 600 local beneficiaries)."

"The targeted outcome is reducing Tajikistan's 10-year rolling average annual total economic losses due to disaster by 3.1% by 2024 from a 2014 baseline of $111.9 million. Success requires achieving this reduction and fulfilling key outputs: mainstreaming DRM in government institutions, strengthening natural hazard management capacity (including hazard maps, DMIS, EWS, and training), developing a 9-year DRM investment plan, and improving resilience for displaced persons and local communities (e.g., operational safe sites, climate-resilient infrastructure, skill acquisition)."

"The activity targets bolstering water supply and sewerage performance, ensuring service reliability and quality, and enhancing SONEDE's operational and financial sustainability. Key outcomes include meeting water demands through 2025, averting service deficits by 2010, and adding 147,800 household connections by 2025. Infrastructure upgrades and SONEDE capacity building (operational/financial management, demand management, institutional strengthening) are central. Success hinges on robust economic viability (IRR >12%), effective management of expenses and revenue, optimized investment planning, improved customer service, and sustained staff and network performance as tracked by service, capacity, and financial indicators."

"Targeted Outcomes: Increased hydroelectric energy production (360 GWh) and winter firm capacity (250 MW), 20% reduced O&M costs, 1.3 million tonnes CO2 equivalent reduction, improved energy supply security/reliability/quality, unimpeded energy market operation, enhanced plant operational stability/efficiency/safety, and opened electricity market. Supported MFE/NERC in Energy Sector Reform and WEM. Upgraded UkrEnergo capabilities, retrofitted facilities for environmental compliance, and improved utility billing/communication. Success Criteria: Requires strong UHE ownership/technical skills, proper project management, sustained political commitment, adequate financial support, a long-term reform-oriented government, and a comprehensive energy strategy aligned with the EU-Ukraine Action Plan to mitigate policy reversal."

"[\"The Guangxi Beibu Gulf Cities Development Project targets improved living conditions & socioeconomic development. By 2016, outcomes: 40%↑ access road length in Beihai Tieshan (from 271km), 80%↑ in Zhulin (from 9km), 15%↑ in Qinzhou port areas (from 86km). Traffic flow on Fangcheng-Jiangshan road to ↑70% (from 5,551 vehicles/12hrs). Maoling town wastewater collection/treatment to reach 100% (from 0%). By 2019, broader impacts: maintain GDP growth > national average, ↑international trade volume by 60%, ↓waterborne diseases in Fangchenggang by 20% (from 2.3/100,000). 20% of construction jobs for women/ethnic minorities/poor.\", \"Overall success requires achieving all targeted outcomes: road length/traffic increases, 100% wastewater treatment, GDP/trade growth, disease reduction, and employment for women/minorities. It depends on key assumptions: continued national priority for regional economic integration, adequate O&M budgets, other urban infrastructure construction, steady population, and industry wastewater pre-treatment compliance. Effective mitigation of risks like delayed land acquisition, poor coordination, higher costs, and ineffective O&M for roads/WWTP is also crucial.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Uganda Ninth Poverty Reduction Support Credit (PRSC 9) are to improve access to and achieve greater value for money in public services. This aligns with Uganda's National Development Plan, aiming for the country to become a middle-income nation by 2017 and a modern, prosperous nation within 30 years, through increased household incomes and enhanced equity. The PRSC series specifically targets improved expenditure efficiency and value for money in the health, education, transport, and water and sanitation sectors by addressing both cross-cutting and sector-specific bottlenecks using performance-based management. The outcomes are categorized into two clusters: 1) Reforms in Public Expenditure Management, Public Financial Management, and Public Service Management, which aims for improved budget credibility, transparent and efficient public financial management and procurement, strengthened public sector management and accountability, and a strengthened local government system. This addresses issues like insufficient budget credibility, inadequate funding at service delivery levels, poor reporting, and unpredictable fund releases. 2) Improving Value for Money in Public Service Delivery Sectors, which targets wider access to and better quality of primary and secondary education, health services (including sexual and reproductive health care and communicable disease control), improved water and sanitation systems, and an improved national road network to reduce transport costs, boost competitiveness, and facilitate economic activity. Overall success would be achieved by meeting the following targets: 
- Maintaining a maximum 5 percent budget variance between allocations and releases for Joint Budget Support Framework (JBSF) sectors.
- Increasing clean audit reports to 45% for Central Government, 59% for Statutory Bodies, and 29% for Local Government.
- Achieving 100% publication of Local Governments' financial transfers and budgets.
- Guaranteeing 90 percent of children are immunized with DPT3.
- Increasing the number of primary pupils passing Primary Leaving Examinations with grades I-III to 267,133 (with 46% girls).
- Ensuring 84 percent functionality for rural water sources and 30 percent for valley tanks/dams.
- Achieving 80 percent of paved national roads in fair to good condition.
- Filling 65% of approved health posts with qualified health workers.
- Reducing the absenteeism rate in health facilities to 22%.
- Increasing the proportion of health facilities without drug stock outs for 6 tracer drugs to 65%.
- Equitable teacher deployment across districts through a new teacher allocation formula.

From a macroeconomic perspective, success also depends on maintaining a consistent and sustainable framework through prudent fiscal and monetary policies, effective management of anticipated oil revenues, channeling fiscal resources to productive public investments, fostering productivity growth and skills development, and achieving projected average growth of about 7 percent driven by agricultural rebound, private sector investment, and growth in services and manufacturing."

"Outcomes: Increased transport efficiency (Almaty-Khorgos WE-WC Road Corridor upgrade to 305 km, 4 lanes), modernized highway management, efficient goods/people movement, improved road safety, fostered economic activity. Success: 10% vehicle operating cost decrease, reduced Almaty-Khorgos travel time, sound O&M practices (km covered by Quality Charter), reduced O&M financing gap, more roads in good/fair condition. Also, 305 km non-rural roads constructed, Kazakhavtodor maintaining 600 km of SWRP WE-WC section, 300 staff trained, 300 km under user-pay principles. The project is economically justified."

"The project targets improved access to water and sanitation in selected urban areas sustainably. Outcomes include 180,000 people gaining access to safe drinking water, 80,000 to improved sanitation, and 330,000 to enhanced water supply, totaling 590,000 direct beneficiaries (50% female). It aims for urban water supply sub-sector financial equilibrium and 90% ONAS sewerage expenditure coverage. Success means meeting all these targets, reducing gender inequalities by easing water hauling and providing safe sanitation, and achieving 80% beneficiary feedback, while IDA supports sectoral reforms."

"Targeted outcomes: Improved Xin'an River upstream economic/environmental conditions by 2028: 95% urban wastewater access, 130k rural beneficiaries, 28k ha improved land, 5k ha green farming, water quality index <=1. Upgraded urban/rural pollution control (e.g., 176km sewers, 85 decentralized systems), 70k ha forest protection, 428 jobs, 6+ green SMEs (30% women in management), \n2.8M incentive fund, 4.5k farmers compensated, strengthened management capacity including MIS & staff training. Success: Achieving all these targets, demonstrating sustainable economic growth and environmental improvement in the Yangtze River Economic Belt."

"The GEF project targets increasing electricity access and renewable energy generation. Key outcomes include new/improved service for 9,345 people (7,695 households, 1,500 community, 150 microenterprises); 5.66 GWh annual renewable energy output (3.4 MW capacity); 5 mini-grids constructed; and 1,350 households (135 female-headed) and 75 micro-enterprises connected. Success requires increased commercial activity, productivity, energy savings, reduced/stabilized electricity prices, SP doubling its customer base to 30,000, strengthened institutional capacity, a sustainable private sector, employment growth, and reducing 96,889 tCO2eq GHG emissions."

"The Accelerator Lab Cote d'Ivoire targets accelerating top-quality programmatic results for the SDGs. Outcomes include increased capability of UNDP Country Offices and partners in scanning, sensemaking, and experimentation, scaling new sustainable development solutions at country level, and establishing a global learning and scaling network. Success entails country offices piloting/scaling innovative tools and methodologies. Specifically, targets involve: using new/diverse data sources (up to 10 new, 9 diverse by Year 3), identifying 50 solutions (Year 3), employing 10 innovation methods per portfolio (Year 3), sharing 480 media posts (Year 3), 30 lab insights taken up by governments/partners (Year 3), 30% of partners adopting innovation methods, 30% UNDP CO programming influenced (Year 3), and consistent weekly lab interactions (global network) with 50% methodology/SDG-related information shared (Year 3). Overall success is embedding innovation as a 'corporate reflex'."

"The targeted outcome of the South Asia Subregional Economic Cooperation Port Access Elevated Highway Project is to increase transport efficiency along the project road. For the activity to be considered an overall success, the following specific targets must be met by 2026: the average travel time from Galle Face to the Colombo-Katunayake Expressway must be reduced to 7 minutes from a 2018 baseline of 30 minutes; the average daily vehicle-km using the project road must increase to 111,500 from a 2018 baseline of 0; and the ratio of import cargo traffic using the project road from the port must increase to 25% from a 2018 baseline of 0%."

"The targeted outcomes are: developing technical guidelines/standards for industrial wastewater reuse (at least 1 guideline, 50 stakeholders consulted); formulating eco-design guidelines for 2 plastic products and a roadmap for recycled content; creating an assessment report and roadmap to reduce imported plastic dependence; and enhancing SME capacity by training at least 100 enterprises, developing 13 investable business plans, and supporting 300 employees. Success hinges on achieving these specific targets, contributing to Viet Nam's low-carbon and circular development through transformed business practices."

"The activity targets the phase-out of 10.01 ODP tonnes of HCFC-22 in the RAC servicing sector by January 1, 2030, with no servicing tail, leading to an approximate 18 Gt CO2 equivalent reduction (834,819 tCO2eq by 2024). Outcomes include strengthening HCFC licensing/quota systems, building capacity of customs/law enforcement and RAC technicians, providing training equipment to seven polytechnics, establishing demonstration facilities, and setting up six recovery/recycling centers. Success hinges on achieving these phase-out and emissions reduction targets, sustained economic conditions, strong industry/political support, and a robust regulatory framework."

"{\"result_1\": \"The primary targeted outcome is increased productivity and sustainability of the Muhuri Irrigation Project. This involves modernizing infrastructure, strengthening national practices for sustainable management, operation, and maintenance (MOM) of large-scale irrigation schemes, and fostering private sector participation by transferring MOM responsibilities to private operators. Key goals include climate change adaptation, enhanced flood risk protection, assured irrigation water supply, and increased agricultural productivity. Also, replacing diesel pumps with electric ones, developing efficient buried-pipe tertiary distribution, and installing prepaid meters.\", \"result_2\": \"For overall success by 2024, the dry-season irrigation area must increase to 18,000 ha (60% increase), O&M funding from farmers and government must reach 100%, and average winter paddy yield must increase to 4 tons/ha. By 2023, a long-term performance-based MOM lease contract must be signed, 100% MOM cost recovery achieved, 300 trained farmers (20% women) adopting productive methods. Also, Muhuri Project infrastructure covering 18,000 ha must be rehabilitated and modernized with 850 electric pump schemes and piped tertiary systems, 17 km coastal embankments repaired, 373 km canal drains re-excavated, and women employed as pump operators (2%), water unit vendors (5%), and construction workers (5%).\"}"

"The targeted outcomes are an increased number of pedestrians, improved pedestrian experience, and reduced safety/mobility deficits. Success means constructing 5 km of elevated, accessible, disaster-resilient walkways by Q2 2023 with responsive design features for all users. This includes a 5% increase in pedestrian traffic by 2024 at key intersections and a 70% reduction in reported safety/mobility deficits by project completion. The project also aims to generate 2.8 million person-days of jobs and contribute to inclusive mobility."

"Targeted outcomes include: improved access to local services for 140,000 people (70,000 women, 25,000 refugees); strengthened Local Governments' capacity to plan and manage local public services; positive impact on 70% of local businesses (women-owned, refugee-owned); 4,500 new electricity connections (1,000 for refugees/host communities); 30% increase in municipal investment budget execution and own-revenue mobilization; 11 approved development plans; 3 signed investment plans; and training for 2,400 LG officials."

"Targeted outcomes include: efficient development of Vietnam's transmission and distribution system; upgrading and expanding 500kV, 220kV, and 110kV lines and transformers (e.g., 567km 500kV lines, 1,950 MVA 500/220kV transformers); modernizing national/regional load dispatch systems, installing a full-function transmission market management system, and improving telecommunications backbone (all \"in place\"); developing a single transmission subsidiary (\"in place\") and a functioning internal power market (\"substantial evidence\"). Success depends on connecting 1,200 units new generation capacity via 500kV links, reducing 500/220/110kV system interruptions (e.g., 500kV number to 0.315), meeting peak hour minimum voltages, achieving planned asset creation, commissioning all market/system control systems, establishing the subsidiary/market, and EVN meeting financial covenants."

"The project targets strengthened environmental sustainability, inclusiveness, and climate resilience in Vinh Yen, Hue, and Ha Giang. Overall success by 2024 means: >80% user satisfaction with green infrastructure, 20% O&M jobs for poor/near-poor, 11,000 m\b3/day wastewater collected/treated in Vinh Yen, Hue Citadel flooding reduced to once in 5 years, and 5 central wards in Ha Giang protected from 1-in-15-year rainfall. Outputs include improved urban infrastructure in all three cities (e.g., drainage, embankments, green spaces, roads, wastewater systems, energy-efficient streetlights) and enhanced institutional capacity."

"The targeted outcomes of the Zambia - Mining and Environmental Remediation and Improvement Project are to reduce environmental health risks to the local population in critically polluted mining areas across Chingola, Kabwe, Kitwe, and Mufulira municipalities, with a specific focus on reducing lead exposure in Kabwe. This is to be achieved by optimizing financial mechanisms for identifying and remediating contaminated areas, strengthening environmental management capacities of regulatory agencies and local governments, and implementing targeted health interventions alongside improved job opportunities for affected individuals, particularly women and youth.To consider the activity an overall success, the following targets must be met:1. The share of the population living within 500m of lead contamination hotspots in the project area must decrease from 100% to 30%.2. 4,000 people in Kabwe, including 2,400 individuals aged 15 or younger, must demonstrate more than a 50% reduction in their Blood Lead Levels (BLL).3. 70% of the population in the project areas must be aware of the project's investments and benefits.4. The project must directly benefit 13,000 people, with 70% of these beneficiaries being female."

"Targeted outcomes include reliable, sustainable, and affordable wastewater services for urban and rural areas, improving living standards for 530,000 poor, and centralized wastewater service meeting environmental standards for 800,000 residents. Success requires achieving these outcomes by end-2028, plus rehabilitating/constructing WWTPs (392,000 m\n\n/day capacity), expanding sewerage (30,000 new connections), full metering, strengthened TST institutional capacity, economic/financial viability (EIRR>9%, FIRR>WACC with tariff reforms), and 34,512 tons/annum GHG reductions."

"The activity targets improving sustainable management of forests and protected areas, increasing benefits to communities from forests in target landscapes, and enhancing climate resilience. This includes addressing pervasive forest degradation, unsustainable management, and missed opportunities for revenue and jobs. Key outcomes are an enhanced forest stock, restored and more sustainably managed forests, and increased revenues and jobs from protected areas, forests, plantations, and trees, contributing to local and national economies. It also aims for enhanced climate resilience through improved livelihoods and better protected watersheds, with a goal of around 62.5 million tons of net GHG emissions reductions."

"[\"Targeted outcomes: National/regional/local institutions and civil society improve rural/urban/agriculture/environment management, and mechanisms prevent/reduce risks to enhance population resilience to disasters/climate change. Outcome indicators: Haiti's INFORM index improves from 6.1 (2015) to 5 (2021); Cost as % of GDP of disaster losses reduces from 2% (1975-2012) to <2% (2021). Output 1 targets (by 2022): 32 training courses in energy service management; 12 strengthened cooperatives/CBOs (min. 3 women's orgs); 7 enterprises trained in system maintenance (min. 2 managed by women); 450 women trained in entrepreneurship; 6 municipalities with electrification plans.\", \"Output 2 targets (by 2022): 5 micro power stations constructed/managed; 7 partnerships for micro power station management; 140 civil society groups consulted (min. 40 women's groups). Output 3 targets (by 2022): 1000 families benefiting from electricity (40% women); 150 micro/single owner enterprises created (min. 75 managed by women); 3000 direct/indirect jobs created (min. 40% held by women); 2 networks of cooperatives for micro power station/reserve fund management. Overall success requires achieving all these targeted outcomes and outputs by 2022.\"]"

"The targeted outcome is safe, sustainable, inclusive drinking water service in project districts. Success entails: By 2025: 1.65 million people receiving continuous potable piped water. By 2025: 70% of gram panchayats recovering \n880% O&M costs, <15% nonrevenue water, serving 100% poor/women-headed households with \n833% female staff. By 2024: 390,000 household connections for 70 lpcd, commissioning climate-resilient water infrastructure (plants, storage, 4,800 km network) with smart management. By 2024: Strengthened institutions, 70% gram panchayats adopting asset management, increased PHED/local capacity."

"The targeted outcome is to protect Phuentsholing's urban area from floods and expand it with improved amenities and services, ensuring balanced and sustainable human settlement development with smart growth principles. Success by 2026 requires Phuentsholing and reclaimed land to be protected from 100-year flood events and at least 10% of serviced plots tendered. By 2025, 4.77km of river walls, 66ha reclaimed land, flood warning/management system, 10km roads (30% female planning participation), 4MLD water treatment plant (12km mains), 3MLD sewerage plant (9km sewer/storm drains), solid-waste recovery system, 630kVA grid substation (16 circuit-km power, 11 circuit-km telecom lines), and township management systems (80% staff improved knowledge, 100% infrastructure geocoded asset system) must be completed."

"Targeted outcomes: Enhance utility operational efficiency & increase electricity access. Key targets: 324,000 household connections (50% female); reduce commercial losses to 4.00% (from 7.60%) & technical losses by 7.18 GWh in Kigali's 15KV network; cut average annual outage duration to 50 hours (from 100); reduce postpaid customer receivables age to 60 days (from 90). Also, establish modern MIS, strengthen EUCL staff/business plan, connect 412 schools/340 admin centers/40 health facilities, rehabilitate 19 switching stations, & build/rehabilitate 1666 km distribution lines. Success means achieving these, improving efficiency, expanding access, and benefiting 72,000 households with cost savings, better lighting, and income."

"Targeted outcomes: improved national water resources management, enhanced institutional performance of BWDB/WARPO, reduced vulnerability, enhanced livelihood opportunities, increased agricultural production, and substantial poverty reduction (7.1% below upper poverty line, 6.7% below lower poverty line for 2M households). Success requires 60% of schemes operational at enhanced levels with increased community participation, showing 60% reduced damage/increased agricultural production to community satisfaction. Also, WMOs must function in 60% of schemes, 100% rehabilitation/O&M completed by YR7, BWDB's reform plans approved, and ERRs of 29.5% (C1) and 48.0% (C2) realized."

"The Grid-Connected Rooftop Solar Program targets two main objectives. The Program Development Objective (PDO) is to increase the installed capacity of Grid-connected Rooftop Solar PV (GRPV) and strengthen the capacity of relevant institutions for GRPV. The Program Environmental Objective (PEO) is to achieve reductions in Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions by displacing thermal energy with solar energy.Specific targeted outcomes include:

1.  **Increased Rooftop Solar PV capacity connected to the grid:**
    *   50 MW by Year 2
    *   100 MW by Year 3
    *   150 MW by Year 4
    *   250 MW by Year 5

2.  **Reduction of carbon emissions:**
    *   100 thousand tons by Year 2
    *   300 thousand tons by Year 3
    *   700 thousand tons by Year 4
    *   1,200 thousand tons by Year 5

To consider the activity an overall success, several conditions and additional targets must be met:

*   **Installed and Commissioned Capacity:** The program expects to finance 600 MW of GRPV orders, with 500 MW installed and commissioned over its 5.5-year lifetime. Specifically, 500 MW of rooftop solar should be installed and commissioned under the program by Year 5.
*   **Strengthened Institutional Capacity:** The State Bank of India (SBI) must launch its GRPV program and develop internal procedures for project identification, risk assessment, appraisal, and approval. A Technical Assistance (TA) and capacity-building program for discoms, State Nodal Agencies (SNAs), and State Electricity Regulatory Commissions (SERCs) needs to be implemented. Ultimately, SBI should design, commit funds for, and announce a second phase program funded by its own resources or through syndication, ensuring the sustainability and replication of the GRPV program and knowledge generated.
*   **Expanded Lending and Market Development:** SBI must sign loans for the financing of GRPV power generation schemes, aiming for an aggregate of US$625 million in approved loans by Year 5. This includes piloting new business models by making at least US$15 million in loans to Non-Bank Financial Companies (NBFCs) and Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs). The program also aims for US$265 million in additional equity financing from private sources mobilized by SBI by Year 5. Total GRPV orders are expected to reach 600 MW by Year 5.

Meeting these capacity, emission reduction, financial, and institutional strengthening targets will signify the activity's overall success."

"The activity targets improved energy efficiency, reliability, and safety of Bengaluru's power supply by reducing technical/commercial losses (to <=9.1% by 2026), average outage duration (to <=72 hrs by 2026), supply interruptions (to <=10.8/year by 2026), fatal accidents (to <=18/year by 2026), and avoiding 113,653 tons CO2e. Success requires converting 7,200 km overhead lines to underground and installing 1,700 automated RMUs with DAS by 2025, alongside strengthening BESCOM's institutional capacity, benefiting 5.2M people, and implementing gender equality measures."

"The targeted outcome is to demonstrate climate-resilient and sustainable rural economic diversification and mitigation of climate change in Gansu Province. Success requires, by 2029: implementing at least four rural pollution control practices; annually reducing 20,000 tons of CO2 emissions; establishing a government agency unit for low-carbon vitalization plans; creating 2,000 jobs (30% for women); and benefiting 260,000 people (130,000 women) from increased rural investment.
