Additionally, by 2028, institutional capacity, sustainable agriculture, low-carbon facilities, and environmental restoration must be enhanced as detailed in output indicators."

"The Lesotho Renewable Energy & Energy Access Project targets: 6 Industrial/Economic Zones connected; 22,825 people (including 11,751 women) with new/improved electricity; 4.09 MW renewable energy generation from mini-grids; 245 community facilities and 490 SMEs with electricity. It also aims for 745,801 tCO2 emissions reduction, strengthened institutional capacity, sustainable development, poverty reduction, improved livelihoods, social services, economic opportunities, and enhanced women's participation. Success requires achieving all these quantifiable targets, realizing the broad socio-economic and environmental benefits, a positive Economic Rate of Return (43.0-46.1% EIRR), and a strengthened, confidence-inspiring regulatory framework for sustainable electricity access expansion and future funding."

"Targeted outcomes: Reduced transit times (e.g., Mombasa-Kigali from 7 to 5.6 days); 50% national paved road network in good condition; 100% Kigali-Ruhengeri road in good condition by YR4; 3,000 annual permanent jobs from maintenance; restored/developed private sector capacity in road industry; rebuilding of depleted road sector capacity (50 staff with Master's); improved overall road sector efficiency; at least four sector agencies implementing performance agreements. Overall success requires achieving these targets, particularly the 20% reduction in transit times, 50% good condition for paved roads, 3,000 jobs, and significant capacity building and governance improvements by project end."

"[\"The primary impact by 2023 is an improved living environment in 20 project towns, achieving \\\"model town\\\" features: improved municipal service delivery (e.g., 95% water supply, frequent solid waste collection, no waterlogging), financial sustainability, citizen participation, and urban development control. Overall project aims for improved urban living conditions. By 2021, outcomes include 60,000 households with new/improved piped water and solid waste collection (twice/week), 85% holding tax collection efficiency in 29 towns, and 1% municipal revenue budget earmarked and 50% spent on Pourashava-specific Gender Action Plans in 23 towns.\", \"Overall success requires: 600km roads, 300km drains, 180km water pipes with 60,000 meters, sludge facilities in 14 towns, and solid waste sites (20 towns, 200 tons/day capacity) built/improved. 80% subprojects must integrate climate considerations, reducing CO2 by 15,000 tons/year. Governance success entails TLCCs/WLCCs (33-40% women) formed, gender-inclusive master plans gazetted, WCA/PRSI committees (40% women) operational, and PDP/GAP/PRAP approved in 35 towns. Annual O&M plans/budgets, functional computerized tax systems, and performance-based funding awards are also crucial. Assumptions like sustained government support, manageable population growth, strong O&M capacity, and timely land acquisition must hold true.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include achieving inclusive and sustainable growth, scaling up climate action, and integrating gender-sensitive policies. Specifically, by 2023, the program aims for: 4 political leaders championing climate ambition; 5 new/improved governance structures; 6 gender-responsive institutional approaches; 5 enhanced monitoring systems; 3 countries disaggregating mitigation targets; 10 countries undertaking gender analysis; 1 country preparing LEDS; 5 strengthened NAMAs; 4 countries undertaking risk analysis; 4 gender-responsive financing mechanisms; and 3 countries addressing market-based barriers. Success hinges on meeting or exceeding these specific cumulative targets by December 2023."

"The Distribution Efficiency Project aims to enhance PCs' performance in delivering reliable, quality electricity and reduce GHG emissions through demand-side response. Targeted outcomes include improved service, reduced technical/commercial losses, increased revenues, lower costs, enhanced transparency, efficient electricity use, and environmental co-benefits. Success by 2018 requires: SAIDI reduction (e.g., HNPC from 299 to 291 mins; HCM PC from 1,682 to 384 mins; SPC from 6,958 to 5,525 mins), SAIFI reduction, total 414.1 GWh consumption reduction by AMI users, total 269,148 Tons CO2 avoided, and 100% implementation of 110 kV, MV, LV networks, AMI, and SCADA systems."

"The targeted outcomes of the Vietnam Renewable Energy Development Project include increasing the supply of least-cost electricity to the national grid from renewable energy sources on a commercially, environmentally, and socially sustainable basis, leveraging market-based financing. This involves scaling up renewable energy capacity, catalyzing market financing, improving the regulatory framework for long-term renewable energy investment, adopting technical standards, issuing environmental and benefit-sharing guidelines for small hydropower projects, enhancing capacity among stakeholders, supporting high-quality feasibility studies, building long-term capacity through educational institutions, and identifying future financing needs.To consider the activity an overall success, the project needs to achieve: 960 GWh/year of electricity supplied to the grid by financed projects and 1250 GWh/year using the ACT tariff by end-2013. 50% of small hydro projects must meet new environment standards and have a benefit-sharing scheme by end-2013. All subprojects must demonstrate economic viability, with an aggregate Economic Rate of Return (ERR) significantly above a 10% hurdle rate, even in pessimistic scenarios (e.g., 11.9% aggregate ERR, or 14.8% with GHG benefits). The risk of not meeting the hurdle rate should be less than two percent per subproject. 70% of commissioned subprojects must meet appraised budget, timetable, and performance. The regulatory framework should be improved, with technical standards adopted and guidelines issued. 250 MW of renewable energy capacity should be installed under REDP, with 150 MW financed by non-IDA sources by end-2013. Participating Banks must consistently meet selection criteria, and the Co-financing Agreement be effective by September 30, 2010."

"The Kabeli Transmission Project targets increasing transmission capacity in the Integrated Nepal Power System and providing electricity and cooking fuel access to communities along the 132 kV line. Success entails: constructing the 132 kV line, aiming for 140 MW capacity by 2023; achieving off-grid targets of 250kW new microhydro, 200 biogas stoves, 300 solar home systems, and 15 institutional solar systems; completing resettlement and environmental assessments, providing project-affected people's entitlements; demonstrating financial viability with a 22.2% equity IRR and 38% EIRR; and adhering to governance plans."

"The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor - I aims to meet growing freight/passenger demand with improved service and develop DFCCIL/IR institutional capacities for DFC network operation. For the Khurja-Kanpur section, targets include additional rail transport capacity, improved service quality, and higher freight throughput. Success requires: 1. **Freight:** 100 additional train paths/day on DFC, 60 KM/hr average speed, and increased throughput (e.g., 36 GTKM bn ton by 2016-17). 2. **Passenger:** Increased express passenger services on existing railway. 3. **Institutional:** DFCCIL HR Plan progress, 'Good or Higher' MoU performance, on-schedule contract management, and meeting staff targets (e.g., 6860 total staff by 2016-17). 4. **Financial:** Achieving attractive economic (22-23% EIRR) and financial returns (17% FIRR), and maximizing new traffic opportunities."

"The activity targets improving road transport efficiency & safety by reducing travel times (Uliastai-Altai by 40%, Myangad-Naranbulag-Ulaangom by 37%) & achieving 3-star+ road safety ratings for 75% of upgraded roads by 2030. It aims to upgrade 263 km of roads with climate-resilient features & EWCD-friendly rest stops, install 2 weigh bridges, implement a fully operational RAMS, train 30+ staff (30%+ women) in asset management, and enhance road safety for 1,500+ residents & 50+ teachers by 2029. Success hinges on meeting these targets, including an EIRR >6.0%, and delivering benefits to 41,701 direct & 308,184 indirect residents (50% women) through better access, reduced costs, & improved income skills for 230+ people (50% women)."

"[\"The activity targets include replacing 800,000 tons of raw coal to reduce particulate matter emissions, scaling up pneumococcal conjugate vaccine coverage nationwide for children under 1, establishing a green financing mechanism for low-cost loans for green appliances/insulation, implementing community engagement for indoor air pollution, and supporting long-term sustainability via enhanced air quality monitoring, urban planning, clean heating plans for ger areas, and energy-efficient building standards (EDGE).\",\"Overall success requires: approval/submission of the Ulaanbaatar Development Plan 2020-2040; operationalization of a green financing mechanism with funding and preferential access for women; approval of a long-term clean heat supply plan for ger areas; adoption/compliance with updated air quality monitoring; a fully operational National Committee secretariat; an established customized EDGE system; implementation of a community engagement program with 40% female participation and quantitative impact assessment; and a study on new affordable night-time heating tariffs with stakeholder consultations.\"]"

"The activity targets enhanced ecological resilience and rural livelihoods, with improved ecosystem-based management and climate-resilient development of the Chao Lake basin, achieving a sustained environmental state. Success by 2030 entails: monthly Class IV water quality for Chao Lake; 2.85 million beneficiaries from improved water/wastewater/climate resilience; 1,318.3 tons/year GHG reduction; 1-in-50 year flood protection; enhanced institutional capacity/public awareness; operational Digital Chao Lake basin DSS with monitoring/early warning; piloted green financing/eco-compensation mechanisms; demonstrated low-carbon/climate-resilient restoration (river works, dredging, wetlands, farmland, 22 wastewater interventions); and gender-specific job creation."

"The targeted outcomes of the Jiangxi Shangrao Sanqingshan Airport Project are to improve airline connectivity in northeastern Jiangxi Province and demonstrate the environmental sustainability of its development and operation. Specific targets include a 27% reduction in travel time between Shangrao and major cities (such as Beijing, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, and Haikou), and the recognition of Shangrao Airport as a \"Green Airport\" through an established process. The project also aims to serve 8.5 million people in the catchment area, cater to regional tourism, and achieve multimodal transport, economic, and financial sustainability, serving as a model for future feeder airports. Success is measured by achieving the 27% travel time reduction and receiving \"Green Airport\" recognition. Additionally, the airport must achieve financial sustainability by exceeding an annual passenger flow of 350,000, and ensure timely, cost-effective construction with the green airport design fully implemented."

"The targeted outcomes of the CN: Beijing Distributed Solar Photovoltaic Scale-Up Project are to increase the share of clean energy in electricity consumption, demonstrate the viability of the renewable energy service company (RESCO) model for rooftop solar PV deployment in schools, and reduce CO2 emissions. Key targets include installing 100 MW of rooftop solar PV systems across 800-1000 schools, securing 800 RESCO contracts, and reducing CO2 emissions by 89,590 metric tons/year by FY2018. Additionally, the project aims to demonstrate two-way metering, establish 20 renewable energy demonstration laboratories, operationalize an online monitoring system, and provide training on the RESCO model for local capacity development. Overall success would require achieving these targets, confirming the materialized share of renewable energy, and demonstrating that the RESCO model provides valuable experience for replication."

"The Energy Sector Development Project targets increasing generation capacity and efficiency of electricity supply, strengthening planning and technical capacities, and providing cleaner, more affordable, and reliable electricity. Specific outcomes include constructing 4.70 MW conventional and 0.20 MW solar generation capacity, achieving 124 million MJ in fuel savings, reaching 50,000 beneficiaries (50% female), and 100% implementation of sustainability reforms. Success also entails fully commissioning generation and street lighting equipment, establishing robust technical and planning capacities at state and national levels, completing master plans, and demonstrating improved utility financial viability and reduced emissions, aligning with the 30% renewable energy target by 2020."

"The activity targets strengthening public investment planning/implementation, enhancing government capacity, improving investment efficiency, and ensuring fiscal discipline. Success requires: implementation of road pavement management and learning assessment systems; completion of industrial extension impact evaluation; submission of public asset management law; formulation of disaster risk policy; meeting fiscal balance, investment expenditure, and ICMS revenue targets; achieving 20% paved road network under CREMA contracts; undertaking school projects; signing APLs/Extension Centers agreements; implementing new triple helix alliances; reducing environmental license emission time to 319 days; and increasing real estate asset inventory."

"The Advanced Electricity Metering (AEM) Project targets the reduction of commercial losses for Uzbekenergo's (UE) three regional power distribution companies in Tashkent City, and the Oblasts of Tashkent and Syrdarya. This will be achieved by improving their metering and billing infrastructure and commercial management systems. Key outcomes include significantly advancing UE's metering, billing, and revenue collection systems with modern \"smart\" metering technologies, enhancing energy efficiency, commercializing utility operations, and reducing commercial losses by strengthening governance in meter reading, billing, and revenue collections. Direct beneficiaries, including the three power distribution companies, are expected to experience increased collection rates, improved financial viability, and enhanced operational efficiencies. Electricity consumers in the project areas will benefit from improved supply reliability, fair and transparent pricing signals, and information to better manage their electricity consumption, leading to reduced energy waste, lower carbon emissions, and environmental benefits. Employees of the distribution companies will benefit from reduced labor-intensive activities. To be considered an overall success, the project needs to achieve specific targets by 2016: an increase in the billing rate in the project areas from a baseline of 80% (2011) to 88%, and an increase in the collection rate in the project areas from a baseline of 78% (2011) to 98%. Intermediate results also define success criteria, including the accumulated percentage of all single-phase and three-phase meters and associated communication infrastructure installed reaching 100% by 2015, the metering data management system being 100% installed and functioning by 2014, and the percentage of customer meters communicating with the management system reaching 100% by 2016. Additionally, the centralized Energy Data Management (EDM)/Billing/Archive system needs to be 100% installed and functioning by 2014, and 100% of existing billing data must be migrated to the new system by 2015."

"The ECOWAS-Regional Electricity Access Project aims to increase grid electricity access for approximately 1.1 million people in Guinea-Bissau, Mali, and The Gambia. Key targets include providing new/improved electricity to 152,000 people and raising access rates to 16% (Mali), 2% (Guinea-Bissau), 17% (The Gambia). Success requires achieving these access goals, constructing 1,900 km (GB/Gambia) and 3,800 km (Mali) distribution lines, strengthening utilities' financial viability (e.g., reduced generation costs, increased revenue), achieving positive economic (NPV US$111M, EIRR 10.6%) and financial (FIRR 3.3%) returns, and addressing 100% of grievances."

"The PA Metro Water and Sanitation Improvement Project aims to assist the Republic of Panamá in increasing the quality, coverage, and efficiency of its water supply and sanitation (WSS) services in lower-income neighborhoods within the Panamá Metropolitan Region (PMR). Specifically, the targeted outcomes include: increasing the number of beneficiaries in lower-income neighborhoods with access to reliable water supply from a baseline of 383,000 to 483,000 by Year 5; increasing the number of beneficiaries with access to improved sanitation facilities from 382,000 to 442,000 by Year 5; and improving revenue collection over total population in the project area from $13/hab. (baseline) to $24/hab. by Year 5.Intermediate outcomes include creating 3,000 new piped household water connections, rehabilitating 20,000 piped household water connections, creating 15,000 new sewer connections, and rehabilitating 3,000 sewer connections by Year 5. Further targets involve increasing the reliability of water supply by eliminating or reducing current intermittence, expanding WSS coverage, reducing real and apparent water losses, improving revenue collection, and implementing micro-meters.The project also aims to optimize water supply infrastructure, enhance information and management systems (including micro and macro metering, controlling water losses, improving customer and network registers, strengthening billing and collection practices, recovering bad debts, and developing a SCADA system). Institutionally, it targets strengthening IDAAN by developing a management and internal benchmarking system, promoting rational water use campaigns, strengthening planning and project management capacity, developing a risk management strategy against natural disasters, training staff, designing incentive frameworks, and devising a strategy for implementing tariff revisions. For the Colón Business Unit, specific targets include increasing active accounts in the commercial system, increasing the percentage of water meters read, improving the percentage of connections with reliable water supply, and increasing revenue collected per cubic meter.To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve a financial return of US$53 million and an economic return of US$102 million, with financial and economic internal rates of return of 24% and 32% respectively. Success also entails achieving the specific targets for beneficiaries with reliable water supply and improved sanitation, and the target for revenue collection. It must also result in increased water supply reliability, significantly reduced water losses, improved commercial performance, and an improved revenue collection rate (including legalizing illegal connections). Crucially, the project is expected to reduce IDAAN's operational deficit by approximately US$85 million over the 2010-2015 period, thereby reducing the Government of Panamá's financial transfers to IDAAN. The full implementation of all project components, leading to reduced production costs and increased revenues, and a positive impact on IDAAN’s financial indicators, are critical for overall success."

"[\"The Eco-Farming Project targets improved rural household quality of life (cleaner kitchens/farmyards, healthier environment, better manure management by Year 3). Outcomes include raised household disposable income through 30-50% reduced coal costs, 1-1.5 hrs/day less cooking time, ~2 hrs/day less firewood collection, and biogas slurry replacing chemical fertilizers/pesticides. It aims for a 600,000-1,000,000 t CO2 equivalent/year greenhouse gas reduction. Further outcomes include poverty reduction, increased agricultural outputs, strengthened technical extension/biogas service systems, enhanced institutional capacity, and improved health (reduced indoor air pollution, better sanitation).\",\"Overall success hinges on: 400,000-500,000 households implementing biogas by Year 5, with 20-30% reduction in chemical fertilizer/pesticide use. Key indicators include improved household well-being, increased disposable income, connection to water supply (150,000 households), 500 km village roads, and reduced travel time (100% of target households). Success also means 300,000 farmers trained, 75% adopting new practices, 75% satisfied with support, and strengthened institutional capacity (8,500 staff trained, 50 workshops, 40 study tours). Critical is a functioning M&E system and overcoming timely counterpart funding for robust economic returns.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include demonstrating energy efficiency benefits in public buildings, developing scalable financing models, and reducing public expenditure by \n18M annually (\n360M over 20 years). Specific targets include 650,000 MWh in projected lifetime energy savings and reducing CO2 emissions by 0.9kg/kWh saved. Overall success requires achieving these energy/cost savings, establishing sustainable entity-wide financing mechanisms, successfully piloting alternative models, strengthening implementation capacity, increasing public awareness, and ensuring end-user satisfaction."

"The Rural Alliances Project II aims to improve market accessibility for small rural producers by promoting productive alliances between producer organizations and purchasers, empowering rural producers through self-managed grass-root organizations, increasing access to productive assets, technology, and financial services, promoting more effective and accountable local service organizations, and enhancing environmental sustainability of productive practices. The project expects to benefit approximately 35,000 poor rural households (around 157,000 people), mostly indigenous, with 25,000 productive units as direct beneficiaries, and about 30% women representation in Producer Organizations. The activity will be considered an overall success if the average volume of sales for products involved in alliances increases by 50%; 80% of producer organizations register income and costs while being accountable to their members; and 70% of producer organizations maintain or improve their commercial relations for at least two productive cycles."

"[\"Restoration of 59,000 hectares of degraded land, comprising 30,000 ha of agricultural land, 12,000 ha of forest, and 17,000 ha of natural grass and shrublands. This includes restoring 22,000 ha of forest areas, 20,000 ha of cropland, and 17,000 ha of pastureland. Success is measured by achieving these specific hectare targets.\",\"Improved management of 210,450 hectares of terrestrial protected areas (Oti-Kéran National Park complex, Fazao-Malfakassa National Park) for conservation and sustainable use. Additionally, 37,000 hectares of landscape will be under improved practices, with 5,000 ha for biodiversity and 32,000 ha for sustainable land management in production systems. Success involves meeting these management targets.\",\"Mitigation of 21,851,054 tons of CO2e emissions over 20 years in the AFOLU sector, achieved through restoration of 12,000 ha of riparian forest, rehabilitation of 10,000 ha degraded land with tree crops/agroforestry, and improved management of 20,000 ha crop and 17,000 ha pasture land. Success is achieving this CO2e reduction and reducing forest loss/wildfires.\",\"Strengthening national capacities and policy frameworks for SLM/SFM. Building local actors' capacities on good SLM/SFM practices, ecosystem services valuation, and degraded land restoration through training programs and field schools. Success is marked by the establishment and effective utilization of these capacities and frameworks.\",\"Development and upscaling of environmentally sustainable nature-based livelihood opportunities with full gender balance. This includes dryland agroforestry products (e.g., Mango, Orange, Oil palm, Lemon, Moringa) and non-timber forest products (e.g., Shea, Néré) across Kara and Savanes regions, meeting specified hectare targets. Success entails achieving these targets, addressing value chain barriers, and promoting women's economic development.\",\"Significant contribution to Aichi Biodiversity Targets, including awareness of biodiversity values, sustainable production/consumption, reduced habitat loss, sustainable management of agriculture/forestry, ecosystem restoration for essential services, and enhanced ecosystem resilience/carbon stocks. Success is measured by progress towards these global and national environmental commitments.\",\"Development and implementation of a human-wildlife conflict mitigation program. Raising awareness and encouraging participation among women and youth on the importance of restoring degraded forests. Success is achieving effective human-wildlife coexistence and broad, inclusive community engagement in restoration efforts.\"]"

"The WAPP APL 2 - OMVS Felou Hydroelectric Project targets several key outcomes. The primary objective is to alleviate power supply deficits and improve power demand coverage in WAPP "Zone B" OMVS countries (Mali, Mauritania, and Senegal) by augmenting the supply of low-cost hydroelectricity. Specifically, by 2010, the project aims for the OMVS Power System to supply 427 GWh to EDM (Mali), 385 GWh to SENELEC (Senegal), and 331 GWh to SOMELEC (Mauritania), with the Felou project itself contributing significantly to an overall yearly output of approximately 350 GWH/year at an estimated cost of 4-5 US cents per kWh. A crucial outcome is the successful commissioning and performance testing of all three units of the Felou HEP on schedule (Unit 1 by June, Unit 2 by August, Unit 3 by October). The project also targets a high performance (time availability) for the Felou HEP, reaching 90% by October 2008 and 95% from 2009 onwards, to ensure stable and reliable low-cost electricity production. Furthermore, the activity intends to establish and ensure adherence to commercially-sound capacity and energy exchange agreements between the OMVS Power System Operator and national power utilities in WAPP "Zone B" countries, deploying a commercially-viable hydro-thermal power pooling mechanism. Lastly, it aims to enhance the WAPP Organization's capability to coordinate Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) functions by achieving 100% collection, analysis, and dissemination of Power Sector M&E data for WAPP "Zone B" OMVS countries by 2009-2010. The project is also expected to be financially viable, with an anticipated Economic Internal Rate of Return (EIRR) of 30-33% and a Financial Internal Rate of Return (FIRR) of 18.0%.

To consider the activity an overall success, the following would be needed:

1.  **Achieved Electricity Supply Targets**: The OMVS Power System must meet or exceed the targeted low-cost hydroelectricity supply to EDM (427 GWh), SENELEC (385 GWh), and SOMELEC (331 GWh) by 2010, effectively alleviating power supply deficits and improving demand coverage in the beneficiary countries.
2.  **Successful and High-Performing Félou HEP**: All three units of the Félou Hydroelectric Project must be successfully commissioned and performance tested on schedule (by October), and the plant must achieve and sustain a high operational availability of 95% from 2009 onwards, consistently producing stable and reliable electricity at low cost.
3.  **Established Power Pooling Mechanism**: Commercially-sound capacity and energy exchange agreements must be fully in place and adhered to between the OMVS Power System Operator and the national power utilities by 2008-2010, indicating a well-functioning hydro-thermal power pooling mechanism.
4.  **Enhanced M&E Capability**: The WAPP Organization must successfully collect, analyze, and disseminate 100% of Power Sector M&E data for WAPP "Zone B" OMVS countries by 2009-2010, demonstrating its enhanced coordination capability.
5.  **Financial and Economic Viability**: The project must achieve or exceed the projected Economic Internal Rate of Return (EIRR) of 30-33% and the Financial Internal Rate of Return (FIRR) of 18.0%, demonstrating its financial sustainability and economic benefits.
6.  **Regional Cooperation and Integration**: The project must contribute to the broader WAPP program objective of reducing fragmentation of West African economies and promoting cross-border electricity trade, supported by continued regional cooperation and adherence to policy milestones like the ECOWAS Energy Protocol."

"The targeted outcomes are to improve power system reliability and efficiency in Bangladesh, primarily through optimizing dispatch operation. This involves implementing frequency controls, investing in generators, removing transmission bottlenecks, and institutionalizing merit order dispatch. Specific outcomes include reduced unserved energy due to underfrequency from 5.08 GWh to 2.08 GWh, and projected fuel savings of 1500506.7 MJ. Overall success will be achieved by meeting these targets, alongside operational enhancements like minimum frequency increasing to 49.50 Hertz, maximum frequency decreasing to 50.50 Hertz, completion of a Dispatch Improvement Study, training 45 staff, amending the Grid Code, upgrading/replacing governors and excitation systems on 15 generation units, installing EMS & Optimization software, enabling NLDC control on 15 generators, and installing 9 Dynamic Line Ratings (DLRs)."

"The activity targets increasing access to improved water services in Greater Conakry and enhancing the urban water utility (SEG)'s operational efficiency. Expected outcomes include at least 730,000 direct beneficiaries (51.7% female) with enhanced water supply services, and 20,000 with access to improved water sources by FY22. SEG's operational efficiency will improve, aiming for a working ratio under 1, and Non-Revenue Water will reduce from 42% to 30%. Water production capacity is targeted to increase by 86,000 m³/day. The project also aims to reduce WASH-related diseases, lower health costs, reduce missed work hours, and improve school attendance, especially for females."

"[\"Targeted outcomes include strengthening key public sector institutions in Brazil's energy and mineral sectors to adapt to economic growth, sustainability, globalization, and technological change. This involves improving institutional capacity for regulatory adjustments, enhancing planning, and modernizing infrastructure, particularly information systems and technology. The activity aims to foster economically, environmentally, and socially sustainable growth, improve public sector management, strengthen regulatory frameworks, enhance long-term planning, mainstream sustainability, and support South-South partnerships.\",\"To be considered an overall success, the activity must achieve its Project Development Objective: \\\"to strengthen the capacity of key public sector institutions to improve the contribution of energy and mineral resources to accelerated national economic growth and increased social and environmental sustainability in a context of globalization and technological change.\\\" This entails: creating and utilizing 9 new databases and 6 new methodologies; conducting 3 training programs; establishing/restructuring 2 key entities; developing/deploying 3 power transmission technologies and 4 geophysical technologies. Additionally, intermediate results contributing to overall success include strengthening government capacity for sustainable development, reinforcing regulatory institutions, advancing technology development, and enhancing South-South cooperation.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Shandong Ecological Afforestation Project are to demonstrate effective afforestation models for environmentally degraded areas and to support Shandong's broader environmental afforestation programs. Specifically, the project aims to re-vegetate 38,000 hectares of degraded mountainous areas by restoring them into dense, multi-story vegetation to reduce soil and water erosion and diversify the landscape. It also targets establishing 28,000 hectares of protection forest plantations on saline coastal areas to protect agricultural land, improve productivity, and stabilize newly created alluvial land, increasing vegetative cover and creating windbreaks. Additionally, the project aims to enhance propagation technology, improve knowledge of environmental afforestation techniques, and establish an operational Monitoring and Evaluation system for learning and scaling up. The project would be considered an overall success if the following targets are met by the end of the project: in degraded mountainous areas, an increase in vegetative cover to 60%, an increase in plant species to 30, and a 20% decrease in soil erosion from baseline levels. In saline coastal areas, an increase in vegetative cover to 55% and an increase in plant species to 15, along with a 15% decrease in wind velocity from baseline levels."

"The activity targets include reducing average journey time for imported containers (e.g., to 5 hours from Songwe/Kasumulu to Lilongwe) and average truck border crossing time (e.g., TZ border to 12 hours, MZ border to 5 hours). It also aims to reduce vehicle operating costs on Karonga-Songwe road by 15%, total time at police roadblocks and border crossings, and accident black spots. Success requires improving road infrastructure condition, corridor management, and increasing beneficiaries of new/improved health facilities/HIV clinics, ultimately enhancing trade and movement along the North-South Corridor."

"[\"Conserve/manage Atsimo-Andrefana biodiversity, strengthening government & civil society capacity against private sector threats. Institutionalize multi-stakeholder platforms; establish ORBE for early warnings, CCAs, sustainable activities, land tenure, and pollution protection. Improve gender equality in land use planning. Mainstream biodiversity into land use planning (national/regional/community). Develop BDLUP system, spatial tools, WebGIS/LUP Toolbox for data-driven decisions, integrating conservation guidelines. Create Geocatalog and document management for stakeholder data sharing.\",\"Activity success hinges on effective biodiversity conservation in Atsimo-Andrefana. Institutions/civil society must effectively counter private sector threats. A functional Multi-Stakeholder Platform & ORBE must be operational, delivering early warnings, securing land tenure, and protecting livelihoods. Gender equality must improve. Biodiversity/sustainability must be demonstrably mainstreamed into all land use planning, with threats addressed. BDLUP system, WebGIS/LUP Toolbox, Geocatalog, and document management must be fully operational and adopted for data-driven decisions and stakeholder collaboration, ensuring conservation objectives are met and private sector integrates mitigation.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Third Phase of the Central Asia Regional Links Program (CARs-3) are to increase regional connectivity and support sustainable tourism development in Issyk-Kul Oblast. To consider the activity an overall success, the following specific targets must be achieved by 2025:

**For increased regional connectivity:**
1.  An increase in the number of vehicles passing through the Kyrgyz-Kazakh Karkyra border crossing point from a baseline of 7,000 to 8,500.
2.  Compliance with aviation safety standards and recommended practices, as measured by USOAP, reaching 55% of the global ICAO average, up from a baseline of 47%.
3.  Rehabilitation of 52 kilometers of roads in Issyk-Kul Oblast.
4.  Road maintenance equipment being in use.
5.  Software and hardware being in use by the Civil Aviation Agency (CAA) for record-keeping.
6.  85% implementation of the CAA training plan, as measured by ICAO USOAP, up from 0%.
7.  15% of women participating in the CAA training plan, up from 2%.
8.  Improvements made to facilities and curriculum at the Kyrgyz Aviation Institute.
9.  A flight training device being operational.

**For supported sustainable tourism development in Issyk-Kul Oblast:**
1.  A 10% increase in community satisfaction with sustainable tourism, up from a baseline of 0%.
2.  Development of a Sustainable Tourism Strategy for Issyk-Kul Oblast.
3.  Establishment of Tourism Satellite Accounts for Issyk-Kul Oblast.
4.  500 beneficiaries participating in community-led tourism development in Tyup and Aksuu districts.
5.  250 women beneficiaries participating in community-led tourism development in Tyup and Aksuu districts.
6.  Construction or rehabilitation of 5 Tourist Service Centers."

"Targeted outcomes include enhancing livelihoods of small forest producers and promoting sustainable management of forest landscapes. Specific targets by Year 5 are: 50,000 inhabitants of forest communities receiving increased monetary/non-monetary benefits from forests; 8% of FNECBN resources allocated to small forest producers (up from a 4% reference); 50% of recipient households having better access to basic services (water, electricity, improved stoves); and 2,000 small forest producers reporting improved access to markets. The project also aims to improve overall forest management."

"Targeted outcomes include upgrading 205.6km of Shaying River to Class IV for 500 dwt vessels year-round, increasing capacity by 135%, boosting cargo throughput, reducing transport costs (e.g., RMB117.0 million coal savings from Anhui by 2030), and achieving environmental benefits like saving 111,034 tonnes of diesel and avoiding 353,787 tonnes of carbon emissions by 2030, alongside land restoration. Success hinges on realizing these navigation standards, capacity utilization (e.g., aggregate traffic volumes and 500 dwt vessel percentages), reduced costs, and environmental targets, as well as effective technical assistance and achieving an EIRR of 14.58%."

"Targeted outcomes include fast-tracking readiness for post-2020 GBF implementation by aligning national targets, NBSAPs, policy, monitoring, and finance frameworks. Specific outcomes are GBF/SDG-aligned NBSAPs, enhanced monitoring systems, identified policy coherence actions, and defined biodiversity finance gaps with resource mobilization plans. Overall success requires all participating countries to achieve endorsed GBF-aligned national plans, updated NBSAPs and targets, strengthened monitoring systems, initiated policy alignment exercises, and developed finance mobilization plans, reaching 10,000 beneficiaries per country. Ultimate success is improved GBF implementation readiness for global biodiversity recovery by 2030."

"The targeted outcome is to demonstrate a model for agricultural sector restructuring and modernization, improved organizational arrangements, and strengthened public service delivery in Project Counties. Success will be achieved when: 60% of cooperatives make a profit (by YR5); RMB 290 million Net Present Value is generated by project investments (by YR5); 80% of poor households join new/improved cooperatives (by YR4/5); and RMB 100 million in private enterprise investments are leveraged (by YR5)."

"Targeted outcomes: Increase agricultural productivity of organized farmers in targeted marshlands/hillsides sustainably and strengthen women/men's participation in market-based value chains. Includes expanding irrigation (6,000 ha), promoting sustainable land management (17,000 ha), improving economic infrastructure, and diversifying activities. Success criteria: Marshland productivity to $1,375/ha; hillside productivity to $1,038/ha; 90% commercialized products for both genders in marshlands; 60% for hillsides; 6,206 beneficiaries (42% female); 90% adoption of sustainable land management by both genders."

"The targeted outcomes of the Second Municipal Services Improvement Project (MSIP2) are to improve transparency, financial sustainability, and inclusive delivery of targeted municipal services in the participating municipalities. For the activity to be considered an overall success, it would need to achieve the following: 100,000 direct project beneficiaries, with 50% of these being female beneficiaries. Additionally, 10% of the vulnerable/marginalized population (measured as unemployed, social assistance recipients, or otherwise vulnerable) should benefit from the project intervention. Success also includes 20 participating municipalities regularly publishing subproject progress information on their official websites or elsewhere. Furthermore, 90% of completed subprojects should generate improved financial performance through increased revenue earnings or cost savings, and 17 completed subprojects should specifically focus on social inclusion."

"The activity targets improving infrastructure and investment support services in Linshui County Town and Qianfeng District Town. Success is defined by: 1. Annual direct investment in Linshui ETDZ reaching 3.33 billion RMB by 2020. 2. 5,956 trainees securing jobs after skills training by 2019 (targeting low-income/poverty, gender disaggregation). 3. 600 daily green traffic persons on Linshui corridors by 2019. 4. 70% of wastewater collected and treated in Qianfeng District Town by 2020. Overall success also requires meeting infrastructure development (roads, sewers, wastewater capacity), sanitation access for 275,000 residents, and institutional strengthening targets."

"The project targets strengthening Fortaleza's capacity for land-use planning and urban financing, and improving the urban environment by rehabilitating public spaces in the Maritime Slope Basin and Parque Rachel de Queiroz. Overall success requires: Increased own-source revenue generation (e.g., 20% from property taxes, 100% via Fortaleza Online, 115% via SEUMA urban instruments); preparation/structuring of Consorciada Rachel de Queiroz urban revitalization; reaching 305,628 direct beneficiaries (53% women); 80% of western Maritime Slope sampling points exhibiting adequate dry season bathing water quality; and achieving a 32% return and $85M in net benefits, with total benefits double the costs."

"The targeted outcomes are: 6,000,000 people (3,000,000 women) gaining access to improved piped water; 20 PDAMs graduating to a higher NUWAS category. Success requires: 1.2M new piped connections (240K for poor households); 40 LGs providing improved financing; 20 LGs/PDAMs receiving matching grants; 200 LGs/PDAMs in TA/capacity building; 50 PDAMs achieving full cost recovery; 40 PDAMs with updated business plans; 40 PDAMs showing improved customer satisfaction; NUWAS Framework operationalized; key guidelines on sector financing/utility reform adopted; and 100% grievance resolution within standards."

"The activity targets enhancing silvicultural management, reforestation, and afforestation, increasing felling residue use, and improving public good contributions from forests for sustainable resource use, employment, and carbon sequestration. Specific outcomes include increased thinned forest areas, enhanced SFE economic performance, greater high-quality seedling production capacity, increased utilizable harvested timber volume, and quantifiable carbon sequestration. Success requires: 165,000 Ha thinned; 145 female trainees; supported forest policy reforms; 11 government institutions capacitated; 4,500,000 Ha biodiversity-friendly managed; project reports published; 25,000 direct beneficiaries (10% female); SFE economic performance of $15,826,000; 4,000,000 seedlings produced; and 35.00 m3/ha utilizable timber harvested. Additionally, successfully mitigating procurement risks and having no adverse social impacts are key."

"The activity targets increasing access to piped water and improved sanitation, strengthening rural service delivery, and bolstering institutional capacity. Overall success requires, by Year 6: 740,600 direct beneficiaries; 529,300 people gaining improved water access (105,200 urban; 424,100 rural/estates); 172,000 people gaining improved sanitation (rural/estates); 100% rural M&E systems established; and diarrheal disease reduced by 45% (urban) and 60% (rural/estates), alongside an increase in fully functioning CBOs and 95% grievance redressal."

"The Competitiveness and Sector Diversification activity targets: 50% of mining concessions subject to formal inspections following international practices; a 15% revenue increase for benefiting SMEs, including women-owned/majority female employee firms; and 14 regulatory or policy reforms adopted to strengthen governance and competitiveness. Success hinges on achieving these PDO indicators, alongside producing 7 mining function SOPs and training 35 government staff. This includes strengthening mining frameworks, establishing a Minerals Institute, and supporting 230 SMEs via SURGE with business services and grants."

"The targeted outcomes of the Saint Lucia Disaster Vulnerability Reduction Project include: reducing interrupted traffic due to climate events in project areas to 5 days; increasing the number of schools, health centers, and emergency shelters with reduced vulnerability to 8; ensuring climate risk analysis is reflected in transport and drainage infrastructure design; installing and activating meteorological and hydrological monitoring networks; completing LiDAR mapping of the entire country; connecting 8 government ministries/agencies to a spatial data sharing platform; training 50 government officials in spatial data management; fully disbursing 100% of the Climate Adaptation Financing Facility (CAFF) portfolio, with 51% female borrowers; rehabilitating 0.5 kilometers of non-rural roads; producing 4 official policies/strategies referencing climate change-related Disaster Risk Management (DRM); and directly benefiting 169,000 individuals. The activity would be considered an overall success if these specific targets are met, alongside a significant reduction in Saint Lucia's overall vulnerability to natural hazards and climate change, demonstrated by the project's projected economic feasibility with benefits three times its costs and a 21 percent rate of return."

"The targeted outcomes of the Nepal: Power Sector Reform and Sustainable Hydropower Development (PSRSHD) activity are to strengthen the capacity of power sector agencies to plan and prepare hydropower and transmission line projects following international standards and best practices, and to improve these agencies' readiness for regulatory and institutional reforms. Success will be measured by the preparation of 360 MW of hydropower projects, 150 km of transmission line projects, and the validation and endorsement of 5 key sector policy and reform proposals by institutions like the MoE, NEA, MoEST, and WECS."

"The Paraiba Sustainable Rural Development activity targets three main outcomes: improving access to water, reducing agroclimatic vulnerability, and expanding market access for Paraíba's poor rural population. Specifically, the project aims to ensure 79,440 people in rural areas gain access to potable water sources, have 64,151 clients adopt improved agricultural technologies (with a disaggregated target of 19,245 clients), and achieve a 20% increase in the average gross value of producers' sales. The activity would be considered an overall success once these final targets are achieved."

"The activity targets include reducing electricity losses in the rural power distribution network from 13.0% (2013) to 10.5% by 2020, saving 277 GWh annually. It also aims to enhance system capacity by adding 1,800 MVA in the 33/11KV network by 2020, enabling 3,329 GWh/year additional consumption. Socio-economic benefits include displacing polluting fuels and fostering income-generating activities. Environmental targets involve avoiding 4.68 million tons of carbon emissions. The project also targets maintaining improved service quality for 25 million beneficiaries, including 56% female, and supporting utility management."

"The BRAZIL BAHIA SUSTAINABLE RURAL DEVELOPMENT PROJECT targets increasing market integration, net revenues, and food security of organized beneficiaries, and improving access to water supply and sanitation services. Success would entail: 600 formal contracts integrating POs into markets (180 with female participation); 10% increase in real net revenue for beneficiary POs (0.30% for female beneficiaries); average vulnerable household dietary diversity indicator reaching 30 (0.30 for female beneficiaries); 350 new piped household water connections (30 for female beneficiaries); 17,000 people gaining access to improved sanitation (5,100 from female-led households). Additionally, approximately 56,200 direct project beneficiaries (30% female), 20% of total beneficiaries being vulnerable/marginalized, 750 business plans implemented (225 female-led), 20% increase in yearly sales volume, 650 social/environmental subprojects, 550 clients adopting improved agricultural technology (165 female), 750 POs/CAs implementing financial management instruments (225 female-led), 170 basic WSS investments sustainably operated, and 10,000 people trained."

"Targeted outcomes: improved sustainable landscape management and access to water supply/sanitation in targeted river basins, addressing erosion, pollution, siltation, and floods. Success requires 3,375 Ha under sustainable landscape management; 5,200 farmers (1,300 female) adopting improved agricultural technology; 45,560 people (21,413 female) with improved water sources; and 22,000 people (10,340 female) with improved sanitation. Also, 93,901 direct beneficiary families (327,713 people), an EIRR of 36.1% (US$181M total economic benefits, incl. 4.7M tCO2e carbon sequestration). Capacity strengthening for INDRHI, dam rehabilitation, and improved irrigation are crucial."

"The targeted outcomes are to strengthen integrated landscape management in selected areas and increase benefits to communities in targeted landscapes. Broader objectives include reduced environmental impact, sustainable natural resource management and use, protection/conservation/restoration of biodiversity and ecosystems, improved livelihoods for excluded/discriminated groups, and enhanced gender equality. The activity would be considered an overall success upon achieving: 500 hectares under sustainable landscape management practices; 50,000 people benefiting from these practices; and net greenhouse gas emissions avoided or sequestered."

"The targeted outcomes of the Support for Strategic Local Development and Investment Project are to improve local public service provision and management by enhancing Local Government Unit (LGU) access to viable financing for strategic infrastructure investments and strengthening their governance and management capacity. This includes facilitating an increase in the number of households benefiting from improved access to basic social, economic, and environmental infrastructure services, and boosting the proportion of capital expenditure to total expenditure among participating LGUs. Additionally, the project aims to support LGU revenue generation through improved real property and business tax mapping, codification, and automation of tax assessment, billing, and collection processes, as well as enhancing financial management and reporting for municipal enterprises and public service utilities. The project also intends to address key bottlenecks in urban infrastructure development and promote city-level urban governance reforms, specifically by building LGU capacity in pre- and implementation activities for local investments, enhancing civic participation and transparency, improving procurement and financial management, developing long-term urban development strategies, and fostering inter-LGU collaborations. Projects will encompass a wide range of infrastructure and services, including water supply and sanitation, solid waste management, wastewater treatment, housing, roads, bridges, drainage, flood protection, mini-hydropower, schools, health clinics, and improvements to municipal enterprises.

To consider the activity an overall success, the following specific criteria must be met:

1.  **Households Benefited:** At least 100,000 households must benefit from improved access to basic social, economic, and environmental infrastructure services in participating LGUs by the end of the project.
2.  **Capital Expenditure:** There must be an increase in the proportion of infrastructure investment to total expenditure among participating LGUs by the end of the project.
3.  **Sub-project Completion (Component 1):** At least 50 sub-projects must be physically complete by the end of the project, including those with revenue-enhancing measures. Additionally, 100% of planned market stalls and slaughterhouses, along with designed lengths of bridges, should be completed as designed in participating LGUs.
4.  **Loan Portfolio Quality (Component 1):** The non-performing loan of Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP)'s LGU portfolio must remain low, specifically below 2% by Year 5.
5.  **LGU Capacity Improvement (Component 2):** At least 20 LGUs should demonstrate improved capacity in conducting pre- and implementation activities for local investments by Year 5. This includes 100% of targeted participating LGUs receiving training and assistance.
6.  **Project Management Office (PMO) & Project Implementation Units (PIUs) (Component 2):** The Project Management Office must be established and fully functional before project start, and 100% of participating LGUs must have their PIUs set up and functioning by Year 5.
7.  **Operational Systems (Component 2):** The Project Operations Manual, financial system, and Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) system, along with their corresponding manuals, must be completed, disseminated, updated, and adhered to before sub-project starts and throughout the project life."

"The Jakarta Urgent Flood Mitigation Project aims to improve the operation and maintenance of priority flood management sections, benefiting vulnerable populations. By Year 5, targeted outcomes include reducing inundated area water levels from 70 cm to 11 cm, water logging from over 24 hours to under 24 hours, and inundated area extent from 100% to 20%. This involves removing 3.4 million m³ of dredge material, adding 596,740 million m³ storage, repairing 42.2 km of embankments, and maintaining 25 km of canals. Success hinges on sustained political commitment, effective institutional coordination, and implementation of subsequent flood mitigation measures."

"The targeted outcomes of the Integrated Modern Agriculture Development Project are to develop sustainable and climate-resilient agricultural production systems in selected areas across Gansu, Hunan, Jiangxi, and Liaoning provinces, the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous region, and Chongqing municipality. This involves enhancing agriculture and water productivity and climate resilience, increasing farmer incomes, reducing climate vulnerability, and strengthening institutional capacity.Specific targets by the end of the project include: 94,000 ha of farmland served with improved irrigation and drainage services; increased water productivity for major crops (e.g., maize, wheat, rice) in participating provinces, with specific targets ranging from 1.27 to 1.90 kg/m3; increased agricultural productivity (e.g., maize, wheat, rice) in participating provinces, with specific targets ranging from 5899 to 9591 kg/ha; and 46,000 farmers becoming members of farmer organizations (Water User Associations, Farmer Associations, or Farmer Cooperatives).To consider the activity an overall success, there must be an effective customization of interventions tailored to diverse local economic and agro-climatic conditions across the participating regions, coupled with robust institutional support."

"The activity targets improving energy efficiency in Chinese enterprises, reducing global environmental impacts by scaling commercial lending for EE investments. Key outcomes include: US$1,000 million in accumulated EE and renewable commercial investments, 2.469 million tons of coal equivalent in annual energy savings, and 6.098 million tons of CO2 equivalent in annual GHG reductions by YR5. US$100 million cumulative IBRD loan on-lending for EE. Minsheng Bank's capacity strengthened in identifying, evaluating, and managing EE projects, including environmental/social risks, through 8 training courses, a risk assessment/control system, a carbon finance study, developing a low-carbon lending business, and exploring Equator Principles."

"The targeted outcomes of the Poyang Lake Basin Town Water Environment Management Project are to reduce pollutant discharge into selected waterways, improve water quality management in specific counties in the Poyang Lake basin within Jiangxi Province, and reduce the overall pollution load discharged into Poyang Lake. The project also aims to improve public health and living conditions in seven selected counties and enhance biodiversity conservation, particularly for migratory bird habitats. Specific outcomes include improving solid waste collection and transportation in rural and urban areas of Shangli, Duchang, Poyang, Yugan, and Jing'an counties, which is expected to reduce pollution, enhance the local environment, improve public health, and potentially increase tourism and property values. Furthermore, the project intends to rehabilitate and expand urban and rural wastewater collection networks, including household connections, in Duchang, Jing'an, and Jishui Counties to significantly cut discharges of pollutants such as BOD, COD, suspended solids, nitrogen, and phosphorus into the lake and local groundwater, leading to improved local environment (reduced odors), better public health (reduced exposure to untreated wastewater and waterborne diseases), and economic gains (increased tourism, investment, and property values). Additionally, the project aims to establish and make operational a Lake Management Platform and implement a participatory water quality management system in relevant counties."

"The Transport Rehabilitation Project aims to improve the Serbian Road Directorate's (SRD) effectiveness in road maintenance and rehabilitation. Key outcomes include rehabilitating 240 km of highways, introducing competitive private sector contracting for routine/winter maintenance in Macva and Kolubara, and strengthening SRD's institutional capacity, planning, and procurement. Success is defined by improved maintenance systems, lower costs, better quality/safety in rehabilitation, and increased SRD capability. Specific targets by Dec 2008 include reducing average road roughness in pilot areas to 4 m/km and rehabilitated segments to 3 m/km, reducing road deaths to 5 per 10,000 vehicles, and implementing 6 competitively awarded contracts. Overall success requires all sections to have a positive NPV and EIRR exceeding 12%."

"The targeted outcomes of the Cambodia Southeast Asia Disaster Risk Management Project are to improve climate-resilient rural road connectivity in select provinces and enhance Cambodia's resilience to natural hazard impacts. Specifically, the project aims to achieve: 340,000 beneficiaries with all-season rural road access (51% women); public investment planning that incorporates disaster and climate risks; and for Cambodia to have the information and capacity to access sovereign disaster risk insurance. Intermediate targets include strengthening 290 km of rural roads, connecting 300 schools and health facilities, developing a rural road maintenance plan incorporating disaster vulnerability, and 60% beneficiary satisfaction with road conditions, safety, and climate. The project is considered an overall success when these end targets are met, alongside the broader goal of improved disaster response and continued service delivery due to enhanced resilience against fiscal shocks."

"Targeted outcomes include enhancing electricity trade among Mali, Mauritania, and Senegal, aiming for an additional 147 GWh annually traded between Mali and Mauritania, and between Mali and Senegal. Other outcomes are reducing the average annual power outage duration on the RIMA system to 60 hours, increasing efficiency and supply of electricity, and providing technical assistance to SOGEM. Success requires achieving these trade and reliability targets, constructing 288 km of transmission lines (Kayes-Tambacounda line), implementing a SCADA system, addressing 100% of grievances, and demonstrating robust economic viability (EIRR of 24.95%, NPV of US$305 million)."

"Targeted outcomes: Secure, affordable power; commercialized power sector; functioning AD MEPSO; liberalized regional electricity market. Success: GDP growth via reliable/competitive power; minimal residential impact; reduced distribution losses; energy balance. APL3 project completion: Skopje 5 substation (≥50MW incremental supply, stable voltages), ≥20% cross-border trade (Macedonia-Greece), 80% fewer Skopje 1-Tetovo line outages, satisfactory EMS/planning software, ≥25% O&M cost reduction (unbundled substations), successful AD MEPSO FIMS deployment, compliant liberalized regional market."

"Targeted outcomes: Strengthen flood risk management and improve wastewater management in Vinh Phuc Province. Key outcomes: 376,065 direct beneficiaries (50.8% female); 255,130 people benefiting from flood control; 120,935 with improved sanitation; 5,720 hectares (68.2% surface area) protected from 10-year floods; US$35.2M annual flood damages averted; US$71.85/capita health/productivity losses averted; 258 tons/year BOD removed. Success requires achieving these outcomes, constructing 38 wastewater treatment facilities, establishing an operational flood early warning system, approving an integrated water resource management protocol, and demonstrating economic viability with NPV of US$246.3M and IRR of 16.44%."

"The activity targets: (i) establishing a strategic framework for national water resource management in Drini-Buna and Semani basins, including IWRM policy/strategy, RBM plans, and a Water Resources Database; (ii) sustainably improving irrigation system performance with 75% compliance in water distribution and 70% O&M cost recovery. It also aims to rehabilitate 14 dams, serve 35,000 water users (4,000 female) over 35,000 hectares, and increase net family income by 37.5%-108% and labor returns to $45-$54/day."

"Targeted outcomes include reducing auto travel times (e.g., Fanchuanpu: 20+2 mins to 14 mins; Yi Ming: 43 mins to 30 mins; Nike: 25 mins to 19 mins) and public transport travel times (e.g., Fanchuanpu: 46 mins to 35 mins; Nike: 35 mins to 32 mins). Institutional outcomes involve adopting Strategic Planning TA, integrating land use/transport planning, increasing public transport coverage, and using a GIS-based land value monitoring system. Overall success requires achieving these targets, maintaining economic viability with an EIRR >= 12% and positive NPV, and delivering benefits like reduced congestion and time savings, supporting sustainable development."

"Targeted outcomes include increasing access to reliable water (at least 16 hours/day, 7 days/week) and improved sanitation services for Greater Managua's population. This involves benefiting 130 low-income neighborhoods (at least 50% of beneficiaries from the two poorest quintiles), improving health, fostering community ownership, and strengthening ENACAL's financial, technical, environmental, and social sustainability. Overall success requires: 1) An additional 150,000 beneficiaries with reliable water supply; 2) An additional 120,000 beneficiaries with improved sanitation; and 3) ENACAL's cost recovery ratio reaching 1.00 (from 0.81 baseline) by project completion (YR5). Additionally, 70% operational micro-metering in targeted areas and 40,000 connections with daily 16-hour service."

"Targeted outcomes: 100% urban water coverage; 60% Timbiqui, 40% El Charco sewerage; 1910 new, 1080 rehabilitated water connections; 45% waterborne disease reduction; 5-7 docks built with 5 maintenance; 100% navigation signals, 25 charts; reduced passenger waiting (for 25% demand), increased vessel speed, 17% operational cost reduction; 75% licensed waterway operators; 80 operators trained; 20% women employed; 70% user satisfaction; 75% women waterway access; WSS models. Success requires meeting these, achieving 17% WSS IRR, 15.7% waterway EIRR, community involvement, and risk management."

"The Yemen Desert Locust Response Project targets controlling the desert locust outbreak, supporting livelihoods, and strengthening Yemen's preparedness for future infestations. Specific outcomes include restoring 90% of affected pasture/rangeland and agricultural land productivity, supporting 1,500 affected households (1,200 female) via social safety nets, and enabling 80% of locust-affected farmers (including female-headed farms) to renew agricultural activity. It also aims to develop a national locust control plan, establish a functioning early warning system, and operationalize 5 Desert Locust Control Centers. The project aims to survey 400,000 Ha and spray 200,000 Ha for locust control."

"The NIGER COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM PHASE 3 targets several key outcomes. Its Project Development Objectives (PDO) are to strengthen the Recipient's local development planning and implementation capacities, including the ability to respond promptly and effectively to eligible crises, and to improve the access of the targeted population to socio-economic services. The Global Environment Objective (GEO) is to promote sustainable land management and productive investments at the commune level in Niger, contributing to poverty reduction, improved food security, and increased income for rural producers. Specific targeted outcomes include: increasing land area with Sustainable Land and Water Management (SLWM) practices, improving vegetation cover, enhancing the adaptive capacity of targeted institutions to climate variability, and increasing carbon accumulation rates in biomass and soil. The program also aims for improved management effectiveness of protected areas, sustainably managed landscapes and seascapes that integrate biodiversity, improved agricultural management, and the adoption of integrated landscape management practices by local communities. Furthermore, it seeks to improve good management practices in existing forests and encourage the adoption of LULUCF practices to reduce forest degradation and deforestation. Other outcomes include building the capacities of local governments (Communes and Regions) and national institutions, supporting investments for sustainable land management, creating/maintaining socio-economic infrastructures, and diversifying income-generating activities, while reducing local vulnerabilities to mis-governance and ensuring gender-balanced participation in decision-making and access to resources. The focus on socio-economic infrastructure and income-generating activities will be complemented by a renewed emphasis on land and environment and natural resources management.  

To be considered an overall success, the activity would need to achieve the following by Year 4 (as outlined in the Result Framework and Monitoring):  
*   85% of newly targeted Communes will have defined and implemented good governance practices (participation, financial accountability, and social equity).  
*   100% of targeted Communes will be enabled to sustain the proper operation and maintenance of local development investments.  
*   An additional 60,000 hectares of land will be under Sustainable Land and Water Management (SLWM) and Sustainable Forest Management (SFM) practices.  
*   90% of newly targeted farming households will have adopted sustainable agro-sylvo-pastoral practices and technology promoted by the project.  
*   3,000,000 direct project beneficiaries will be reached, with 50% being female.  
*   Funds will be made available within 4 weeks when requested by the Government for an eligible crisis or emergency.  
*   Additionally, specific sub-component targets include: 60% of targeted Communes' planning and AIP sessions being public; 75% of targeted Communes timely preparing annual financial reports; 90% of Commune micro-projects integrating gender equity; 65% of Communes having created and operationalized grievance mechanisms; 60% of targeted Communes being effectively interested in inter-communal collaboration; 60% of representatives of key Regional agencies having strengthened planning capacities; 90% of representatives of deconcentrated line departments having strengthened local development-related capacities; 80% of targeted Communes utilizing at least 80% of their investment allocations; 70% of targeted Communes having protected and/or restored at least 200 hectares of land; 80% of beneficiaries having increased their income by 30% due to farm and off-farm jobs; 90% of populations in newly targeted Communes having improved access to health/nutrition services and education; 50% of targeted inter-governmental initiatives having been implemented; and 80% of targeted knowledge and communication products having been prepared and disseminated."

"The project aims to improve highway asset management efficiency and cost-effectiveness in Yunnan. Outcomes include a 10% increase in budget utilization, annual savings (RMB81.26M from postponed periodical maintenance, RMB290.51M from enhanced maintenance/reduced VOC). Cost-effective technologies target longer rehabilitation intervals (10-15 years), 40% cost reduction (RMB93.27M/time), and decreased VOC (RMB42.91M/year). By 2020, targets are >60% national/provincial highways in good/fair condition, 75% maintenance funds allocated via asset management system, and 4.8 Km/staff. Intermediate outcomes involve establishing an integrated asset management system, enhancing all maintenance/emergency centers, reducing emergency response time to 3 hours, and training nearly 5,000 staff by 2018. The project expects an EIRR of 16.1% and ENPV of RMB442.54M. It benefits 46M people through reduced transport costs, improved access/safety, and job creation, covering 88% poverty counties."

"Targeted outcomes: 100% eligible families with land tenure security, 90% public infrastructure/services provided, 70% client satisfaction with agricultural services. Also, 5,141 individuals with recorded land rights, 80% land under productive agriculture, 5,000 ha sustainable land management, and HFIAS index reduces to 50. Success means land recipients sustainably improve welfare, escape poverty, and participate in local development, with disaster/climate risk mitigation via water/irrigation and improved nutrition through gender-sensitive support, ensuring sustainability."

"The targeted outcomes include the development of integrated river basin management (IRBM) for the Ayeyarwady River Basin, aiming for climate-resilient management. Specific targets are: completion of a River Basin Master Plan and two preparation studies; 90% of hydro-met stations functioning per SOP and producing three hydro-met products; 90% navigation improvements and a detailed navigation design. Additionally, the project aims to reduce disaster-related asset losses (e.g., US$8.5 million annually) and enhance economic productivity in climate-sensitive sectors (e.g., US$19.4-194 million annually), ultimately saving lives."

"The activity targets improving governance and strengthening capacity in CAR's forest and mining sectors. Outcomes include enhanced fiscal and governance frameworks, fighting illegal logging, promoting conservation, sustainable forest management, and increasing carbon stocks. It also aims to assist forest communities with local development, increase climate change awareness, foster low carbon practices, and formalize artisanal mining. Success entails 11 forest communities accessing local development packages, reducing diamond certification time from 90 to 30 days, 30 registered artisanal mining entities receiving grants, establishing an international best practice mining regulatory framework, an operational national forest taxation information system, and strengthening forest administration capacity."

"The activity targets: (i) strengthening integrated water resources management, with an annual Paraiba river water management plan and 83 operational hydromet stations; (ii) improving water service reliability in Agreste/Borborema regions, providing piped water for 328 days/year to 235,000 beneficiaries (water over 80% of days/year); and (iii) enhancing operational efficiency in João Pessoa Metropolitan Region, reducing non-revenue water by 17 million m³/year and BOD pollution by 9,600 tons/year. Success requires meeting these targets, achieving 80% beneficiary satisfaction, and resolving 80% of grievances."

"Targeted outcomes aim to reduce isolation for 250,000 rural residents, granting year-round access to markets/services. This involves cutting transport rates and access times by 30%, and essential commodity prices by 15%. Outputs include paving 200 km of intermediate and improving 75 km of village access roads, establishing 3 functional decentralized rural road management offices, training 200 local professionals, and ensuring 950 km of roads are properly maintained with a functional Pavement Management System."

"The targeted outcomes of the Vietnam Scaling up Urban Upgrading Project are to improve access to infrastructure in priority city areas and enhance urban planning in participating cities. Specifically, it aims to strengthen integrated planning capacity in seven cities (Bac Lieu, Ben Tre, Long Xuyen, Soc Trang, Tan An, Vi Thanh, and Vinh Long), improve connectivity of priority infrastructure in urban cores, upgrade selected Low Income Areas (LIAs) to benefit the urban poor, foster urban resilience, and ensure technical and economic soundness. The project also intends to provide technical assistance to local governments for urban planning, land management, and city resilience, and to integrate climate and disaster risks into investment designs, including steering urban growth to less hazardous areas and incorporating low carbon development principles. Other outcomes include increasing drainage capacity, preserving green spaces for water retention, improving public health through better drainage, sanitation, and waste management, and promoting citizen engagement, gender inclusion, and capacity building for risk-informed urban planning.To consider the activity an overall success, the following end targets must be met: 500,000 people provided with improved living conditions (including female users and the bottom 40 percent), 90,000 people with access to improved basic urban infrastructure facilities and services in targeted LIAs (including female users and the bottom 40 percent), an 80% increase in user satisfaction with basic infrastructure quality (including female users and the bottom 40 percent), 30 LIAs with Community Upgrading Plans prepared and implemented, and 3 cities with revised master plans developed and approved. Additionally, 90,000 direct beneficiaries in LIAs should have enhanced protection from rainfall flooding. Infrastructure-specific targets include 60 km of new or improved lanes in LIAs, 19 km of new or rehabilitated drainage canals in LIAs, 15,000 households with new or improved sewer connections, 48 km of new or improved primary or secondary roads, 32 km of rehabilitated or newly constructed drainage, 29 km of new or rehabilitated canal embankment designed with 'green infrastructure principles,' 310 new serviced plots constructed, and 380 serviced plots provided to relocated households. Economically, the project must achieve an Economic Internal Rate of Return exceeding the 10% economic opportunity cost of capital, and provincial people's committees must maintain fiscal compliance regarding debt repayment and overall debt limits."

"The targeted outcomes of the \"Innovation in Solar Power and Hybrid Technologies\" activity are to demonstrate the operational and economic feasibility of utility-scale innovative renewable energy technologies and battery energy storage solutions, and to strengthen institutional capacity for their commercial scale-up in India.Specific end targets include constructing 400 MW of renewable energy capacity (totaling 764,550 MWh generated) and avoiding 510,000 tons of CO2 emissions annually. Institutional capacity strengthening aims to demonstrate hybrid, floating solar PV, and battery energy storage solutions at least once, issue relevant regulations/standards, develop a career path study for women in SECI, and increase women's representation in SECI's total workforce to 30% and in technical departments to 23%.Overall success hinges on several factors: the economic viability of BESS reaching viability at specific cost thresholds (e.g., US$236/kWh with externalities), robust Economic Internal Rate of Return (ERR) for subprojects (13.5-27.4%), and financial viability (e.g., FIRR of 12.2% exceeding WACC). Success also requires SECI's ability to attract competitive bids, active stakeholder participation, private investors acting on enhanced knowledge, regulators applying improved policies, and SECI's strengthened capacity for implementation. The project aims to de-risk the sector by absorbing first-mover risks and facilitating financing for these technologies."

"[\"The activity targets improving urban mobility and job accessibility in North Lima. Outcomes include reducing public transport travel time (Chimpu Ocllo-Naranjal) from 31 to 17 mins; 60,000 daily passenger trips; increasing low-income passengers to 42% and women passengers to 49% of total validations; and growing jobs reachable within 60 mins from 156,595 to 165,990. Also, 10% of women shifting from informal transport modes.\", \"Overall success requires achieving 100% completion of 10.2 km of segregated BRT lanes, 17 passenger stations (all accessible), and 3 U-turns; 65% user satisfaction (10% increase for women on safety/security); 20% women employment in BRT; implementing environmental measures, a sexual harassment protocol, and a Gender Action Plan. Economic viability with NPV of US$75.43M, EIRR of 11.52%, and C/B ratio of 1.53 is also key.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes: improved rural living conditions (infrastructure, income, sustainable NRM) and reduced land degradation/climate change threats. Success requires 60% of targeted rural Imadas to achieve improved infrastructure/services, 60% of IGAs to generate positive income, and 45% of rural Imadas to have validated PDPs implemented via ACs. Additionally, 30% of ACs must include sustainable land management/climate adaptation, with a 20% reduction in soil loss from erosion. An Economic Rate of Return of 16% over 20 years is essential for overall success."

"Targeted outcomes: improved accuracy/timeliness of hydromet services in Central Asia (focus: Kyrgyz Republic, Tajikistan), strengthened public sector management, enhanced service delivery, mitigated natural disaster risks, increased regional cooperation. Success by Year 5: 72-hour weather forecast accuracy/timeliness: 85% (KgHM/TjHM), 90% (KzHM/UzHM). Seasonal river flow forecast accuracy: 85% (KgHM/TjHM), 90% (KzHM/UzHM). Daily NHMS data received/sent: 600Mb/70Mb. Fully functioning KgHM/TjHM regulations/O&M budgets.
