Additionally, key outputs must be delivered: six new transmission substations by 2020; rehabilitated distribution network with new feeders, lines, and transformers by 2021; enhanced NEA distribution center performance via smart meters and staff training by 2021; and NEA's capacity for smart grid management with GESI aspects developed by 2020.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include enhancing agriculture production systems and income-generating opportunities, improving access, stimulating agriculture productivity growth, and supporting non-farm employment for poor communities. Specific aims are improving market access, promoting improved irrigation and land management, and supporting post-harvest/value-added activities. Success would be achieving a 20% increase in agricultural productivity and at least a 15% increase in rural household income in participating geogs."

"The Kenya Coastal Development Project targets increased Government of Kenya fisheries revenue from foreign vessel licensing, improved sustainable management and regeneration of near-shore fisheries and ecosystems with community participation, support for sustainable alternative coastal livelihoods, and strengthened conservation and sustainable use of marine and coastal biodiversity. Overall success requires quantifiable increases in fisheries revenue, measurable improvements in resource health, effective community co-management, viable alternative livelihoods, enhanced GoK capacity for monitoring and surveillance of offshore fisheries, a robust legal framework, and integrated coastal management that reverses natural resource degradation."

"[\"The Mexico Forests and Climate Change Project targets supporting rural communities to sustainably manage their forests, build social organization, and generate additional income from forest products and services, including the Reduction of Emissions from Deforestation and Degradation (REDD+). This contributes to higher-level objectives of ensuring sustainable management, restoration, and expansion of Mexico's forest resources, promoting local socio-economic development, strengthening climate change resilience, and spearheading global REDD+ efforts.\",\"For the activity to be an overall success, it must achieve: 1) An increase in forest area under improved management and reduced carbon emissions practices. 2) An increase in communities building social organization and generating income from sustainable forest goods and services (including REDD+). 3) A reduction of net deforestation and forest degradation rates in selected REDD+ Early Action areas (measured in equivalent CO2 emissions). Intermediate steps include modernizing CONAFOR's M&E systems, harmonizing public policies, and piloting new governance models.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes include enhancing competitiveness (addressing contract enforcement, permits, finance access, MSME support), improving fiscal management (expenditure, revenue, wage bill, pensions, tax waivers, public enterprises, budget), reducing vulnerability and enhancing resilience (natural disasters, health, education, crime), and building foundations for growth and improved public services, job creation, and environmental management. Overall success requires sustained fiscal progress to restore investor confidence and resolve Jamaica's debt problem, alongside addressing rigid expenditure causes and ensuring an effective regulatory framework for sustainable development."

"The activity targets reconstructing damaged infrastructure and restoring public services (water, energy, transport, health, housing) in targeted Iraqi municipalities. Outcomes include repairing water/wastewater systems, roads/bridges for safe traffic, improving health services via mobile units, generating local employment, supporting local industries, fostering state/citizen trust, and designing a housing subsidy scheme. Technical assistance for urban development, infrastructure plans, and M&E is also targeted. Overall success requires adapting to volatile realities, effective two-way citizen communication with feedback, and meeting the crucial prerequisite of rubble removal, including EOD clearance."

"Targeted outcomes include: improved sector institutional performance (mainstreaming Fund Board, effective monitoring, performance-based budgeting, autonomous Fund Board, scalable RWSSP I delivery), enhanced health and hygiene (85% rural water coverage, suitable sanitation, reduced water collection time/increased girls' school enrollment/women's income, decreased child mortality), and full participation of marginalized groups. Success would involve Government mainstreaming the Fund Board approach, achieving 85% rural water coverage, reducing child mortality, and establishing sustainable, community-run WSS services widely."

"The targeted outcomes of the Irrigation & Drainage Rehabilitation Project (Serbia) include: (a) reducing the risk of damage to land, crops, property, and infrastructure from flooding, benefiting approximately 200,000 inhabitants; (b) increasing agricultural productivity through rehabilitation of drainage and irrigation infrastructure, benefiting around 350,000 hectares and leading to crop yield increases of 20-30% for affected field crops; (c) improving water resources management and strengthening associated institutions and policies, including institutional reform, capacity building, and upgrading the flood forecasting and warning system; (d) initiating and significantly contributing to agricultural regeneration; and (e) improving economic conditions in rural areas through increased incomes and fiscal benefits. Additionally, key outcomes involve the development of strategies and programs for future irrigation sub-sector development, flood control, drainage, environmental reclamation, and the finalization of legislation on Water User Associations (WUAs)."

"The activity targets increasing agricultural productivity and farmers' incomes, better protecting against floods, properties, and lives in predominantly rural areas of the Huai River Basin. It also aims to effectively reduce the severity and impact of flooding and water logging in Anhui, Henan, Jiangsu, and Shandong provinces."

"Targeted outcomes: improved transport sector management, lower user costs, increased safety/reliability of rehabilitated infrastructure. Specifics: ANAC meets ICAO standards; financially sound airport concession; Nouakchott Airport runway resurfaced. Maritime sub-sector reorganized (capacity, info system, updated regulations, port vision). Updated National Transport Strategy/Policy Document. GoM institutional reforms, coordination, PPP promotion. Enhanced road safety, rehabilitated key paved/rural roads. Environmental management capacity in MET. Success is achieving these, benefiting transport users."

"The activity targets reducing transport costs by improving Bulgaria's road network quality, enhancing Class I, II, & III roads, leading to lower vehicle operating costs, time savings, and improved road safety. It aims to boost NRIF's capacity to manage multi-year maintenance/rehabilitation programs via an improved road management system, ensuring efficient use of resources, including EU funds. Overall success requires good economic returns, sound maintenance practices, and critically, the successful, timely implementation and robust supervision of institutional development activities, particularly technical assistance."

"The activity aims to improve water supply and sanitation services through upgrading and reconstructing facilities, expanding coverage, and improving water quality. It targets laying a sustainable policy framework, building Iraq's reconstruction and operational capacity, creating short-term employment, and mitigating health hazards from contaminated water. Overall success requires achieving enhanced service delivery, increased coverage, improved water quality, established sustainable sector policies, strengthened local capacity, and reduced health risks, coupled with reduced water losses and efficient system operations."

"Targeted outcomes: Enhance Mexico's energy security, mitigate climate change by increasing energy efficiency and promoting a sustainable market for EE equipment to reduce GHG emissions. Specifics: distribute 70M CFLs to low-income households, replace 1.2M inefficient refrigerators/ACs, support municipal EE projects (e.g., street lighting) via standardized replication, and strengthen energy agencies to operationalize the Sustainable Use/Energy Efficiency Law. Success hinges on achieving these targets, yielding increased energy efficiency, reduced GHG, enhanced energy security, and a sustainable EE market, benefiting consumers and manufacturers."

"The activity targets strengthening the Government of Kenya's institutional, technical, and financial capacities to manage climate and disaster risks. This involves: (i) strengthening the institutional/legal framework for climate and disaster risk management; (ii) integrating disaster risk reduction into development planning; and (iii) strengthening national financial capacity to respond to disaster shocks. It also aims to provide Kenya with immediate, liquid assets after a catastrophic disaster to enable emergency response and reduce fundraising delays."

"The Hubei Jingzhou Historic Town Conservation Project targets conserving cultural heritage (City Wall, Confucius Temple, Jingzhou Museum), improving water quality (moat water to Class IV BOD, 90% sewage collection, reduced TP, TN, COD, NH4-N), and enhancing tourism services (increase tourist satisfaction, 4.01M annual visitors by 2020). It also aims to improve accessibility and strengthen institutional capacity. Success means achieving these, ensuring heritage sites are conserved, water quality targets met, and tourist satisfaction/beneficiary numbers increase, alongside effective transport and institutional strengthening."

"The Mozambique Transmission Upgrade Project aims to: (i) strengthen regional power integration by connecting Malawi to SAPP, enabling it to receive and contribute to the regional power market; (ii) allow Mozambique to earn export revenues from wheeling charges and expand power trading opportunities; (iii) enhance operation and maintenance practices, update ESCOM's master plan, and improve ESCOM's financial performance via institutional development; and (iv) rehabilitate and strengthen Malawi's transmission/distribution networks. Specific success criteria are not detailed, as performance indicators are noted as being deleted from the Project Agreement."

"[\"The activity targets include: 1. Improved Living Conditions & Food Security: Increase rural household income by at least 50% by 2013, and NWFPs to contribute 25% to household food security by 2014. 2. Legal & Policy Frameworks: Describe 20 NWFP value chains (4 per country) by 2013. 3. Capacity Building: Elaborate 5 national NWFP strategies integrating COMIFAC directives and adhering to the right to food by 2013. 4. Economic Development: Creation of 10 SMEFs per country by 2013, with at least 50% managed by women. 5. Human Capital: Provide at least six study grants between 2012 and 2013. 6. Environmental: Promote rational NWFP exploitation, sustainable management, reduce deforestation, and greenhouse gas emissions. 7. Social: Create jobs, increase beneficiary incomes, reduce poverty, and benefit vulnerable indigenous populations.\",\"Overall success requires: 1. Achieving impact targets: 50% increase in household income by 2013 and 25% NWFP contribution to food security by 2014. 2. Meeting effect targets: 20 NWFP value chains described and 5 national NWFP strategies elaborated by 2013. 3. Fulfilling other specific targets: 10 SMEFs created per country (50% women-managed) and six study grants provided. 4. Effectively mitigating identified risks: Ensuring institutional commitment and preventing abusive exploitation through participatory design and awareness. 5. Demonstrating positive environmental and social impacts as planned.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes: Universal/equitable access to safe drinking water/sanitation, end open defecation, enhanced WSS reliability/efficiency in Dehradun/Nainital. Specifics: 24/7 water supply (135 lpcd), NRW <=25%, 5.4k water meters by 2028 (Output 1); Integrated resilient sanitation systems (STPs, sewers, drainage, FSSM) benefiting 138k people in Dehradun/Nainital; 20 mobile toilets (Output 2); Citywide CMMS for WSS O&M (Output 3); Strengthened institutional capacity, climate resilience tools (Output 4). Success: Achieving all outcomes, sustainable WSS services, O&M recovery, enhanced climate resilience."

"The activity targets promoting income generation, natural resource conservation/sustainable use, and strengthening community action in Pará. Key outcomes include detailing macrozoning for conservation, strengthening protected area administration, and enhancing state land administration by surveying and registering lands. Success requires subprojects to sustainably deliver expected results within a reasonable timeframe, with bottlenecks to income generation resolved. Also, effective implementation of zoning, strengthened protected areas, improved land administration, and continuous learning/consensus on environmental/social policies."

"[\"The activity targets increasing access to water and sanitation services for peri-urban dwellers and disadvantaged groups, thereby improving livelihoods. Key outcomes include: substantial expansion of urban water supply infrastructure in Niamey, Agadez, Arlit, Tahoua, and 18 secondary centers, encompassing new water storage tanks, extensive distribution network expansion, thousands of household connections and standposts, construction of groundwater boreholes, and rehabilitation of existing facilities. Water quality will improve in five towns. Sanitation outcomes involve providing 23,500 household latrines, 5,000 hand-washing/soakaway facilities, 20 public facilities, 500 school latrine blocks, and a sludge treatment facility, supported by IEC campaigns. Institutional strengthening aims for urban water sub-sector reform, environmental/social management, and capacity building for implementing agencies.\",\"Overall success requires achieving the targeted water supply and sanitation infrastructure developments, leading to increased access for peri-urban dwellers and disadvantaged groups. This includes successfully constructing and rehabilitating specified water tanks, networks, connections, boreholes, latrines, and the sludge treatment facility. Realized water quality improvement in five towns and effective institutional support for urban water sub-sector reform and capacity building for MEELCD are critical. Success also demands diligent implementation of environmental and social safeguards, ensuring minimal adverse impacts, manageable sludge treatment operations via technical assistance and partnerships, and continuous monitoring of safeguard impacts, reflecting satisfactory implementation arrangements.\"]"

"The primary outcome is to improve access to sustainable water supply and sanitation services in selected rural villages and towns of Zhejiang Province. This involves constructing and rehabilitating water supply and wastewater facilities in 138 administrative villages, 259 natural villages, and 9 rural towns. Other outcomes include enhancing technical, financial, and managerial performance and O&M competency of local water companies and project staff through training and capacity building, and fostering rural economic development."

"Targeted outcomes: improved IWT efficiency/safety on Chittagong-Dhaka-Ashuganj Corridor, enhanced sector sustainability. Specifics include: guaranteed navigation depths/widths via performance-based contracts (PBCs), improved services at 2 cargo/4 passenger terminals (Pangaon, Ashuganj, Shashanghat, Narayanganj, Chandpur, Barisal) and 14 landing ghats, integrating women/less-abled users' needs, and enhanced BIWTA institutional capacity for modern IWT management. Success hinges on achieving year-round least advertised depths, adequate physical conditions for users, better asset maintenance, reduced expenditure, demand-met facilities with improved safety/resiliency, and a sustainable, efficient BIWTA."

"The targeted outcomes include restoring basic water supply and sanitation services for Baghdad's 4.5 million inhabitants through reconstruction, rehabilitation of priority networks and treatment facilities (e.g., Al-Karkh WTP, Abu Nuwas, Sadr City sewers for 50,000, Za'afarania water for 350,000), and capacity building for the Mayoralty of Baghdad (MOB) in project management and sustainable utility practices. Expected benefits are significant health/welfare improvements (e.g., reduced water-borne diseases, improved drinking water quality), short-term employment, and building Iraq's reconstruction capacity. The development of a Comprehensive City Development Plan is also a key outcome. Overall success would be the achievement of these service restorations, health/welfare benefits, operational sustainability through effective capacity building and financial management, and the creation of employment."

"The targeted outcomes of the Hubei Yiba Highway activity are multi-faceted, focusing on enhancing regional connectivity, economic development, safety, and environmental stewardship. Specifically, the Project Development Objective (PDO) is to improve passenger and freight flows within the Yichang-Badong corridor through expressway construction and demonstrating improved environmental management practices during construction. This objective aims to reduce poverty, inequality, social exclusion, trade/investment barriers, and manage environmental challenges, aligning with China's 'Western Development Strategy'. Key outcomes include providing a crucial communication node for north-south and east-west traffic, serving as a gateway to less-developed western provinces, stimulating trade-led growth, strengthening regional integration, and promoting tourism by improving access to natural and cultural resources. The project also aims to facilitate economic growth in non-coastal regional hubs and promote linkages with inland and western regions. Health benefits are anticipated through improved traffic safety, reduced disease transmission, and better water quality. Environmental and social outcomes include improved compliance with the Environmental Management Plan (EMP), enhanced environmental supervision during construction, better understanding of worker's rights, and effective implementation of institutional policies related to environmental management and tunnel safety. Additionally, the project intends to reduce construction impacts, enhance employment and living conditions for workers, and establish a demand-driven system for rapid identification and resolution of safeguard grievances. Institutional strengthening components are designed to improve environmental protection, environmental monitoring, and tunnel safety, along with providing equipment for expressway management, maintenance, and environmental monitoring. Studies on tunnel design, construction technology, and Hubei's comprehensive transport and logistics development plan, as well as an environmental supervision and management system, aim to further enhance these outcomes.Success of the Hubei Yiba Highway activity would be measured by several key outcome indicators and the fulfillment of specific operational and institutional goals. Primarily, the activity will be considered an overall success if there is a demonstrated: 1) decreased freight and passenger tariffs within the Yichang-Badong corridor, 2) reduced travel time between Yichang and Badong, and 3) a decrease in accident rates within the corridor. Furthermore, success hinges on: (a) improved compliance with the Environmental Management Plan (EMP) and enhanced environmental supervision during construction; (b) the effective implementation of key institutional policies related to environmental management and tunnel safety; (c) the successful pilot testing of environmentally and socially responsible procurement elements; (d) an improved understanding of worker's rights; (e) the reduction of construction impacts and the enhancement of employment and living conditions for workers; (f) the establishment of a demand-driven system for rapid identification and resolution of safeguard grievances; (g) the successful completion of the 173.6 km expressway, including all associated facilities, operating at an 80 km/hour design speed with a 24m integral subgrade width; (h) effective operation of the tolling system and telecommunications equipment; (i) the integration of recommendations from tunnel safety studies to improve construction practices and supervision, leading to a reduction in potential dangers and accidents; (j) the overall improved capacity of the Hubei Provincial Communications Department (HPCD) through dedicated training programs and the provision of new equipment; and (k) the effective preparation and utilization of environmental supervision guides, leading to reduced geological landslide disasters and improved environmental effectiveness."

"The SAPPHIRE Large Marine Ecosystem Project in WIO targets several outcomes, including: 1. The establishment of legal and regulatory frameworks, policies, and institutions to ensure the conservation, sustainable use, and equitable benefit-sharing of natural resources, biodiversity, and ecosystems, in accordance with international conventions and national legislation. 2. The development of solutions at national and sub-national levels for the sustainable management of natural resources, ecosystem services, chemicals, and waste. 3. Support and assistance to appropriate and formally mandated government institutions and intergovernmental bodies in the region to implement the activities required to deliver the Strategic Action Programme (SAP) for the Western Indian Ocean Large Marine Ecosystems (LMEs). To consider the activity an overall success, it would need to achieve the delivery of the SAP and ensure the long-term sustainability of efforts and actions for managing activities within the LMEs, as well as the sustainability of the associated institutional arrangements and partnerships. Additionally, the project's activities must meaningfully address progress towards meeting UNDP goals and targets related to sustainable development, poverty alleviation, early warning of disaster and climate change, SDGs, gender mainstreaming, and youth. Success also involves coordinating closely with the UNDEP GEF WIOLaB SAP project to harmonize activities and integrate institutional and administrative processes for a single implementation strategy covering both WIO SAPs."

"The targeted outcomes of the VIETNAM: Haiphong Urban Transport Project are to improve urban accessibility and strengthen capacity for urban transport management and planning in Hai Phong. For the activity to be considered an overall success, the following would need to be achieved: a reduction in travel times on urban arterial corridors; an increase in public transport ridership on a pilot corridor; the establishment and operationalization of a Geographic Information System (GIS) platform for urban road infrastructure; the effective execution of its extensive resettlement plans; the robust implementation of environmental mitigation measures; and the successful integration of GIS technology and public transport reforms in Haiphong."

"Targeted outcomes include increasing access to efficient, sustainable integrated energy services for ~50,000 rural households, businesses, and public facilities in indigenous areas using renewable off-grid solutions; developing a sustainable market for least-cost energy solutions and a replicable rural electricity provision strategy; and improving quality of life and economic development via intensive electricity use, remittance support, and linking CSR with community needs. Success requires achieving new electricity connections, improved public expenditure efficiency (co-financing/least-cost), increased productive electricity uses, more community/economic development projects co-financed with remittances, and demonstrated replicability."

"[\"The Water Sector Investment Project targets two main outcomes: (i) to improve the quality of water and wastewater services in the Durres Utility Service Area, and (ii) to improve the financial performance of Durres Water Utility. This aims to address a daily water supply deficit of some 30,000 m3 and enhance sanitation conditions, collection for appropriate treatment, and disposal to minimize environmental impact in the Durres region, which serves a significant and seasonally fluctuating population.\", \"For the activity to be considered an overall success, the following indicators must be met: (i) improved reliability of service, measured by increased hours of supply in the Durres region; (ii) improved water quality, measured by increased compliance with Albania's bacteriological standards for e-coli in the Durres region; and (iii) improved demand management and cost recovery, indicated by expansion of connection metering, reduction in non-revenue water, and an improved Durres Utility financial working ratio.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Benin Emergency Urban Env. Project are to improve environmental quality and strengthen institutional capacity for addressing urban environmental issues in the coastal city of Cotonou and neighboring municipalities of Seme-Kpodji, Porto Novo, Abomey-Calavi, and Ouidah. Specifically, the project aims to improve infrastructure, mitigate the negative environmental impact of floods in Greater Cotonou, and increase Benin's level of preparedness for future flooding. This includes rehabilitating and improving drainage networks in key areas of the greater Cotonou area affected by the 2010 flooding, such as calibrating, grading, cleaning, scouring, and expanding drains and channels in the AA, P, and W collectors, and upgrading the Fifadji Bridge. To consider the activity an overall success, the following would be needed: an improved collection rate of solid waste in the greater Cotonou metropolitan area, improved monitoring and reporting of key air quality indicators in the greater Cotonou metropolitan area, and an increase in the number of staff with training in disaster and climate risk at both national and municipal levels."

"The targeted outcomes of the Ghana Commercial Agriculture activity are to increase smallholder farms' access to land, private sector finance, input, and output markets through private-public partnerships in commercial agriculture within the Accra Plains and SADA zone. To consider the activity an overall success, the following would need to be achieved: an increase in yield per hectare for major crops (rice, soya, maize) on nucleus farms and out-grower schemes; an increase in area (hectares) under formal commercial arrangements like out-grower schemes and contract farming; an increase in the number of new formal or semi-formal marketing arrangements for smallholder farms; an increase in gross margins for selected crops; and an increase in the total number of direct project beneficiaries, with a focus on female participation."

"The Indonesia Energy Sector DPL aims to support a sustainable and inclusive energy sector, contributing to economic development and poverty reduction. Key outcomes by pillar include: Pillar A: Reduced budget spending on electricity subsidies and improved PLN efficiency through cost-reflective tariffs. Success: Fiscal cost reduction and efficiency gains. Pillar B: Improved investment climate in the energy sector, evidenced by new long-term gas supply agreements by PLN and reduced IPP license processing times. Success: Facilitated private sector investment. Pillar C: Removal of constraints to renewable energy expansion, specifically new geothermal power projects. Success: Increased renewable energy development. Pillar D: Expanded access to modern, reliable energy, with an increased national electrification rate. Success: Improved national electrification approach."

"The activity targets improved socio-economic and local governance for rural villagers by providing direct grants for basic infrastructure and women's revolving funds, fostering community participation, and strengthening government capacity. Success requires: improved household expenditure/access to services in \">=4,100 sub-districts by 2012\"; EIRR \">30% for major rural infrastructure\"; and \">80% beneficiary satisfaction. Overall success also depends on effective quick disaster response, informing future policy via pilots, and addressing project management unit capacity gaps."

"Targeted outcomes include enhanced fiscal and oil sector risk management and transparency, promotion of economic diversification and resilience (via agricultural productivity and ICT sector strengthening), and improved social protection for the poor and vulnerable (via effective social safety nets and cash transfers). Overall success requires public debt sustainability (e.g., Glencore debt restructuring, cleared arrears), significant poverty reduction, inclusive growth in agriculture and affordable ICT services, improved fiscal space from fiscal/debt management efficiency, macroeconomic stability, a diversified economy, and positive gender equity impacts (e.g., improved health/education for women, reduced maternal mortality, better girls' school attendance)."

"The activity aims to expand access to reliable, efficient electricity services and scale up renewable energy generation in Zanzibar. Success entails: (1) Operational 10-15MW solar PV plant with Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) piloting utility-scale renewable energy and supporting grid stability. (2) Operational 132kV transmission lines and strengthened 33/11kV distribution networks, with increased last-mile household connections, improving supply quality, reliability, and reducing losses. (3) A functional SCADA/EMS for enhanced network management. (4) Strengthened ZECO, MoWEM, and ZURA capacity in planning, operations, energy efficiency, gender mainstreaming, and mobilizing private financing for renewables."

"The activity targets the improved preservation and management of Lebanon's built cultural heritage, specifically in the historic centers of Baalbeck, Byblos, Saïda, Tripoli, and Tyre. It aims to create conditions for increased local economic development and an enhanced quality of life in these centers, integrating cultural heritage assets into municipal development plans for local economic growth and poverty alleviation. A key outcome is to raise local awareness and appreciation of cultural heritage. The project also supports the Ministry of Tourism in promoting cultural heritage tourism, with a medium-term annual target of 200,000 cultural heritage tourists, alongside enhancing the diversity and quality of offerings and services to increase tourist spending and revenue. Ultimately, it seeks to assist municipalities in ensuring local populations benefit from these developments, driven by effective management of cultural assets, urban regeneration, and enhanced cultural tourism."

"The targeted outcomes of the First East-West Highway Improvement activity are to gradually reduce road transport costs, and improve access, transit traffic, and safety along Georgia's East-West corridor. This should result in better road quality, increased transit, improved safety, shorter travel times, better infrastructure management, enhanced regional economic integration, fostered economic growth, and reduced poverty. Overall success will be achieved through: (i) improvements in transit time/roughness index between Agaiani and Igoeti; (ii) a decrease in accidents on improved sections; (iii) adoption of modern western standards in road design; and (iv) a successful Rikoti tunnel concession."

"The targeted outcomes include establishing a viable basis for irrigated agriculture and natural resources management in Andapa, Marovoay, Itasy, and Lac Alaotra, and improving environmental sustainability of land management in these watersheds. The project aims to reverse low productivity, deferred maintenance, and poor water management into a cycle of increased productivity, full cost recovery, and acceptable O&M. Specific outcomes involve improved market access, sustainable intensification/diversification of agriculture, enhanced farmer capacity, reduced marketing costs, increased farm gate prices and value-added products, better irrigation management and infrastructure, reduced erosion/sedimentation, increased agricultural productivity via agroecological methods, and strengthened natural resource management. Overall success requires achieving these objectives, specifically increased productivity, full cost recovery, acceptable O&M, and competitive allocation of irrigation investments based on site performance in O&M cost recovery and expenditures. Eligibility for support also requires pre-project O&M cost recovery, functioning WUAs, and current government expenditure levels. Success also involves rural populations becoming more accountable and sustainably managing land/resources, with reduced erosion and increased agroecological productivity."

"The activity targets reducing Buenos Aires' flood vulnerability by improving its drainage system and implementing a risk management program focusing on identification, prevention, mitigation, and education. Outcomes include increased city resilience via land use planning, improved information (hazard maps), and enhanced flood defenses (new tunnels, secondary connections in Maldonado Basin). Success requires achieving reduced flood vulnerability, a functional improved drainage system, effective risk management, strengthened city agencies for flood emergencies, stable resources for maintenance, and a comprehensive communication/education program, all supported by strong city government commitment and robust project implementation."

"The targeted outcomes of the DRC Emergency Urban and Social Rehabilitation Project are to help the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) address urgent post-election challenges by maintaining macroeconomic stability, funding critical expenditures, and addressing urgent rehabilitation and social needs in Kinshasa. This is expected to create an environment where reforms can be sustained and prevent political and economic regression, social instability, civil unrest, and potential reversion to conflict.  Specific component outcomes include:

*   **Component 1 (Primary Education Recovery):** To test and launch an effort that will eventually result in a reduction of school fees paid by parents nationwide.
*   **Component 2 (Domestic Debt Settlement):** To improve the investment climate and inject resources, thereby increasing available working capital for private sector activities, by completing the settlement of government debt to domestic suppliers accrued during the war and instability.
*   **Component 3 (Rehabilitation of Priority Urban Roads in Kinshasa):** To restore or significantly improve vehicle accessibility for an estimated 1.4 million residents by financing the heavy rehabilitation of approximately 50 km of access roads to poor neighborhoods.
*   **Component 4 (Water and Sanitation in Kinshasa's Peri-urban Areas):** To provide potable water access to about 260,000 people who currently rely on improper sources, by expanding the distribution network from the recently-rehabilitated water treatment plant of Lukaya to poor neighborhoods.
*   **Component 5 (Malaria Reduction and Prevention in Kinshasa):** To reduce malaria-related infant mortality and prevent an estimated 30,000 deaths among under-five children over the life of the project, through the distribution of 2 long-lasting insecticidal nets per household in Kinshasa.

To consider the activity an overall success, it would need to achieve the following:

*   The maintenance of macro-economic stability and successful funding of critical immediate expenditures.
*   The effective addressing of urgent rehabilitation and social needs in Kinshasa.
*   The successful reduction of school fees paid by parents (as initiated by Component 1).
*   An improved investment climate and increased working capital for the private sector (from Component 2).
*   Restored or significantly improved vehicle accessibility for 1.4 million residents through road rehabilitation (from Component 3).
*   Potable water access provided to 260,000 people (from Component 4).
*   A reduction in malaria-related infant mortality and prevention of an estimated 30,000 deaths among under-five children in Kinshasa (from Component 5).
*   Ultimately, the creation of an environment where reforms are sustained, and political and economic regression, social instability, civil unrest, and conflict are prevented."

"The UY APL2 project targets increasing water supply/sanitation efficiency, coverage, and sustainability. Outcomes include: improved OSE efficiency, effectiveness, coordination, customer satisfaction; increased treated water pumping capacity; reduced Unaccounted for Water (UfW) and increased revenue collection via physical upgrades (pipes, meters, leak detection) and behavioral shifts; improved drinking water quality (modernized Aguas Corrientes plant); completed sewerage expansion in five cities; increased household connections. Success requires achieving these measurable improvements in OSE's operations, UfW reduction, infrastructure, and service coverage."

"The targeted outcomes of the Xinjiang Turfan Water Conservation Project are: to reduce the risk of flooding; to provide a reliable, equitable, and stable water supply for industry and agriculture; to increase water productivity for irrigated agricultural production; and to reduce groundwater overexploitation. These objectives are to be achieved through improved water resources planning and management, incorporating an innovative concept of evapo-transpiration (ET) management for sustainable water use and ecological environment protection in Turfan Prefecture. The primary beneficiaries are the ethnic minority rural poor in the project areas.To consider the activity an overall success, there must be a fundamental change in the economic growth pattern of the Prefecture. This change entails moving away from the current unsustainable reliance on overexploiting groundwater resources and devastating the ecological environment towards a sustainable pattern supported by sound water resources planning and management."

"The targeted outcomes of the \"Governance and Institutional Dev.\" activity are primarily to strengthen the Government of Cote d'Ivoire's capacity in three main areas: (i) to enhance efficiency and transparency in the use of public resources; (ii) to manage the development of its petroleum in a socially and environmentally sound and sustainable manner; and (iii) to foster governance and efficiency in the cocoa sector.Specific outcomes for each component are detailed as follows:Component 1: Strengthening public financial management aims to improve efficiency and transparency through enhanced procedures for budget planning, execution, and control; capacity development for these functions; harmonization of the PFM legal and regulatory framework with WEAMU and international norms; and improvement in the generation and dissemination of economic and financial information. This includes improving capacity for budget preparation (public investment planning, Medium-Term Expenditure Framework, macro-economic modeling), budget classification to identify poverty-related spending, budget execution (institutional audit, manpower plan), developing an interface between key financial systems (SIGFIP, SIGMAP, ASTER, SIGFAE), deconcentrating the SIGFIP system to 23 \"départements,\" improving the Ministry of Economy and Finance's (MEF) production and dissemination of economic/financial information (quarterly reports), strengthening audit and control functions (reviewing procedures, manual production, expenditure control streamlining, M&E system), and enhancing public procurement transparency and efficiency (regulatory/institutional framework improvement, public/private sector capacity development, control system strengthening, regulatory entity setup, manuals, ethics code, national strategy, training, internet dissemination).Component 2: Strengthening capacity to manage the upstream petroleum sector aims to create conditions for long-term institutional and policy reform by strengthening the Government's capacity to: (i) improve the transparency of petroleum revenue and maximize its generation over time (including attracting new investors) through reviewing/updating legal/contractual/fiscal frameworks, developing financial models for forecasting, establishing attractive/transparent promotion procedures, improving negotiation/supervision capacity, and supporting the Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative (EITI); and (ii) develop policy and operational responses to support institutional/technical capacity to manage social and environmental aspects of increased petroleum exploration and production activities (involving reviewing environmental regulations, establishing an environmental management plan monitoring system, and building social/environmental assessment capacity).Component 3: Setting up of an adequate institutional and regulatory framework for the cocoa sector aims to foster governance and efficiency by helping the Government establish an adequate policy and regulatory framework and increase the accountability of the entities entrusted with the management and regulation of the sector. This encompasses supporting the design/implementation of a cocoa sector reform strategy (with a focus on governance structure and accountability), conducting an audit of the cocoa grower census, and performing institutional, technical, and financial audits of the entities overseeing the cocoa sector and their associated funds.Component 4: Project management and communication supports the project coordination unit and technical committee in coordinating activities across implementing ministries, monitoring/evaluating project activities, and financing project audits. It also aims to finance communication outreach to inform the public and build demand for governance/transparency reforms, improve access to economic information, and strengthen the communication capacity of involved public institutions.To consider the activity an overall success, there would need to be demonstrable achievement of all the targeted outcomes. This includes: tangible improvements in the efficiency and transparency of public resource management; effective and sustainable management of the petroleum sector with increased revenue transparency and sound social/environmental practices; and a well-governed, efficient cocoa sector with an established policy framework and increased accountability. Furthermore, success would entail the sustainable embedding of these enhanced capacities within the Government's institutions, creating conditions for capacity to grow and be effectively utilized and retained."

"Targeted outcomes: Bridge the immediate energy supply-demand gap via 2x4,800 MW Medupi and Kusile coal plants (supercritical, CCS-ready, dry-cooled); transition to a low-carbon economy by integrating 200 MW renewable energy, improving Eskom's energy efficiency, and shifting coal transport to rail. Also, ensuring the electricity sector's financial viability, supporting vulnerable populations, and contributing to economic growth. Success requires: Medupi/Kusile effectively closing the supply gap; carbon emissions peaking 2020-2025, plateauing, then declining; achieving 12% energy efficiency by 2015 and 4% renewable electricity by 2013; a financially viable sector fostering private investment; and overall economic growth with reduced poverty."

"[\"The targeted outcome of the activity is to increase the volume and proportion of municipal solid waste recycled in selected districts of Ningbo Municipality through improved processes for waste separation at source and enhanced recycling.\",\"Overall success requires achieving the increased volume and proportion of MSW recycled. This depends on sustainable household participation through public campaigns and neighborhood incentives; effective institutional mechanisms for policy, facility operation, and program coordination; adequate financial support for capital and recurrent expenditures; a reliable Solid Waste Management Information System (SW-MIS); and strong project leadership for implementation and sustainability, aligning with China's 12th Five-Year Plan.\"]"

"The project aims to improve the efficiency and sustainability of national road asset management and maintenance practices by the Ministry of Transport. Key outcomes include introducing global practices, enhancing implementing agency capacity, establishing short- and mid-term national preservation plans, increasing maintenance resources, and improving performance monitoring based on scientific criteria. It also seeks to address climate change impacts by developing robust methodologies and implementing flood-resilient designs. Furthermore, it aims to develop a comprehensive Road Asset Management System (RAMS), demonstrate efficient performance-based maintenance contracts (PBCs) for policy advice, improve specific high-priority road sections, and strengthen the capacity of road agencies in planning, budgeting, constructing, and monitoring assets, including improved human resource management and RMF transparency."

"The activity aims to establish policy and institutional measures for green growth in transport, energy, environmental health, and disaster risk management. It targets improving environmental quality by reducing PM2.5 in air, strengthening water pollution instruments, and boosting solid waste disposal/recycling capacity. Pillar 1 focuses on long-term environmental degradation improvements and reduced climate/natural disaster vulnerability. Pillar 2 supports pollution management via urban wastewater treatment and solid waste policies, stronger air/water pollution standards, and solid waste technical requirements. Specific outcomes include updating diesel quality, establishing wastewater discharge/reuse norms, and requiring municipal Integrated Solid Waste Management Plans with economic incentives for landfill waste minimization and waste picker inclusion. The overarching goal is a gradual, verifiable transformation to a low-carbon, low-pollution, sustainably managed, and resilient growth trajectory across sectors."

"Targeted outcomes: Enhance water supply and long-term water security for Greater Beirut and Mount Lebanon through the construction and operation of the Bisri Dam, WTP expansion, and hydropower plants. Strengthen BMLWE capacity for sustainable service delivery, including O&M, cost recovery, and water resource management. Mitigate environmental and social impacts via ESMP/RAP implementation, ensuring compensation and livelihood restoration for affected people, and guarantee dam safety through expert oversight. Success requires all infrastructure to be fully constructed, operational, and safe, delivering increased and improved water to GBML consumers cost-effectively. BMLWE's capacity must be sustainably enhanced, all safeguard measures including compensation and livelihood restoration effectively implemented, dam safety robustly maintained, and World Bank standards adhered to."

"The BR Manaus DPL targets assisting Manaus in implementing sound public sector and fiscal management to improve service delivery in transportation, education, and urban management, benefiting the poor. Outcomes include: increased fiscal sustainability (eliminating R$360M deficit by 2016); improved public sector management (increased revenue, reduced expenditure, transparent procurement, tax compliance); enhanced education quality (improved IDEB scores, merit-based director selection, efficient staff allocation); and improved urban mobility/city center rejuvenation (reduced travel time, integrated regeneration, better bus management). Success requires achieving these specific targets."

"Targeted outcomes: Strengthen environmental governance by restructuring the EPD, reforming regulations, and improving service delivery, alongside promoting green investments via green financing schemes and public/private projects. This aims to address pollution at source, improve energy/resource efficiency, enhance green competitiveness, improve OHS/labor conditions, reduce air/water pollution, and establish health advisories for vulnerable groups. Success requires proper design, implementation, full stakeholder consultation, adoption of suitable international best practices, and strong citizen engagement with transparent information disclosure and annual consultations for effective risk management."

"The activity targets achieving fiscal stability and consolidating Lao PDR's pathway towards green growth. This involves: 1) Reducing fiscal and financial sector risks by boosting revenues, controlling deficits, establishing debt management, and strengthening financial sector regulation. 2) Establishing green growth planning, financing, and monitoring instruments, including adopting a National Green Growth Strategy with targets and indicators, identifying sustainable financing, improving fund efficiency, and strengthening environmental risk assessment tools. 3) Consolidating green growth principles in sectors by promoting sustainable use of natural assets (water, forests, biodiversity) and developing cleaner production/consumption systems, including less polluting agriculture, improved pollution management, and climate risk management for infrastructure."

"Targeted outcomes include: poverty reduction and income generation for 2 million people; updated and fully operational OMVS legal, institutional, and financial mechanisms; implementation of sustainable multipurpose water resources activities at local levels for income generation and poverty reduction; development of a regional planning and management framework for economically, environmentally, and socially responsible water resources development; strengthened OMVS institutional capacities; inclusion of Guinea in OMVS; rehabilitation of OMVS Documentation Center; promotion of grassroots income generation (small hydraulic infrastructure, fisheries, land/water management); and preparation of the SRB Master Plan with pre-investment support for future hydropower."

"Targeted outcomes: Increase sustainable use of new/rehabilitated water/sanitation facilities for ~1.5 million rural people, improving hygiene. Strengthen local communities' (JASSS, financial principles, participation), municipal, and central government capacity for sustainable service delivery/policy. Success needs: Sustainable services from clear stakeholder roles, demand-driven approach, cost-sharing, robust O&M, long-term support, capacity building, integrated hygiene education, high-quality construction, and strong community ownership."

"[\"The primary targeted outcomes are to achieve sustainable management of plantation forests and conservation of biodiversity in special use forests, enhancing forestry's contribution to rural poverty reduction and global environmental protection. This involves: improving the environment for sustainable forestry and biodiversity; providing attractive tree-planting packages for poor farming households for income/employment; offering competitive grants for managing priority special use forests; and enhancing capacity at regional, provincial, district, and site levels for support services and monitoring. The project also aims to reduce the timber demand-supply gap and increase rural employment and incomes through sustainable wood production.\",\"To be considered an overall success, the activity needs to achieve sustainable management of plantation forests, evidenced by financial profitability, cost recovery, and credit repayment by smallholders. Biodiversity conservation in special use forests must be realized, supported by the expected replenishment of the Vietnam Conservation Fund (VCF) if successful, benefiting up to 50 priority SUFs. Additionally, improved land tenure security, increased rural employment and incomes, and enhanced institutional, environmental, and social sustainability across all components are crucial indicators.\"]"

"Targeted outcomes: improved WSS governance (SJPNL autonomy, staff capacity), 24x7 continuous pressurized water supply/sewage for all households (with private sector participation, grievance redressal), improved financial/operational performance (volumetric tariffs, NRW reduction, energy efficiency, O&M cost recovery). Success: SJPNL's effective operation, 24x7 supply, volumetric billing, reduced NRW/energy use, increased O&M cost recovery, timely tariffs/subsidies, transparent reporting, 30% female employment, high complaint redressal."

"The activity aims to increase sustainable land management (SLM) adoption and expand access to secure land titles in non-rangeland rural Ethiopia. Outcomes include reducing land degradation via participatory watershed management, building capacity, strengthening SLM policy, and improving rural land tenure security through SLLCs and the NRLAIS. Overall success requires achieving PDO-level results indicators: increased land area under SLM, a minimum 4% increase in the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), increased securely registered landholdings, and a greater share of these held by women. Also, successful establishment of WUAs, adoption of participatory watershed development processes, provision of SLLCs, and effective NRLAIS functioning are key."

"The targeted outcomes are long-term poverty reduction and increased shared prosperity in Jamaica, achieved by eliminating high debt and low growth, boosting competitiveness, stimulating economic growth, and creating jobs. Specific outcomes include: reclaiming fiscal space for pro-poor spending; reducing electricity costs; increasing economic activity; enhancing safety (Building Act); medium-term job growth from logistics; protecting the poor/vulnerable; safeguarding social spending; and expanding the social safety net. Success requires achieving these long-term goals through sustained economic growth and job creation, realizing the positive impacts of Pillar 1 reforms (e.g., reduced electricity costs, safety, job growth), ensuring social spending sustainability, and effectively mitigating negative impacts and identified risks."

"The targeted outcomes of the activity are the finalization and approval of a National Legal Framework or Legislation on Climate Change for Eswatini, including the development of an implementation plan and dissemination to stakeholders. Success would entail the approval of this legislation after extensive stakeholder consultations and a thorough review by international partners (UNDP's Regional Bureau for Africa, COMESA, and the British High Commissioner) to ensure its robustness and alignment with best practices."

"Targeted outcomes aim to strengthen institutional/regulatory frameworks for ambitious climate action towards net-zero, climate-resilient, and sustainable development, enhancing NDCs' scale, speed, ambition, and inclusivity. Key outputs: a 2050 Carbon Neutrality Roadmap, a Country Report on Stockholm+50 themes, and mechanisms for enhanced climate resilience. Success requires: the Roadmap developed/handed over with approved long-term strategies; the Country Report developed/handed over after inclusive consultations; a Damage/Loss Assessment Framework developed; and government capacity built for recovery/reconstruction planning, evidenced by trained officials and data extracts."

"Targeted outcomes include transforming Dong Ha, Lao Bao, and Moc Bai into economic hubs by improving urban environmental infrastructure (roads, wastewater, solid waste, flood control), strengthening institutional capacities, increasing climate resilience, and improving the living environment and health, especially for the poor and women, leading to poverty reduction and increased income opportunities. Success requires: priority urban infrastructure operational by 2018; 30% project employment by women; strengthened institutional capacity (e.g., 2,237 staff trained, 30% female PMU/PSU management); specific infrastructure achievements like improved roads, operational wastewater/water supply, sanitary landfills, and materials recovery facilities."

"The activity targets increasing stable, reliable electricity access, alleviating supply deficits, and reducing drought vulnerability in WAPP \"Zone A\" coastal states. Key outcomes include establishing a robust 330 kV transmission link facilitating cross-border exchanges until 2020, reducing losses, operating costs, and reliance on aging facilities, and improving system reliability and dynamic response to faults. Success requires full deployment of a WAPP Cooperation Agreement by 2009, including an \"Operational Security and Mitigation Plan,\" ratification of the Energy Exchange Protocol, and adherence to common rules for system protection and operation."

"The activity targets improving upstream watershed management and increasing agricultural water productivity in selected Gram Panchayats in Himachal Pradesh. This aims to boost agricultural productivity, strengthen market linkages, diversify production for value addition, maintain biodiversity, and ensure sustainable land and water use. Success will be achieved when: selected water sources are managed according to project-supported plans; a percentage of participating farmers adopt climate-smart agriculture practices (gender disaggregated); an increased area is under higher efficiency irrigation in targeted GPs (gender disaggregated); and a percentage of target beneficiaries rate 'Satisfied' or above on project interventions (gender disaggregated)."

"The Transport Sector Development Project in the Solomon Islands targets an overall impact of improved access to socioeconomic opportunities for the people, especially remote rural populations, the national population, and socially vulnerable groups including women, children, and the disabled. The intended outcome is that the Ministry of Infrastructure Development (MID) will provide sustainable transport infrastructure as prioritized in the National Transport Plan (NTP).

To achieve this, the project aims for three main outputs:
1.  **Efficient and effective project implementation and management:** This will be provided by a Central Project Implementation Unit (CPIU), staffed by existing MID personnel and consultants. Capacity building through coaching and on-the-job training will facilitate a gradual transition to government staff. Performance targets include establishing the CPIU, generating an implementation plan, establishing a monitoring and evaluation system, recruiting experts, carrying out on-the-job training for CPIU staff, and overseeing the gender action plan.
2.  **Rehabilitation and maintenance of transport infrastructure:** Investments are planned through multi-criteria analysis, considering economic and social aspects, safeguards, engineering standards, and incorporating climate change adaptation measures for socially vulnerable groups. Success in this output would involve conducting public consultations, providing HIV/AIDS and gender awareness workshops (with 50% women participants), preparing feasibility studies, design and bid documents (including for labor-based maintenance), tendering and awarding civil works contracts, commencing civil works, and selecting and training small contractors in labor-based maintenance (with 30% women workers). Additionally, literacy and numeracy training will be provided, targeting 50% female workers and contractors.
3.  **Upgraded technical and managerial capacity of MID staff:** This involves reviewing training needs, determining individual training plans, identifying training providers, conducting and overseeing training programs, providing gender sensitization training, and evaluating training outcomes. Performance targets include ensuring that at least 700 residents of communities and small contractors receive labor-based maintenance workshops, and that at least 700 residents achieve a certificate or higher in project-related skills.

Overall success will be measured by the sustained provision of transport infrastructure by MID, aligned with the NTP, leading to improved access to socioeconomic opportunities for the targeted populations, and significant capacity development within MID and among local contractors and communities."

"The targeted outcomes of the Morocco Digital and Climate Smart Agriculture Program are to increase the adoption of digital and climate-smart technologies in agri-food value chains across Morocco. This is expected to transform the Moroccan agri-food sector, contribute to job creation in rural areas (particularly for youth and women), and promote sustainable, climate-resilient management of natural resources. The activity will be considered an overall success by tracking the number of farmers and agri-food SMEs adopting climate-smart technologies and practices, and those adopting digital technologies."

"Targeted outcomes include meeting MDG target 10 by 2015, specifically providing safe drinking water access to 268 million people and sanitation to 372 million in the PRC. The project aims to support the government's 11th Five-Year Plan and environmental policies by prioritizing water and sanitation infrastructure, improving critically needed water infrastructure in the Songhua River Basin (SRB) through capital, high-grade technology transfer, and managerial expertise. It also intends to catalyze public and private sector investments in SRB pollution control and management, serve as a demonstration and catalyst for private sector operations in the PRC, promote local capital and financing markets, and contribute to integrated water resource management and transboundary pollution prevention."

"The targeted outcomes are: enhanced power generation (60-80 MW) and transmission capacity for Burundi, Rwanda, and Tanzania; improved sustainable livelihoods in affected areas; and regional stability/integration via joint hydropower and transmission. Success requires: 60-80 MW installed and operational, with ongoing power production and sales (GWh/year); full implementation of ESMP, RAP, and LADP for dam/reservoir/transmission corridors; and establishment of a fully functional institutional mechanism (Rusumo Power Company, joint oversight entity) between the three countries."

"Targeted outcomes include improving access to Ha Noi metro line 3 stations, enhancing connectivity between the line and other public and private transport, and strengthening urban transport policies. This aims to increase public transport's share of overall passenger traffic in six districts from 8% (2014) to 14% by 2023. It also targets over 40% satisfaction with metro line 3 access and service improvements by 2018, an annual reduction of 8,400 tons CO2 equivalent by 2019, and an increase of 15,635 daily passengers on urban rail- and bus-based mass transit systems by 2019. Outputs include completing all accessibility measures for stations, including gender-sensitive features, and implementing a station access management system by 2018.Fifty-two new feeder buses with an information system linked to metro line 3 stations are to be operational by 2018. Policies include a developed station access management system by 2016, a parking strategy developed and piloted by 2018, and an urban transport pricing framework with approved regulations by 2018.To be considered an overall success, the activity requires the scheduled implementation of other planned metro lines, availability of government resources for metro line 3 operation and maintenance, higher public acceptance of public transport modes, and timely completion of metro line 3 station works."

"The targeted outcomes of the Second Green Power Development Project include increasing clean hydropower generation in Bhutan by 491.52 GWh and avoiding 459,734 tons of CO2 equivalent emissions per annum by 2020.The activity would be considered an overall success if power trading from Bhutan to India increases by at least 16,000 GWh by 2025, and investments from public and private sectors into hydropower development for export from Bhutan increase by at least 4,400 MW by 2025. Key outputs enabling this success include the construction of the 118 MW Nikachhu hydropower plant and 18.6 km of 132 kV transmission line by 2019, securing DGPC's equity financing through PPP by 2015, establishing safeguard implementation and monitoring systems by 2015, and providing commercial weaving training to at least 25 women."

"The targeted outcomes of the Xi'an Urban Road Network Improvement Project are to develop an efficient, safe, and environmentally sustainable integrated urban transport system. Specific targets by 2016 include increasing average project corridor and bus speeds to 20 km/h (from 14 km/h), reducing traffic accidents (fatalities/serious injuries) by 10%, increasing vehicle emission test pass rate to 80%, and raising public satisfaction with pedestrian facilities to 75%. By 2020, aims include increasing annual Grade II+ air quality days from 280 to 311 and reducing 2010 vehicle emission levels by 10%. Overall success requires robust long-term PRC growth, sustained local government focus on sustainable urban transport, effective implementation of other pollutant control programs, efficient transport system management, public awareness of road safety, and at least 50% compliance with emission requirements."

"The activity targets empowering local communities in Southeast Mexico for sustainable and resilient terrestrial/marine landscape management. Outcomes include developing adaptive management, production, and marketing strategies; conserving biodiversity, improving environmental services, and reducing carbon loss via agroecological production; and strengthening second-level networks for scaling sustainable goods/services, market integration, and financial access. It also aims to overcome community barriers in planning, finance, and coordination, strengthening governance. Success requires communities to demonstrate sustained capacity to responsibly manage natural assets, efficiently generate goods/services, and maintain equitable market relationships long after PPD funding ends, ensuring self-sufficiency and lasting outcome achievement."

"The activity targets the improvement of urban governance and infrastructure and service delivery in pourashavas, focusing on citizen awareness and participation, urban planning, women's participation, integration of the urban poor, financial accountability and sustainability, and administrative transparency. Success would entail pourashavas meeting performance criteria for fund allocation, positive public perception via citizen report cards, financial sustainability through debt management, fair representation for low-income groups, and successful implementation of municipal transport, drainage, solid waste, water supply, sanitation, municipal facilities, and basic slum services. This includes establishing coordination committees (TLCCs, WLCCs), CBOs, gender committees, planning units, and preparing comprehensive development plans with clear budgets for poverty reduction and gender action plans."

"The activity targets: improved transport connectivity in DRC for national economic integration; restoration of SNCC's financial and operational viability; implementation of a sector-wide governance plan to strengthen transport SOEs' operational performance; rehabilitation of three main national transport and trade corridors; and reduction of port, railway, and airport transport costs along these corridors by at least half. Success would entail achieving all these objectives, particularly the sustained financial and operational viability of SNCC and the cost reduction."

"Targeted outcomes include: reducing losses in agriculture/water sectors from extreme weather; improving climate/disaster resilience of infrastructure/water security for ~75,000 people; modernizing 20,000 ha irrigation infrastructure; enhancing irrigation/agricultural land management (O&M, WUA strengthening, farmer capacity building on resilient water use/agronomy); and improving national disaster risk management capacity (asset management, pilot hydrological monitoring/forecasting). Success requires achieving these, demonstrating economic viability (e.g., 16% EIRR), sustainable O&M plans, optimized/resilient agricultural production, improved water productivity, and inclusive, gender-sensitive water management."

"The targeted outcomes of the \"Peru: Enhancement of Environmental Quality Services\" activity are to enhance environmental management by increasing the quality, availability, and reliability of environmental data for decision-makers and civil society, improving mechanisms to identify and address environmental priorities at regional and national levels, and opening up decision-making on environmental aspects to public scrutiny. The ultimate goal is to strengthen environmental management systems in Peru, particularly the capacity of national, regional, and local governments, to promote environmental sustainability in key economic sectors, enhance competitiveness, establish a national system of environmental information for decision-making, and reduce social conflicts. It also aims to address existing weaknesses such as ill-prepared public servants and a lack of institutional channels for environmental complaints. Furthermore, the activity intends to enable the creation of an institutional and policy framework that promotes environmental sustainability of key economic sectors, foster a national system of environmental information that supports decision-making in investment and production activities, and bolster the demand side of good environmental governance. An amendment also added environmental health initiatives to prevent COVID-19 contagion and promote counter-transmission measures of infectious diseases.To consider the activity an overall success, the following key results indicators would need to be achieved: an increase in the number of laboratories certified by a reference laboratory; the production of an annual report identifying environmental priorities based on an economic analysis of environmental degradation costs; and an improved user perception (as measured by a survey) of environmental data and information provided by SINIA (Sistema Nacional de Información Ambiental)."

"The targeted outcomes include improving transport sector connectivity and efficiency via an improved national road network, enhanced road safety management capacity, and better overall transport sector and national road management. This involves improving access to rural/economically productive areas, developing a national road safety policy, strengthening the road crash data system, establishing safety units in MoWT/UNRA, and building sustainable road sector planning, design, and management capabilities, including strengthening UNRA's internal audit functions for road projects."

"[\"The targeted outcomes include safeguarding fiscal sustainability by improving domestic revenue mobilization and designing a hydropower revenue stabilization fund. It also aims to strengthen the private sector's capacity to generate employment, especially for youth and women, by reducing public-private sector disparities through enhanced pension coverage and addressing skills mismatches. Furthermore, it seeks to foster a more dynamic private sector by strengthening corporate governance of state-owned enterprises and promoting a level playing field.\",\"Overall success would entail achieving improved domestic revenue mobilization and effective management of hydropower revenues through the stabilization fund. It would also require a demonstrably increased capacity of the private sector to generate sustainable employment opportunities, particularly for youth and women, coupled with a reduction in public-private sector disparities. Ultimately, success means an improved macroeconomic stability, enhanced growth, and positive impacts on poverty, social aspects, and gender.\"]"

"The targeted outcomes of the Kakheti Regional Roads Improvement Project are: 1) To reduce transport costs and improve access between Vaziani, Gombori, and Telavi. 2) To improve traffic safety along the Vaziani-Gombori-Telavi (VGT) road and the Vaziani-Sagarejo-Bakhurtsikhe-Gurjaani-Telavi road. 3) To strengthen the capacity of the Roads Department (RD) regional office in Sagarejo for improved operational effectiveness. 4) To enable the RD regional office in Sagarejo to provide road management training and advisory support to local governments. For the activity to be considered an overall success, these outcomes must be achieved. Additionally, success requires: 1) Adequate winter maintenance and timely landslide response on the VGT road post-completion, included in yearly maintenance contracts. 2) Sustained Government commitment and action for addressing traffic safety issues. 3) Continued availability of sufficient funds for road maintenance, with adequate allocation for secondary roads. 4) The enhanced capacity of the RD regional office effectively improving operations and providing support."

"The targeted outcomes of the activity, \"Afghanistan: Sustainable Development of Natural Resources Project II,\" include creating a more sustainable mineral resource sector by strengthening the capacities of institutions responsible for regulating access to mineral resources, as well as monitoring and enforcing environmental, social, and cultural regulations. Specific outcomes involve significant strengthening of the National Environmental Protection Agency (NEPA) and the Ministry of Mines (including inspectorate, environmental/social protection, and mining cadastre functions). The project aims to ensure sound recovery processes for Buddhist artifacts from the Aynak valley, increase the collection and digitization of geodata to attract exploration, and assess non-mining socio-economic opportunities within planned resource corridors. It also targets the preparation of a Sector Development Policy, completion of the Hajigak tender, support for Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative (EITI) next steps, civil society capacity building for demand-side governance with a gender focus, education for sector professionals, technical assistance for the artisanal gemstone sector (e.g., cutting and polishing), and the first phase of reform for State Minerals Enterprises. The activity would be considered an overall success if a demonstrably more sustainable mineral resource sector is established in Afghanistan, characterized by effectively strengthened institutional capacities to regulate and enforce environmental, social, and cultural standards in mineral operations. Success would also be marked by the transformation of mineral investments into broad-based sustainable growth, encompassing effective management of major mining projects, robust protection and recovery of cultural heritage, enhanced transparency through EITI, and the development of alternative socio-economic opportunities within resource corridors."

"The activity aims to meet Cape Verde's electricity and water supply needs sustainably, fostering economic growth and population well-being. Targeted outcomes include improved sector governance, enhanced technical and financial performance of Electra, and addressing energy/water scarcity hindering FDI. Success requires reliable, least-cost electricity/water production, Electra's financial sustainability without burdening government, removal of scarcity as an FDI impediment, a strengthened regulatory agency, improved Electra internal functions, successful sector restructuring, and effective investment/efficiency planning to meet growing demands."

"The Development Policy Operation 1 New Series aims to support job creation in Armenia through growth-enhancing reforms, specifically by strengthening competitiveness and ensuring fiscal, social, and environmental sustainability, while maintaining macroeconomic stability and reorienting the economy towards tradables. Targeted outcomes include: improving the business environment by reducing the burden of inspections; enhancing credit reporting systems; increasing flexibility in civil service recruitment and promotion; fostering increased competition in the aviation sector; improving pension fund asset management; increasing revenue collection, with a long-term goal of raising the revenue-to-GDP ratio to 27-28 percent by 2025; expanding the Family Benefits Program to approximately 100,000 households by the end of 2012 to reduce poverty and inequality; and improving policies for natural resource management, strengthening Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) legislation, and enhancing regulatory provisions for environmental and social issues in the mining sector. Success will be measured by tracking indicators such as the number of inspection agencies, the tax-to-GDP ratio, and the coverage of the Family Benefits Program, among others mentioned in the detailed results matrix. Additionally, successful implementation requires the approval of key legislation, strong political commitment, and effective mitigation of macroeconomic and operational risks, ensuring sustained growth, employment creation, and improved civil services for the population, particularly benefiting children and female-headed households, and improving environmental stewardship."

"The targeted outcome of the Ma'anshan Cihu River Basin Improvement Project is to improve drainage and flood protection capacity in selected areas of Ma'anshan City, China. To consider the activity an overall success, a result-based monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system will be utilized. This system will incorporate a database of overall project outcome indicators to measure the achievement of the objective and track changes in performance, behavior, or resource status. The Project Management Office (PMO), in coordination with implementing agencies, will collect, collate, and analyze data through this M&E system. Further analysis will be provided by external evaluation reports on environmental and social safeguards implementation and construction supervision. Semi-annual progress reports, a midterm review report, and an implementation completion report will also be prepared to assess the project's success."

"The Irrigation System Enhancement Project aims to achieve the following targeted outcomes: (i) a reduction in the amount of energy used for irrigation; (ii) an improvement in irrigation conveyance efficiency within targeted irrigation schemes; and (iii) an enhancement in the availability and reliability of crucial sector data and information for decision-makers and other stakeholders. Additionally, the project intends to restore irrigation services to over 1,300 hectares of agricultural areas that were previously served but cut-off due to infrastructure deterioration. The activity would be considered an overall success if there is a demonstrable reduction in energy consumption for irrigation, a measured improvement in the efficiency of water conveyance in the designated schemes, the establishment and functioning of systems providing reliable and accessible sector data, and the re-establishment of irrigation services to the identified 1,300 hectares."
